observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Mayor to:
1. Create an Inventory Committee composed of adequate number of
members, which shall have at least one member each from the
Accounting and Property Divisions/Unit, who shall be responsible for the
actual count of all PPEs owned by the LGU;
2. Instruct the Inventory Committee to:
a. Prepare a Physical Inventory Plan containing the specific
assignments/duties of the Committee members, the cut-off date and a
schedule specifying the dates and locations of the inventory taking
activities from start up to the targeted completion of the physical
inventory, among others. Also, conduct the physical count pursuant to
COA Circular No. 2020-006; and
b. Cause the submission of a complete Report of the Physical Count of
Property, Plant and Equipment to the Audit Team not later than
January 31 of each year.
3. Require the Property Custodian to prepare and maintain property cards
and reconcile with the PPE Ledger Cards periodically; and
4. Require the Municipal Accountant to exert earnest effort in the
preparation and maintenance of PPE leger cards, update and
reconciliation of PPE records and provision of depreciation on identified
assets by designating the corresponding accounting personnel who will be
responsible in undertaking the same.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to require the:
1. Municipal Accountant to:
a. Exert utmost effort to retrieve supporting documents relative to the book
reconciling items aggregating P4,161,926.98 and reconcile the difference
in balances between the bank confirmation replies and the amount
recorded in the Statement of Financial Position by preparing bank
reconciliation statements and record the book reconciling items, if
warranted;
2. Municipal Treasurer to:
a. Coordinate with the authorized depository bank and send confirmation
letters to ensure the existence/status/balance of other four accounts namely
the Bus Terminal Equity Account, SEF Loan Account, SEF Grant
Account and SEF Equity Account maintained in LBP-Sogod which were
reported to be inactive accounts as per records of the Municipality; and
b. Confirm the bank status and balance of the Special Education Fund
amounting to P1,080,622.82 which was reported in the Statement of
Financial Position but the corresponding balances of the said account was
not duly confirmed by the bank.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to:
a. Require all the Revenue Collection Clerks to remit to the Municipal
Treasurer the full amount of their collections on a daily basis as required
by COA Circular No. 92-382;
b. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to indicate the date of receipt of
collections and remittances from the Revenue Collection Clerks and
ensure that the deposit of the collections to the Authorized Government
Depository Banks are made daily or on the next banking day; and
c. Require the Municipal Treasurer and the Revenue Collecting Clerks that
the official receipts be written in hard indelible ballpen and the duplicate
and triplicate copies will be clear carbon reproductions in all respects of
whatever may have been written on the original.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and Municipal officials
concerned to:
a. Ensure that all approved PPAs are procurement and
implementation-ready and warrant the implementation of all approved
PPAs for the optimal utilization of the appropriation for development
projects; and
b. Continuously adhere to the provisions of DBM-DOF-DILG Joint
Memorandum Circular No. 1, dated November 4, 2020 to ensure that
maximum attainment of desired socio-economic development and
environmental management outcome of the Municipality is achieved and
for the realization of the intended purpose of the 20% Development
Fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC to:
1. Submit justifications why the specific contractor was awarded with
thirteen (13) contracts despite misrepresenting itself by not declaring all
its on-going contracts. The justification is necessary for the proper
disposition in post - audit of the payments to the contractors, if to be
disallowed in audit; and
2. Equipped themselves with the necessary skills and knowledge related to
procurement by attending seminars/trainings to be able to scrutinize
carefully the eligibility documents submitted by interested bidders so that
any misrepresentations will be detected and to declare as eligible only
those who strictly comply with the provisions of the RIRR of RA 9184.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor, through the BAC to submit
written justification why award of contract was still made to the particular
contractor despite having poor performance in its on-going contracts.

Likewise, we recommended to the BAC to thoroughly scrutinize the post-
qualification of the bidding documents and to require, where necessary,
documents to comprehensively undertake the post-qualification of bidders in
future procurement of infrastructure projects.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to require the:
1. Municipal Accountant to:
a. Record donations in cash under the Trust Liabilities – DRRMF
account in the Trust Fund-DRRM books and ensure that the
donations are duly supported by the Report of Collection and Deposit
(RCD) and supporting documents submitted by the Municipal
Treasurer, or based on the credit memo if the donations are directly
deposited by the donor to the agency bank account;
b. Furnish the Budget Unit copies of the RCD, Journal Entry Voucher
(JEV) and credit memo as bases in the preparation of the Registry of
Cash Donations and Utilization (RCDU);
c. Coordinate with Municipal Budget Officer on the preparation of the
Report on the Receipts and Utilization of DRRMF sourced from GAA
and Cash Donations; and
d. Require the CDUR, duly signed by the head of the requesting unit and
the budget officer, as attachment to the disbursement voucher on the
utilization of cash donations.
2. Municipal Treasurer to:
a. Ensure the received donations in cash should be deposited in the bank
account exclusively maintained for the DRRM fund; and
b. Submit to the Accounting Office a separate RCD for DRRM and
other supporting documents such as Official Receipts and deposit
slips pertaining to the donations in cash received, for recording in the
books of accounts of the Municipality.
3. Municipal Budget Officer to:
a. Prepare RCDU per donor and per purpose, and timely update the
registry by posting the transactions based on the source documents
such as the RCD for the receipt and the CDUR for its utilization; and
b. Coordinate with the Accounting Unit on the preparation of the Report
on the Receipt and Utilization of DRRMF sourced from GAA and
Cash Donations.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to require the:
1. MDRRM Council to include in the LDRRMFIP, under a separate
caption, the list of projects and activities charged to the unexpended
LDRRMF of previous years;
2. Municipal Budget Officer to prepare separate registries of
appropriations, allotments and obligations for Quick Response Fund,
Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses, and Capital Outlay for
LDRRMF; and
3. Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain subsidiary ledgers for
transfers of agency’s unutilized DRRMF to the special trust fund by year
of transfer, among others.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to:
a. Instruct the HRMO to adopt the 2022 Guideline and Standards in the
design of the Municipality’s Organizational Structure and Staffing
Pattern, and issue policies/guidelines/standards/procedures relative to the
hiring/retention/renewal of job order and casual personnel;
b. Comply with the existing daily minimum wage rate for Region VIII as
prescribed by the RTPWB; and
c. Require the Municipal Accountant to process payrolls with complete
documentary requirements and with the complete signatures of the
required signatories of the payroll.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and the Municipal Disaster
Risk Reduction and Management Council ensure that the municipality is
prepared for calamities as prescribed in NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint
Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 by procuring standard disaster response
equipment required in DILG Memorandum Circular 2012-73.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Inventory Committee to conduct/complete the physical count of PPE and submit to the Audit Team copies of complete RPCPPE and RPCLRN in forms prescribed in the existing rules and regulations and ensure that all PPE owned/controlled by the Municipality are included in the physical count and presented per class. Furthermore, require the Inventory Committee and the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the results of the physical count with the accounting records and make the necessary adjustments, if any.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality still has to prioritize the conduct of the physical inventory. The Inventory Committee was not able to conduct the physical count; efforts are on-going.
observations.reason: Non-conduct of inventory precluded reconciliation with accounting records.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following courses of action to rectify the foregoing deficiencies:
The Inventory Committee be directed to segregate the unserviceable properties and equipment from those properties which are still serviceable but no longer needed by the agency and enter the same in the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) pursuant to Section 59, Volume I of LGU NGAS, Manual;
The Municipal Accountant should record as debit to Other Assets (251) and credit to the appropriate PPE accounts the value of obsolete and unserviceable assets including property and equipment no longer used in the agency operation but still serviceable pursuant to Section 140 of LGU NGAS Manual, Volume 3; and
The Municipal Treasurer should apply for the disposal of said unserviceable properties for approval by the Municipal Mayor and Sangguniang Bayan and forward the duly approved IIRUP to the Auditor for his appropriate action pursuant to Section 379 of RA 7160.
observations.managementAction: No actions taken yet by the Management.
observations.reason: Not complied by Management.

Also, coordinate with Pag-ibig on the possible application of the over remittance of employer’s (government) share for succeeding dues on monthly contribution, otherwise, the excessive remittance shall be disallowed in audit pursuant to Section 103 of P.D. 1445.

Further, the Municipal Accountant should request the concerned depository bank to adjust their books of the unrecorded transfer of Bank Account No. 014C-1130-021668 of ₱31, 832.00 and the overstatement in recording the checks No. 18311285 dated May 27, 2009 and Check No. 1831442 dated June 24, 2009 in the amount of ₱500.00 and ₱272.01, respectively.  
Moreover, require the concerned Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to adhere strictly to the provisions of Section 59 of LGU NGAS Manual, Volume I.  Likewise, the Municipal Accountant should make the necessary Journal Entries on the stale checks in accordance with Section 61 of same manual.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to send within ten (10) days before the expiration of the prescribed period a written reminder under signature of the LCE or his duly authorized representative enjoining officials and employees concerned to settle their cash advance; require the Municipal Accountant not to sign claims for additional cash advance until the previous cash advance is settled; compel the Municipal Accountant to cause the immediate withholding of salaries and claims of officers and employees who failed to settle their cash advances within the prescribed period after being sent notice and demand; require the Municipal Accountant to stop the practice of offsetting the amount due to the accountable officer (for reimbursement) against his other outstanding cash advances; and require the Municipal Accountant to furnish all department heads listings of all unliquidated cash advances of employees under their charge to enhance efforts to effect liquidation.
observations.reason: Not all cash advances were liquidated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: a. Compel the Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant a duly certified list showing the names of the taxpayers and the amounts due and collectible from them for the current and prior years; b. Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare journal entries to record the uncollected RPT and SET of the current and prior years, and henceforth, to account for the receivables annually, including discounts granted and penalties imposed; and c. Direct the Municipal Assessor to clean up the Assessment Role to establish a realistic and accurate RPT and SET Receivable.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to fast-track the implementation of priority programs, projects and activities funded by the 20% DF, BUB and LGSF to provide constituents of the benefits due them.
observations.reason: Funds were not fully utilized.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: 1. Require the Municipal Accountant to instruct the personnel in-charge in the preparation of the remittance of employees and employer due to GSIS to adhere strictly to the provisions of RA; 2. Require the Municipal Treasurer that the amount withheld shall be remitted to GSIS in full within 10 days following the end of the month the withholding was made or the withholding was accrued; and 3. Require the concerned employee in-charge in the preparation of GSIS remittances and balance to conduct thorough reconciliation with its records with that of the GSIS.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant failed to reconcile said balance due to lack of documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive directs the Municipal Accountant coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer to identify the employees with unremitted contributions to enable the Municipal Government to settle its obligations. The Municipal Accountant should also regularly reconcile the amounts withheld and remitted to Pag-IBIG in order to ascertain the accuracy and reliability of the balances of Due to Pag-IBIG account in the financial statements.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant failed to reconcile said balance due to lack of documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor consider creating and organizing an Internal Audit Services, in compliance with R.A. No 4177 and AO 70 to give assurance for a sound and adequate internal control system in the operations of the Municipality.
observations.reason: Deficient funding to focus on the implementation; no IAS created.

observations.recommendation: We further recommended that whoever is appointed to perform physical count shall undergo a training on Property and Supply Management System (PSMS), to gain adequate understanding and working knowledge of the systems, processes, policies and other regulations pertaining to property supply and management.
observations.reason: The inventory committee did not conduct periodic physical count and did not submit RPCPPE.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous year recommendation for strict compliance to Sections 74 and 111 of PD 1445 to avoid incurrence of negative balance in the Cash in Bank account. Specifically, we suggested the following remedial steps: 1. Create a task force that will take charge in the preparation of the BRS for the five LBP accounts setting a time-frame for its completion. The team is required to prepare and submit to the head of the agency monthly progress reports with emphasis on the reconciling items, copy furnished the audit team. 2. Make written representations with the depository banks to ensure prompt submission of the Bank statements. 3. Maintain an updated SL which will show the details or breakdown of the balance of the controlling account appearing in the general ledger. 4. Require the Municipal Accountant to exert enough effort for the reconciliation of the negative balance reflected in the books of accounts against the balance of the bank statements as of December 31, 2013, and effect adjustment thereof.
observations.reason: No task force was created; in process of preparing the updated SL.

observations.recommendation: 1. Require the Municipal Accountant to strictly follow with accounting procedures contained in Section 105-110 of chapter 6, Manual on the New government Accounting System for LGUs regarding maintenance of special accounts and subsidiary ledger in the General Fund; and Section 5.0 of COA Circular 2012-002 for reporting on the Sources and Utilization of Fund for LDRRMF. 2. Require the Municipal Accountant to always assign special accounts and maintain separate subsidiary ledgers in the GF for the LGUs 5% GAD & 5% LDRRMF in order to have readily adequate and reliable information as to assets, liabilities and the corresponding object of expenditures, fund balance, and if these were in line with the guidelines.
observations.reason: Subsidiary ledgers were not maintained.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Inventory Committee to: conduct a complete physical count; require the General Services Office to prepare written guidelines prior to inventory taking with clear instructions and define roles of the members of the Inventory Committee to ensure the timely completion and submission of the RPCPPE; require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to determine the validity of the PPE account through reconciliation of the accounting records and the actual inventory; and require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the PPE Ledger Card (PPELC) for each class of PPE listing therein the data as to its acquisition, description, custody, estimated life, rate of depreciation, disposal, and other information about the property, plant and equipment, based on the source documents of the transactions and drop from the books of accounts the unserviceable properties after the Waste Material Report and the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property are duly approved and evaluated by the COA.
observations.reason: Inventory committee did not submit RPCPPE; subsidiary ledger cards and property cards were not maintained and updated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: a. Require the Municipal Accountant to send within ten (10) days before the expiration of the prescribed period a written reminder under signature of the LCE or his duly authorized representative enjoining officials and employees concerned to settle their cash advance; b. Direct the Municipal Accountant not to sign claims for additional cash advance until the previous cash advance is settled; c. Compel the Municipal Accountant to cause the immediate withholding of salaries and claims of officers and employees who failed to settle their cash advances within the prescribed period after being sent notice and demand; d. Require the Municipal Accountant to stop the practice of offsetting the amount due to the accountable officer (for reimbursement) against his other outstanding cash advances; and e. Require the Municipal Accountant to furnish all department heads listings of all unliquidated cash advances of employees under their charge to enhance efforts to effect liquidation.
observations.reason: Not all cash advances were liquidated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: a. Compel the Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant a duly certified list showing the names of the taxpayers and the amounts due and collectible from them for the current and prior years; b. Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare journal entries to record the uncollected RPT and SET of the current and prior years, and henceforth, to account for the receivables annually, including discounts granted and penalties imposed; and c. Direct the Municipal Assessor to clean up the Assessment Role to establish a realistic and accurate RPT and SET Receivable.
observations.reason: No basis for the preparation of journal entries; still in process.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Planning and Development Officer, Municipal Engineer, Municipal Agriculturist, Local Disaster and Risk Reduction Management (LDRRM) Focal Person, and Municipal Social Work and Development Officer (MSWDO) to judiciously plan proposed projects and fast-track the implementation of said programs, projects and activities funded by the BUB to provide constituents the maximum benefits due them.
observations.reason: Not all projects were fully implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Assessor to expedite the processing of documents for the titling of lands owned by the Municipality. We further recommended that before any construction is made, land ownership thru a certificate of title is obtained.
observations.reason: The land in question is not titled yet.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor communicate with PCSO to follow-up the documentary support relative to the donated ambulance vehicle and to request for the disposal of the said vehicle.
observations.reason: The Ambulance vehicle is not in running condition.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor institutionalize the LDRRMO by filling up the statutory/mandatory positions required under NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) 2014-1 to assure implementation of actions and measures related to disaster risk reduction and management.
observations.reason: The position is not fully yet filled up.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Accountant to send demand letter to those employees who have not yet settled their disallowances, and strictly monitor the settlement of suspension and disallowances.
observations.reason: Unsettled.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to exert effort in enforcing the immediate settlement of audit disallowances and, ensure that all employees who are retiring or transferring to other agencies shall first settle the disallowances and charges for which they are liable in accordance with Section 7 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.
observations.reason: Unsettled.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor institutionalize the LDRRMO by filling up the statutory/mandatory positions required under NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC JMC 2014-1 to assure implementation of actions and measures related to disaster risk reduction and management.
observations.reason: The position is not fully yet filled up.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Inventory Committee to conduct the physical inventory and instruct them to commence the conduct of physical inventory.
observations.reason: The inventory committee did not conduct periodic physical count and did not submit RPCPPE.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: a. Cause the creation of a task force that will take charge in the preparation of the BRS for the nine LBP accounts setting a time-frame for its completion; b. Make written representations with the depository banks to ensure prompt submission of the Bank statements; c. Compel the Municipal Accountant to maintain an updated subsidiary ledger; and d. Compel the Municipal Accountant to maintain an updated subsidiary ledger.
observations.reason: No task force was created; in process of preparing the updated SL.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of PPE and train members to ensure timely completion and submission of RPCPPE; require the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant to determine validity of PPE through reconciliation; prepare PPE Ledger Cards and drop unserviceable properties from books after proper reports and COA evaluation.
observations.reason: Subsidiary ledger cards and property cards were not maintained and updated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require Municipal Accountant to send reminder before expiration, not sign claims for additional advances until previous are settled, withhold salaries of those who fail to settle, stop offsetting reimbursements against other advances, and furnish department heads listings of all unliquidated cash advances.
observations.reason: Not all cash advances were liquidated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Municipal Treasurer to furnish Municipal Accountant certified list of taxpayers and amounts; Municipal Accountant to prepare journal entries to record uncollected RPT and SET; Municipal Assessor to clean up Assessment Role.
We recommended Municipal Mayor fast-track implementation of priority programs, projects and activities funded by 20% DF, BUB and LGSF.
observations.reason: Funds were not fully utilized.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Municipal Accountant instruct personnel to adhere to RA provisions; Municipal Treasurer remit amounts within 10 days following the end of the month; conduct reconciliation with GSIS.
observations.reason: Municipal Accountant failed to reconcile due to lack of documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Municipal Accountant coordinate with Municipal Treasurer to identify employees with unremitted contributions to settle obligations; reconcile amounts withheld and remitted to Pag-IBIG regularly.
observations.reason: Municipal Accountant failed to reconcile due to lack of documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended creating and organizing IAS in compliance with R.A. No 4177 and AO 70 for sound internal control.
observations.reason: Deficient funding; no IAS created.

observations.recommendation: We recommended provision of financial and technical assistance to barangays for establishment and operations of functional MRFs and wide dissemination of information on waste reduction.
observations.reason: Some barangays yet to be provided financial and technical assistance; construction of perimeter fence with gate is still for implementation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended strict compliance to Sections 74 and 111 of PD 1445 and creation of a task force to prepare BRS for five LBP accounts, make written representations with banks to ensure prompt bank statement submission, maintain updated SL and reconcile negative balance reflected in books.
observations.reason: No task force created; in process of preparing updated SL.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Municipal Accountant comply with NGAS procedures on maintenance of special accounts and subsidiary ledgers; assign special accounts and maintain separate subsidiary ledgers for 5% GAD and 5% LDRRMF.
observations.reason: Subsidiary ledgers were not maintained.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management prepare a supplemental
AIP/LDRRMFIP duly approved by the local sanggunian.

We recommended that management advise the GSO and other office
concerned with inventory of supplies to adhere to the provision of the
NGAS on the management of inventories, and conduct physical count
of inventories at least twice a year.

We recommended that management compel the MSWDO or the proper
office charged with the preparation of the report of
distribution for the immediate submission thereof to the accounting unit, to fully account the
food supplies inventory and to correct the financial statements.

We recommended that the Management fully adhere with the
provisions of COA Circular 2015-008.

We recommended that management advise officials and employees to
liquidate immediately their outstanding cash advances pursuant to
COA Circular No. 97-002.

We recommended that management submit the report of all on-going
government PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the
year pursuant to the full disclosure policy and to provide the public
with a complete information.

We recommended that management provide appropriate documentation upon issuance of the PPE or semi-expendable items and keep records for their monitoring; and regularly reconcile these with the Accounting Office’s records.

We recommended that management reclassify the funds received for typhoon Odette victims as Trust Liability-LDRRMF in the Trust Fund books and maintain a separate subsidiary ledger by transferring agency and other sources by donor in compliance with COA Circular No. 2012-002 and COA Circular 2014-002.

We recommended the immediate submission of the Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts (SCBAA) for CY 2021, and to ensure its timely future submissions as prescribed under Paragraph 21 of IPSAS 1 and Paragraph 14 of IPSAS 24.

We recommended that Management update the Notes to Financial Statements submitted to the COA and affecting the requirement of comparative presentation of data with that of the preceding year, and henceforth, comply with the guidelines on the FS presentation as provided in Paragraph 53 of IPSAS.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
• Establish an Inventory committee to conduct the physical count of PPE of the
Municipality and submit the RPCPPE not later than January 31 of the following
year, pursuant to Section 124 of the MNGAS;
• Require the Municipal Treasurer to prepare and maintain Property Cards, and
reconcile their records regularly with the Accounting Office; and
• Prepare and submit the approved Physical Inventory Plan to the Audit Team at
least 10 days before the scheduled start of inventory taking activities in adopting
COA Circular 2020-006 relative to one time cleansing of PPE accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile
the GL and SL balances of the CIB-LCCA as well as the individual TF balances of the
three bank accounts and other assets with its corresponding liabilities to establish the
correct TF account balances.
We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant make the necessary
adjusting entry/ies after reconciliation to reflect the correct TF account balances in the books of
accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
make the adjusting entry by debiting the Prior Period Adjustment account by
P175,500.00 and crediting the Advances to Officers and Employees account by the same amount.

We also reiterated our prior year’s recommendation to demand the concerned
officials and employees to liquidate immediately the remaining unliquidated cash
advances of prior years. Otherwise, implement the withholding of their salaries.

Further, we reiterated our recommendation for the adjustment of the negative
balances of the Advances to Officers and Employees account of prior years.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to conduct a complete physical count of the PPE and reconcile the same with the records of the Accounting Office and submit the RPCPPE to the Audit Team within the reglementary period.  Further we recommend that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer maintain the required subsidiary ledgers and property cards, respectively, for each type of PPE and update the PAR every three years or every time there is a change in custodianship / user of the property.

We recommended that Management revise the existing guidelines to introduce a different method of determining the amount of financial assistance to be given under the AICS Program and to include criteria to be used in screening and identifying the Program’s beneficiaries.

We recommended that Management:
Follow the prescribed PO form/template and include the provisions on liquidated damages pursuant to the NGAS Manual and Section 68 of R.A. No. 9184 for the protection of the Municipality in case of supplier’s failure to perform or deliver the goods or services on time.
Be guided on the forms/templates, particularly the PO form, as prescribed under the NGAS Manual.

We recommended that Management direct the BAC to submit the FDA issued License to Operate of the awarded supplier to regularize these procurement transactions. Further, we recommended that, henceforth, Management should ensure that eligible suppliers have acquired the appropriate permits and licenses before awarding the contracts.

We recommended that Management submit the lacking documents required to support the payment of hazard pay to the MSWD Officer and stop the practice of granting hazard pay at a rate not prescribed by the law. Henceforth, we recommend that Management follow the rules and regulations on the grant of Magna Carta benefits to PSWs and PSWDWs as provided in the DBM-DSWD JC No. 1 s. 2018.

We recommended that Management submit justification on the non-imposition of liquidated damages (LD) on the delayed completion of the project. Also, we recommend that Management recoup the overpayment, representing the un-imposed LD, from the supplier or from the person/s responsible for the non-computation of the LD.

We recommended that Management submit its legal basis on the inclusion of lot acquisition under the Build and Design Scheme for evaluation by the Audit Team and to appropriately make manifestation with the National Housing Authority on the noted deficiency in the aforementioned Terms of Reference.

We recommended that Management submit a written explanation why the posting of performance security was made after the contract was signed and executed. Also, we recommended the Management to require all winning bidders of infrastructure projects to submit performance securities within the prescribed period.
We recommended that Management submit the required Permits to Purchase and to Sell as required by NTC in procuring radio communications equipment to prevent disallowance in audit.

We recommended that the Management, through the BAC Chairperson, comply with the posting requirements of the complete bidding documents, minutes of pre-bid conferences, the NTP and the related approved contracts on the Municipality’s website and in PhilGEPS as stipulated in the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.

1.8 We recommended that:
1. The Municipal Accountant reconcile the balance per book with the balance
per bank for the Cash in Bank – Local Currency, Current Account, trace the
details of the differences, and record the necessary adjusting entries;
2. The Municipal Treasurer to prepare at year end a List of Unreleased
Checks to be attached to the Report of Checks Issued and submit to the
Municipal Accountant and COA Auditor;
3. The Municipal Accountant should restore unreleased checks by preparing a
journal entry voucher at the end of the year based on the list submitted
from the MTO. At the start of the ensuing year, draw another entry to
reverse the previous entry made and recognize the availability of the checks
for release; and
4. The Municipal Accountant prepare the monthly Bank Reconciliation
Statements.

2.7 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
1. Obtain all source documents from the source agencies to review the terms
and conditions corresponding to the funds transferred in relation to the
implementation of programs and projects;
2. Determine if the specific programs or projects for which the funds were
transferred were implemented, goods/services were delivered, liquidation
of funds were already submitted and received by the source agencies, and
the liabilities were settled for proper adjustments in the books;
3. Return to the source agency any unused/unexpended balance; and
4. Request authority for the write off of dormant fund transfer accounts in
accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

3.12 In order to establish the regularity of the grant of the AHDP/SRA, we
recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to
immediately submit the lacking supporting documents for the proper
disposition in audit of the disbursement. Henceforth, only disbursement
vouchers/payrolls with complete supporting documents should be processed
for payment.

4.8 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require:
a. The Municipal Social and Development Officer together with the Property Supply
Officer to conduct physical count of all the unissued donated items;
b. Prepare inventory list of the unissued donated items for DRRM every month or
until all donations are fully distributed;
c. Henceforth, prepare a Report on the Receipt and Distribution/Issuance of Donated
Relief Goods and Report on the Receipt and Utilization/Issuance of Donated PPE
to account and monitor the receipt and issuances of all donations in kind and
submit a copy of all the reports to the Audit Team;
d. The Municipal Supply Officer to furnish the Municipal Accountant with the
duplicate copies of all Acknowledgement Receipts of in-kind donations and
inventory list of donated items for DRRM; and
e. The Municipal Accountant to summarize data from the documents submitted by
the Municipal Social and Development Officer and Supply Officer, compare
records with the Registry of Donated Relief Goods for DRRM maintained by the
Municipal Social and Development Officer for check and balance, and disclose
necessary information in the Notes to Financial Statements.

5.7 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant
to:
a) Submit the lacking documentary requirements presented in Table 1, such as the
Waste Material Report, Proof of Distribution to the
Beneficiaries/Distribution List, Program of Works. This is without
prejudice to the issuance of Notices of Suspension as a result of our audit; and
b) Henceforth, secure complete documentation on all disbursements and ensure the
validity and propriety of the disbursement transactions.
Significantly, adhere to the policy of non-payment of claims without complete
documentation.

6.9 We recommended for the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal
Development Council to oversee the implementation of the PPAs through
direct monitoring of the accomplishments of the Municipal Engineer of the
projects determined to be accomplished during the year. Ensure that the
priority projects funded from the 20% DF will be finished on time to give a
reasonable assurance that the socio-economic outcomes for the LGU will be
addressed.

6.10 Also, we recommended that unused balances for completed projects totaling
P71,869.48 be made available for re-appropriation for other projects under the
20% Development Fund.

7.9 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the LDRRMO Head to
ensure full utilization of funds and implementation of approved PPAs
incorporated in the LDRRM Fund Investment Plan and AIP.

a. Issue a demand letter to individuals and all accountable officers who are not already connected with the LGU for the immediate liquidation/ settlement of their unliquidated cash advances and in case of non-compliance, find any legal remedies to compel the liquidation/settlement of cash advances; and
b. Make necessary actions for the proper reporting of dormant accounts by submitting request for write-off of dormant accounts and its underlying supporting documents to prove that the accounts are no longer collectible.
observations.managementAction: Demand letters were issued to the concerned accountable officers who are not already connected with the LGU for the immediate liquidation/ settlement of their unliquidated cash advances and will continue to deduct from their payroll, if applicable.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Management instruct the Inventory Committee to conduct actual count of all its inventories as at year-end, then furnish the Accounting Office for recording. Instruct the GSO to submit the Report of Materials Issued together with the distribution list and/or issuance slips to the Accounting Office as basis for drawing the Journal Entry Voucher.
observations.managementAction: The Inventory Committee is continually reconciling the inventory report movement to arrive at a correct figure. Demand letters have been forwarded to the MSWDO requesting to submit the required liquidation reports.
observations.reason: Also, the MSWDO is yet to submit the beneficiaries together with the Relief Distribution Sheet (RDS) to substantiate the materials related to relief distribution facilitated by the MSWD.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management instruct the Inventory Committee to conduct a physical inventory of all the local road networks and reconcile the local road inventory with the records of the accounting office and strictly follow the provisions of COA Circular 2015-008.
observations.managementAction: Have set the schedule the conduct of physical count. Report required by COA Circular 2015-008 was submitted.
observations.reason: However, road components is yet to be reported until proper valuation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management stop charging ineligible expenses to 5% LDRRMF; allocate additional funds for regular operation expenses of the LDRRM EOC Office; and follow strictly the regulation in the utilization of the 5% Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund as provided under Section 5.1 of the Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 of DBM and DILG dated March 25, 2013.
observations.managementAction: Adhere to the regulation in the utilization of the 5% Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund as provided under Section 5.1 of the Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 of DBM and DILG dated March 25, 2013.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management submit the required supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor immediately. Henceforth, all disbursements should be supported with complete documentation to establish the validity and regularity of transactions.
observations.managementAction: Provided the required eligibility documents immediately.

observations.recommendation: 1. Direct the concerned personnel to prepare the required summary list together with the required supporting documents and reports on cash and in-kind and submit the same to COA; 2. Post the reports on the receipt and utilization of DRRMF sourced from GAA and in-kind in the LGU website; 3. Instruct the GSO to coordinate with the MSWDO and Mayor’s Office with regards to the donated items to be able to maintain inventory list thereof; 4. Strictly adhere to COA Circular Nos. 2014-002 and 2020-009 on the accounting and reporting guidelines of cash and in-kind donations.
observations.managementAction: 1. Submitted together with the required supporting documents and reports on cash donations and in-kind and submit the same to COA; 2. Post the reports on the receipt and utilization of DRRMF sourced from GAA and in-kind in the LGU website; 3. Instruct the GSO to coordinate with the MSWDO and Mayor’s Office with regards to the donated items to be able to maintain inventory list thereof; 4. Strictly adhere to COA Circular Nos. 2014-002 and 2020-009 on the accounting and reporting guidelines of cash and in-kind donations.

observations.recommendation: 1. Instruct the Municipal Engineer to submit the list of on-going PPAs and those projects to be implemented and require all contractors to install billboards on site containing complete and updated information; 2. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to require attachment of photos of the installed billboards at the project site upon payment of Mobilization Costs or First Progress billing. Also, collect the cost of materials and labor under pay item project billboards from all contractors for the uninstalled project billboards; 3. Strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004 for timely verification of the PPAs’ status and validation of photos of projects/programs/activities installed signboards and/or public notices and on the information and publicity on Programs/Projects/Activities of government agencies as well as the provisions of law on full public disclosure policy.
observations.managementAction: 1. Instruct the Municipal Engineer to submit the list of on-going PPAs and those projects to be implemented and require all contractors to install billboards on site containing complete and updated information; 2. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to require attachment of photos of the installed billboards at the project site upon payment of Mobilization Costs or First Progress billing. Also, collect the cost of materials and labor under pay item project billboards from all contractors for the uninstalled project billboards; 3. Strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004 for timely verification of the PPAs’ status and validation of photos of projects/programs/activities installed signboards and/or public notices and on the information and publicity on Programs/Projects/Activities of government agencies as well as the provisions of law on full public disclosure policy.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management to direct the Municipal Engineering Office and the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC), through its secretariat, to submit all contracts, together with all the supporting documents forming part thereof, within 5 days from its perfection to the Auditor and technical evaluation and to ascertain the regularity of the procurement transactions as required in COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.
observations.managementAction: BAC, through its secretariat, submitted all contracts, together with all the supporting documents forming part thereof, within 5 days from its perfection to the Auditor and technical evaluation and to ascertain the regularity of the procurement transactions as required in COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.
observations.reason: Institute more stringent measures in the evaluation of suppliers of projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management to direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC), through its secretariat, to submit all contracts, together with all the supporting documents forming part thereof, within 5 days from its perfection to the Auditor for auditorial review and technical evaluation and to ascertain the regularity of the transactions as required in COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009. The Municipal Engineering Office shall submit the required supporting documents to effect the necessary inspection of the completed infrastructure projects.
observations.managementAction: The Bids and Awards Committee (BAC), through its secretariat submitted all contracts, together with all the supporting documents forming part thereof, within 5 days from its perfection to the Auditor for auditorial review and technical evaluation and to ascertain the regularity of the transactions as required in COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.
observations.reason: The Municipal Engineering Office is yet to submit the required supporting documents to effect the necessary inspection of the completed infrastructure projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to withhold the amount of P1,070,646.88 during the payment of the contract and instruct the Bids and Awards Committee to strictly adhere to the guidelines in the preparation of the Approved Budget of the Contract set forth in DPWH Department Order No. 197 series of 2016.
observations.managementAction: Local Engineering Office prepared Variation Order to facilitate the deficiency.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management, through the Municipal Local School Board to maximize the utilization of the Special Education Fund by full implementation of all planned programs and projects in a year in order to attain the objectives for which the fund was intended and to provide benefits for the students.
observations.managementAction: Conducted Local School Board meeting on April 13 and 19, 2021 to formulate plans for the full implementation of all planned programs and projects in a year in order to attain the objectives for which the fund was intended.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive conduct regular monitoring of the implementation of GAD Plan and Budget to ensure the full utilization of the fund. Also, submit the Annual GAD Accomplishment Report and its supporting documents within 5 days from end of January of the preceding year.
observations.managementAction: Submitted the GAD Accomplishment Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management, through the Municipal Peace and Order Council, to maximize the utilization of the POPs Fund by full implementation of all planned programs and projects in a year in order to attain the objectives for which the fund was intended.
observations.managementAction: Evaluated the Peace and Order Programs to facilitate billing of unbilled projects that contribute to the low utilization of the fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the implementing office to fast track the implementation of the programs/projects so that targeted beneficiaries can enjoy the intended benefits therefrom. Activities for the utilization of funds are set to be implemented this year.
observations.managementAction: Projects identified to be eligible for liquidation have been liquidated.
observations.reason: Activities for utilization of funds are set to be implemented this year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management.
observations.managementAction: Management had commenced tagging its property with already issued property numbers but has not covered all of their properties. It only needs to issue property numbers issued in year 2021. It only needs to issue property numbers issued on previous years
observations.reason: Partially Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed to provide updated property numbers for the respective equipment.
observations.managementAction: Management will comply with the audit recommendation and have already assigned responsible personnel to exercise due diligence in issuing properties to be accompanied with the required documents.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II Finding No. 1.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management direct the concerned accountable officers to accomplish the necessary report/s and/or procedure in property disposal as provided in the Manual on Property and Supply Management and use the reports (IIRUP) as the basis of the de-recognition of the property from the inventory.
observations.managementAction: Management will comply with the audit recommendation and have already initiated progress on the implementation of the return of the unserviceable properties. In fact they have already gathered unserviceable items for disposal and was able to issue IIRUP on the properties.
observations.reason: Currently, upon validation of the LGUs action, the Management was starting on the disposal of these properties per COAs advice of the process of disposal, it just happened the LGU suffered damages caused by Typhoon Odette and affected their processes.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed to submit the Report on Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment for CY 2020 and adhere to the prescribed date of its submission for CY 2021 and succeeding years.
observations.managementAction: Management had already complied with the audit recommendation.
observations.reason: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management strengthen the GAD Focal Point System of the LGU by (1) providing enough proficient personnel to support the Focal Person; (2) require the Focal Person together with her team to: (a) develop and maintain GAD database containing gender statistics and sex-disaggregated data, gathered systematically, among the LGU population and clients; (b) regularly update these data; (c) conduct gender analysis on these data for planning, programming and policy formulation; (d) implement the identified PPAs; and (e) closely monitor the progress of activities to meet the objectives and realize the performance indicators and targets; (3) ensure that the gender issues are clearly stated in the GAD Plan and design and formulate its PPAs according to the specific identified gender issues to address and resolve the same, supported with valid analyses.
observations.managementAction: Management had already complied with the audit recommendation.
observations.reason: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Mayor, together with the key personnel of the LGU like the Municipal Accountant and the MPDC, were directed to convene with the SMWD officers together with the respective resident Auditors. Clarify the intention of both parties as regards the transfer of the waterworks/sewerage facilities, including the water rights of the LGU’s Water System, to the SMWD in exchange of the latter’s payment of P5,000,000.00. Amend the provisions in the project study and the MOA that are found conflicting with the established intentions of the parties. Ensure that the municipality and the water district should have a uniform interpretation of the provision/stipulation stated in the project study and MOA. Adjust the PPE account of the LGU based on the established/clarified intentions of the parties and in accordance with the accounting standard set in IPSAS No. 1.
observations.managementAction: The Management asserted that there was no transfer of ownership of the water system to the SMWD. The LGU had already talked with the SMWD parties of the management on this matter.
observations.reason: The recommendation is considered implemented as what is being set-forth and practiced by the parties. No issues noted by the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: Submit to COA the justification on the payments made amounting to P156,749.46 which shall be evaluated on whether to issue Notice of Suspension or Disallowance; Instruct the MDRRMO to carefully plan and include only in the LDRRMFIP the PAPs that are considered very important and urgent; Require the MBO to prepare and furnish COA the Reports on the Receipt and Utilization of Cash Donations; Advise the MDRRMO to timely submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to COA and ensure compliance to applicable provisions.
observations.managementAction: Management corrected the practice and submitted the justification regarding the identified disbursements.
observations.reason: However, previous transactions cannot be undone and the Receipt and Utilization report remain unsubmitted.

observations.recommendation: Formulate and comply the regular collection of non-recyclable and special wastes; include budget for protective equipment; procure proper collection vehicle; enter MOA for temporary dumping site; locate sanitary landfill sites and expedite establishment; adhere to R.A. No. 9003 provisions.
observations.managementAction: Management has yet to design a process of special wastes of the LGU. The Management has implemented other recommendations apart that of the landfill provision. The Municipality has encountered difficulties securing Environmental Compliance Certificate or ECC. As of today, the LGU is on process of securing the permit in the area located in Brgy Junrado, San Francisco, SDN.
observations.reason: The Management has implemented other recommendations apart that of the landfill provision.

observations.recommendation: Direct the GFPS to formulate the LGU GPB based on the GAD Agenda; ensure implementation of GAD activities; formulate and include only GAD PAPs that resolve identified gender issues; adhere to R.A. 9710 and PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 provisions.
observations.managementAction: The Management satisfactorily complied with the recommendation.
observations.reason: The Management complied with certification by the DILG.

observations.recommendation: Enact an ordinance promulgating rental fee rates for municipal facilities; formulate operating procedures covering utilization, booking form, OR issuance, cleaning responsibilities, maintenance, grievance measures and safeguards to protect properties.
observations.managementAction: Management already endorsed this concern to the Sanguniang Bayan. Ordinance is under consideration for update responsive to the current status of the LGU. Issue was brought-up with the management in the exit conference to remind them.
observations.reason: Ordinance is under consideration for update responsive to the current status of the LGU.

observations.recommendation: Execute a comprehensive set of guidelines/policies and MOA governing the fund transfers for operational fund to the barangays, clearly providing legal basis, objectives, duties and responsibilities of municipality and barangays, penalties, and ensure liquidation at year-end; adhere to Section 2 of P.D. No. 1445 and Item 3.0 of COA Circular No. 2012-001.
observations.managementAction: The LGU satisfactorily implemented the recommendation.
observations.reason: The LGU satisfactorily implemented the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer in coordination with the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer (MSWDO) to submit to the Audit Team the SACs, IDs, valid photographs of the recipients during the actual payout, and other supporting documents of the aforementioned 22 SAP recipients.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality submitted to the Audit Team the SACs, IDs, photographs of the recipients during the actual payout, and other supporting documents of the 22 SAP recipients.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to have their records updated and reconciled and subsequently make the necessary adjustments for the discrepancies in their respective records. Furthermore, require the Municipal Accountant to: a. continue exerting efforts in retrieving relevant documents to reconcile the relevant prior years. b. make the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts relative to the unrecorded reconciling items in the BRSs and errors in disbursements of CY 2020.
observations.managementAction: Records from the office have been updated. Exerted efforts in retrieving relevant documents to reconcile the relevant prior years. The necessary adjustments were made to record the reconciling items and errors for CY 2020.
observations.reason: Difficulty in retrieving documents from prior years.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to require the MCs to remit their collections daily.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer required the MCs to remit their collection daily.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to retrieve relevant documents regarding the recordings made on the respective accounts and reconcile the accounts. Prepare the necessary adjustments where appropriate.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality retrieved the relevant documents. The Municipal Accountant already made the adjusting entries in the books of accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all cash advances are liquidated at the end of each year. Furthermore, instruct the concerned officials/employees/accountable officer to immediately liquidate the remaining cash advances. Otherwise, implement the withholding of their respective salaries. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to: a. continue exerting effort in the collection of relevant documents that will support the reconciliation of the accounts’ balances; b. make the necessary adjustments pertaining to the errors in the respective accounts; and c. henceforth, ensure that all transactions are properly recorded.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant exerted effort in locating relevant documents. The Municipal Accountant prepared the adjustments in the books of accounts and ensured that all transactions were properly recorded. Demand will be sent to accountable officers/employees once review is completed.
observations.reason: Prior years cash advances are yet to be submitted to the Accounting Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to provide the details or documents to identify the unidentified/unaccounted account of P997,775.71. We also recommended to adjust/refund to employees over-deduction of loan payments in salary payrolls after verification of supporting documents.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant retrieved the relevant documents and provided the details. The Municipal Accountant prepared the necessary adjustments after verification of supporting documents.
observations.reason: The Municipality still has to prioritize the reconciliation of the Due from Officers and Employees account; reconciliation is still on-going.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Assessor to: a. exert effort in providing the breakdown or details of the recorded land account amounting to P2,091,433.00 or reconcile the same with the unrecorded lots owned by the Municipality; b. facilitate the titling of all untitled lots owned by the Municipality and ensure that all titles are in the custody and safekeeping of the Municipal Treasurer; and c. prepare the necessary appraisal/valuation as basis to record unrecorded parcels of land.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality exerted efforts in identifying the breakdown of the land account. The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Assessor exerted efforts in identifying the breakdown and locating the documents. Titling of five out of nine untitled parcels of land were started in CY 2021.
observations.reason: Identification of the unrecorded/titled lots is on-going.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Municipal Engineer in providing the details or breakdown of the unidentified CIP amounting to P513,625.01 and the supporting documents of the non-moving CIP amounting to P4,042,700.44. Subsequently, make the appropriate adjustments in the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant retrieved the supporting documents and prepared the adjustments in the CIP accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to continue his effort in retrieving all relevant documents such as Statement of Receipts and Disbursements (SORDs) for all funds received from the National Government to establish the details or breakdown of the aforementioned unidentified balances and make necessary adjustments, if any.
observations.managementAction: Some of the SORDs were retrieved. The Municipal Accountant exerted efforts in retrieving the relevant documents and prepared the necessary adjustments thereof.
observations.reason: On-going reconciliation of the Due to NGAs account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to continue gathering all relevant documents regarding the Cash in Bank reconciliation of the Due to LGUs account and make the necessary adjustments; reclassify the DPWH’s share in building permit fees from Due to LGUs account to Due to NGAs account. Consequently, require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to remit the amount due to the respective LGUs/agency. Henceforth, ensure that the subsidiary ledgers of the Due to LGUs account pertaining to the Provincial Government and Barangays of the Municipality are properly maintained.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant prioritized the reconciliation of the Cash in Bank accounts and other accounts in CY 2021 and committed to reconcile the Due to LGUs account in CY 2022. The Municipal Accountant committed to properly maintain the subsidiary ledgers of the Due to LGUs account in CY 2022.
observations.reason: Reconciliation is on-going; committed to reconcile in CY 2022.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to retrieve relevant documents regarding the reconciliation of the abovementioned accounts and make the necessary adjustments thereof.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant retrieved relevant documents and reconciled the abovementioned accounts and made the necessary adjustments.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Accountant continue their efforts in retrieving the unsubmitted DVs in CY 2017 and oblige the former Municipal Accountant to comply with its submission to the Audit Team for verification and post audit. We also recommended to consider appropriate legal action, if warranted.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality sent a letter to the former Municipal Accountant demanding the missing DVs. The former Municipal Accountant was not able to attend any of the requested meeting with the Municipal Mayor and Municipal Accountant in CY 2021 due to COVID-19 pandemic. The Municipality still has to consider the filing of appropriate legal action, if warranted.
observations.reason: The former Municipal Accountant was not able to attend requested meetings due to COVID-19 pandemic; further action pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to submit to the Audit Team the Monthly Report of Official Travels and Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption for each motor vehicle duly supported with Driver’s Trip Tickets issued during the month for review and evaluation. Henceforth, see to it that the Driver’s Trip Tickets were properly accomplished.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer required the submission of the Monthly Report of Official Travels and Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption. Data on the Driver’s Trip Tickets were provided. Some monthly reports were prepared per vehicle.
observations.reason: Regular submission of the monthly reports is yet to be complied.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Secretariat to submit to the Audit Team the copies of the contracts and purchase orders as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2009-001 together with its supporting documents.
observations.managementAction: The BAC Secretariat committed to submit the required Contracts/POs with complete supporting documents within the prescribed period.
observations.reason: Submission of some Contracts/POs with complete supporting documents is still pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer/BAC Chairperson to: a. immediately submit to the SA and the ATL the list of all on-going PPAs of the Municipality and those that are to be implemented in CY 2021. Henceforth, to submit the same reports at the beginning of the succeeding year; b. inform the Audit Team within ten days after the award of PPAs or before its start that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices were already posted for validation; and c. regularly submit the monthly monitoring report.
observations.managementAction: Some reports were submitted. During ocular inspection, the Audit Team noted that signboards or public notices were posted. Regular submission of monthly monitoring reports is yet to be complied.
observations.reason: Regular submission of the monthly monitoring reports is still pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MENRO to complete the conduct of WACS and subsequently secure the approval of the 10-year SWM Plan from the NSWMC. Furthermore, require the MENRO to continue their efforts in establishing the SLF.
observations.managementAction: Conduct of WACS was completed and incorporated in the prepared SWMP. Procurement of the lot for the SLF is on-going.
observations.reason: On-going efforts in establishment of the SLF; data collection not completed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to: a. reclassify the financial assistance from various Municipalities, Provincial Government of Quezon, and National Government to TL-DRRMF account; b. reconcile the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF with the general ledger balance of the account in CY 2017 and make the necessary adjustments; c. transfer the P2,000.00 savings to the TF; and d. prepare and submit to the Audit Team on a monthly basis the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF. Furthermore, instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to make the necessary cash transfers to the LDRRMF account in the TF.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant reclassified the account. The Municipal Accountant reconciled the report with the general ledger balance and prepared the necessary adjustments. The Municipality transferred the P2,000.00 to the TF. The Municipal Accountant prepared and submitted the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary correcting entries in the GF for the reverted DRRMF of CYs 2014 and 2015.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality prepared the necessary adjustments in the GF and transferred the necessary amount to the TF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MPDC to continue the collection of SDDs and establish a GAD database that will be the basis of the GAD programs to be implemented. Furthermore, require the GAD Focal Person to ensure that the Municipality continually adheres with the provisions of the JMC.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality continued the collection of SDDs. Efforts to compile the database are on-going.
observations.reason: Data collection not completed; budget consideration.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to: a. continue his efforts in gathering all relevant documents regarding the reconciliation of the aforesaid Inter-Agency Payables in order to establish the breakdown of the unidentified/unaccounted amounts and make the necessary adjustments for the errors in recording; b. remit the amounts due to the respective government agencies; and c. properly maintain the subsidiary ledgers of the Due to BIR, Due to GSIS, Due to Pag-IBIG and Due to PhilHealth accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant gathered the relevant documents regarding the reconciliation of the inter-agency payables. The Municipal Treasurer remitted the remaining identified/unaccounted balances. The Municipal Accountant maintained the subsidiary ledger for all accounts to establish the correct balances. The HRMO informed that permanent and temporary employees have already secured their government remittance numbers; some newly appointed employees' numbers are still to be secured.
observations.reason: Reconciliation of inter-agency payables is on-going; some remittances pending; government remittance numbers for some newly appointed employees yet to be secured.

observations.recommendation: We recommended, henceforth, that Management submit the required financial transaction documents on or before the 10th day of the ensuing month for prompt verification purposes pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-006. We also recommended that Municipal Accountant submit justification on the noted lapses.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality submitted the DVs within the prescribed period.
observations.reason: The management acted on this matter in this CY 2021 audit.

observations.recommendation: We recommended also that the Municipal Mayor, as the head of the agency, to impose administrative disciplinary action to the responsible officials every time the reports due to our office are not submitted within the required time.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer has submitted the Quarterly Report on Publicized Government Projects/Programs/Activities (PPAs).
observations.reason: The management acted on this matter in this CY 2021 audit.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) submit justification as to the non-compliance with GPPB Circular No. 01-2020 and to be always updated with the issuances of the GPPB as the governing board for all the procurement activities of the government.
observations.managementAction: In CY 2021, the Management submitted its justifications and the revised OSS of the supplier were already submitted.
observations.reason: Justifications from the Management were received together with the revised OSS of the supplier.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant review the Due to NGAs account and reclassify the completed projects to its appropriate PPE account so that this could be subjected to depreciation. We also recommended that management immediately implement the programs/projects funded by the National Government Agencies in order not to delay further the benefits expected from the implementation of those PPAs.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant transferred completed projects to its appropriate PPE account. The remaining balances in Due to NGAs account are those projects that are still on-going or not yet completed during the year.
observations.reason: Completed projects were transferred to PPE account and remaining balance of Due to NGAs account are those project that are not yet completed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit monthly Bank Reconciliation Statement in a timely manner, in compliance with the existing rules and regulations to properly substantiate the Cash in Bank balance reflected in the accounting records.
observations.managementAction: In CY 2021 audit, the Municipality already prepared and submitted their Bank Reconciliation Statement.
observations.reason: The management acted on this matter in this CY 2021 audit.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management take appropriate actions to cause for the immediate resolution of these deficiencies.  Henceforth, we advised management to enforce accounting controls so as to prevent the incurrence of dormant account balances in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant had started to make a subsidiary ledger of the assets and liabilities.  Also, the Municipal Accountant adjusted some of the erroneous entry made on this accounts.
observations.reason: Based on our validation, adjusting journal entries had been made to adjust the said accounts.  One of which is the Construction in Progress account that was adjusted per Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) No. 2021-06-006.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management direct the Municipal Accountant to compute and record the depreciation in the books of accounts in order to present fairly the value of the Property, Plant & Equipment and other affected accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: In CY 2021 audit, the Municipal Accountant already provided depreciation expense in their PPE account.
observations.reason: Depreciation expenses were already provided by the Municipality in the CY 2021 audit.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the officials or offices responsible to submit henceforth, copies of all perfected government contracts, purchase orders and all supporting documents for the procurement of goods & services within five (5) working days from execution/issuance pursuant with COA Circular No. 2009-001 to facilitate timely auditorial and technical review thereof.
observations.managementAction: In CY 2021 audit, the Municipality submitted POs, contracts and other related documents within prescribed period.
observations.reason: The Management acted on this matter in this CY 2021 audit.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the Journal Entry Voucher to close the Construction in Progress account representing the finished projects and record the same to the appropriate PPE accounts.  Further, we recommended that subsidiary records be maintained for proper monitoring of the account and ensure completeness and accuracy of the recorded transactions.
observations.managementAction: The Construction in Progress accounts were already transferred to its appropriate asset accounts. The Municipal Accountant will continue to review and monitor the Construction in Progress account.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant provided Adjusting journal entries on this matter under JEV No. 2021-06-006.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the OIC Municipal Accountant ensure that the noted deficiencies be submitted and with complete signatures to avoid disallowance in audit. Further, we recommended that the OIC Municipal Accountant ensure that the required supporting documents of all claims are complete before affixing his signature in Box A of the disbursement voucher.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant complied the lacking documents and incomplete signatures during the year.
observations.reason: Lacking documents and incomplete signatures were submitted by the Management in CY 2021 audit.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to conduct a physical count of the supplies, prepare and submit the RCPI semestrally to the audit team duly reconciled with the records of the Accounting Office. Moreover, the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant should prepare and maintain stock cards and subsidiary ledgers for each type of inventory, respectively, which should be reconciled on a regular basis.
observations.managementAction: The Management conducted a physical count of the assets and had submitted a report in CY 2021 Audit.
observations.reason: RCPI had been submitted by the Management in CY 2021 Audit.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to transfer the unexpended balance of the DRRM Fund in the Trust Liabilities-DRRMF account in the Trust Fund. Also, require the Municipal Accountant to include in the Notes to Financial Statement the DRRM Fund disclosure in accordance with COA Circular 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: Unexpended balance of LDRRM fund was transferred already.
observations.reason: The unexpended balance of DRRM Fund was already transferred in the Trust Liabilities – DRRMF account in CY 2021 Audit.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee submit explanation as to the non-submission of the APP and PMR to GPPB.
