Also, we recommended that the Budget Office and the BAC revisit their functions and coordinate with each other to facilitate the preparation of the APP and its inclusion in the Annual Budget. Henceforth, strictly follow the provisions in R.A. 9184 in the preparation and submission of the Annual Procurement Plan and the Procurement Monitoring Report.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality was able to submit the Annual Procurement Plan (APP) to GPPB.
observations.reason: The Management acted on this matter in this CY 2021 audit.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that henceforth the concerned municipal officials adhere strictly to the provisions of RA 7160 in the computation of PS limitation to avoid excess expenditures for Personal Services.
observations.managementAction: Currently, the Municipality no longer exceeds the 45% PS limitation.
observations.reason: The Management acted on this matter in this CY 2021 audit.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the construction materials totaling ₱1, 036,304.00 purchased for the improvement of the Auction Area of the Municipal Abattoir and Roof Deck of the Public Market be capitalized to fairly present the Property, Plant and Equipment account in the financial statements. The Municipal Accountant must be reminded that any improvements, alteration, replacements, major repairs, etc., when it extends the life, increase the capacity or improve the efficiency or safety of the property must be capitalized.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant already capitalized the said transactions.
observations.reason: In CY 2021 audit, the Management reclassified the said transactions in the appropriate PPE account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management send demand letters to the ineligible beneficiaries for the refund of the illegally received assistance totaling ₱1,140,000.00 and ensure that these illegally disbursed funds are collected and returned to DSWD.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality has issued a demand letter to the barangays for the return of the assistance for those ineligible beneficiaries.
observations.reason: Copies of demand letters were received in CY 2021 audit.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Accounting Office continue with the reconciliation of the Due to BIR balance to come up with the details of the overdue amount and ensure that these are fully remitted to the BIR. We further recommended that, henceforth a monthly reconciliation of remittances against amounts withheld be conducted to prevent the recurrence of unreconciled/unremitted balances.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has started reconciling the Due to BIR account and adjusted some erroneous entry made on this account.
observations.reason: Reconciliation of Due to BIR account is still on going in CY 2021 audit.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management direct the members of the local finance committee of the Municipality of San Francisco, Cebu, to refund honoraria they received as members of said committee for CY 2012.  Henceforth, the giving of honoraria to the local finance committee be discontinued.
observations.managementAction: Stopped the practice of giving honoraria to the local finance committee.
observations.reason: Paid honoraria were not yet refunded.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the local government officials concerned read and follow the legal guidelines that are created/issued for the implementation of the ESP thereby, enabling the measures adopted to respond to the crisis pandemic and become effective and efficient.
We recommended that the officials concerned read, follow the guidelines created for the proper selection, utilization and/or paying of the cash assistance of the program.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Budget Officer, Municipal Planning and Development Officer, and Municipal Engineer to fully utilize the 20% Development Fund thereby, optimizing the allocation for various Programs/Projects/Activities to achieve desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: 1. Compel the Municipal Accountant to review and identify the details of the Due from NGAs, Due from LGUs and Other Receivable accounts and to exert all efforts to identify the purpose of the fund transfers; and 2. Direct the Municipal Accountant to comply with the reportorial requirements on funds transferred to local or national government agencies or and other debtors in order to avoid accumulation of unliquidated balances of the accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Council prioritize the implementation of the CBMS for the creation of GAD database to serve as inputs or basis for planning, programming and policy formulation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor through the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board strictly implement feasible re-use, recycling, and composting programs and other prescribed procedures of RA No. 9003. Also, implement the projects, programs and activities listed in the AIP. We also recommended that the Mayor provide the necessary financial and technical support in enjoining the barangays to establish their own Materials Recovery Facility.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: 1. Compel the Municipal Accountant to review and identify the details of the Due from NGAs, Due from LGUs and Other Receivable accounts and to exert all efforts to identify the purpose of the fund transfers; and 2. Direct the Municipal Accountant to comply with the reportorial requirements on funds transferred to local or national government agencies or and other debtors in order to avoid accumulation of unliquidated balances of the accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the officials concerned to discontinue the subscription with the current internet services provider and consider other available/ responsive provider that can deliver better connectivity for the key offices of the LGU.

We recommended Municipal Accountant to review and identify details and purpose of fund transfers; comply with reportorial requirements on funds transferred to avoid accumulation of unliquidated balances.
We recommended prioritize CBMS implementation for creation of GAD database.
We recommended strict implementation of reuse, recycling and composting; implement AIP projects; provide support to barangays to establish MRFs.
We recommended creation of Inventory Committee and instruct them to conduct physical inventory and prepare RPCPPE.

We recommended Municipal Mayor direct Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the corresponding disbursement vouchers together with the related supporting documents pertaining to the one hundred seventy-three (173) checks totaling P4,144,389.63. Henceforth ensure reports submitted are complete and intact.
We recommended Municipal Mayor discontinue use of open dump sites and enact ordinances against open burning and littering.

We recommended intensify collection efforts including auction, levy and distraint of personal property; tax information campaign; publication of delinquent properties and sending billing notices to defaulting taxpayers.
No recommendation required as no ODA funds were received.
In view of this, we recommended that management assess and review the contract entered into by the Municipality and consider revision pursuant to the authority by the Sangguniang Bayan.
observations.reason: Not complied by Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the OIC Municipal Accountant ensure that the above-mentioned deficiencies be submitted and with complete signatures to avoid disallowance in audit. Further, we recommended that the OIC Municipal Accountant ensure that the required supporting documents of all claims are complete before affixing his signature in Box A of the disbursement voucher.
observations.reason: Not complied by Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Bids and Awards Committee submit the required Sworn Affidavit that it is not related to the supplier concerned to prevent disallowance of the transaction in audit. Henceforth, ensure compliance with RA 9184 by requiring all bidders to disclose their relations with the members of the HOPE, the BAC, the TWG, the BAC Secretariat, the head of the PMO or the end user or implementing unit.
observations.reason: Not complied by Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit the memorandum of agreement or any document to support the payment of SSS premium of the farmers and fishermen pursuant to Section 4(6) of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: No submission made.
observations.reason: Not complied by Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee submit justification and proof that the supplier is qualified to sell fishing and agricultural supplies and equipment; and the required Post-Qualification Evaluation Report to avoid disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: No justification made and the Post-Qualification Evaluation Report was not submitted to the audit team.
observations.reason: Not complied by Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management establish an Internal Audit Service Office with sufficient support from the top management to gain the cooperation/confidence of the auditee in compliance with AO No. 70, NGICS and Section 124 of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: IASO still not created.
observations.reason: Not complied by Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the GAD Focal Person submit the GAD Accomplishment Report, duly endorsed by the DILG, to the Audit Team for proper evaluation of the GAD Plan and Budget execution.
observations.managementAction: No submission made.
observations.reason: Not complied by Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the officials or offices responsible to submit henceforth, copies of all perfected government contracts, purchase orders and all supporting documents for the procurement of goods & services within five (5) working days from execution/issuance pursuant with COA Circular No. 2009-001 to facilitate timely auditorial and technical review thereof.
observations.managementAction: No submission made.
observations.reason: Not complied by Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Planning & Development Office and the Municipality’s GFPS to lead or spearhead the setting up and maintenance of a GAD Database to serve as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation in compliance with the undated PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01.
observations.managementAction: GAD Database still not established.
observations.reason: Not complied by Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the Journal Entry Voucher to close the Construction in Progress account representing the finished projects and record the same to the appropriate PPE accounts.  Further, we recommended that subsidiary records be maintained for proper monitoring of the account and ensure completeness and accuracy of the recorded transactions.
observations.managementAction: Still not transferred to appropriate PPE account.
observations.reason: Not complied by Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant provide depreciation for the affected PPE accounts in the current and prior years to reflect the correct balances of the accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Still no depreciation provided.
observations.reason: Not complied by Management. Reiterated in CY 2020 AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor create/instruct the Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical count of the PPE and reconcile the same with the records of the Accounting Office and submit the RPCPPE to the audit team within the reglementary period.  Further, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer maintain the required subsidiary ledgers for each type of PPE and property cards, respectively, and update the PAR every three years or every time there is a change in custodianship / user of the property.
observations.managementAction: Still unreconciled.
observations.reason: Not complied by Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to conduct a physical count of the supplies, prepare and submit the RCPI semestrally to the audit team duly reconciled with the records of the Accounting Office. Moreover, the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant should prepare and maintain stock cards and subsidiary ledgers for each type of inventory, respectively, which should be reconciled on a regular basis.
observations.managementAction: Count still not conducted and RCPI still not prepared and submitted to the audit team.
observations.reason: Not complied by Management.

observations.recommendation: We reiterate our recommendation that management instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare a reconciliation report of the reciprocal accounts and  take up the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts to ensure that all reconciling items are resolved and/or adjusted on a timely basis and perform constant review and appraisal of transactions to deter error and omissions.
observations.managementAction: Still not reconciled.
observations.reason: Not complied by Management. Reiterated in CY 2020 AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the bank reconciliation statement to present fairly the balances of the cash in bank account in the financial statements. Henceforth, the said Municipal Accountant should always see to it that the DVs covering claims against government funds are supported with complete documents pursuant to Section 4 of P. Direct the BAC to review Purchase Requests thoroughly to ascertain compliance of Section 18 of IRR of RA 9184.

Adopt public bidding as the general mode of procurement and resort to alternative method of procurement only in the highly exceptional cases provided for in the IRR of R.A. 9184.

Direct the Municipal Accountant to submit the aforecited documents as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

Also, refrain from using account title not prescribed in the Chart of Account in order not to give misleading information to the stakeholders.

Also require the submission of the proof of ownership of the firearms and the certification on the peace and order situation of the municipality to support that the issuance of the firearms and procurement of ammunition is necessary.

Cause the return of the remaining balance of the fund from the Presidential Social Fund by depositing the same to the Bureau of treasury.
Prepare the necessary entry to transfer the account to DUE FROM NGOs/POs for the funds entrusted to Non-Government Organizations/Peoples’ organization for the implementation of specific government project subject to liquidation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following courses of action to rectify the foregoing deficiencies:
The Inventory Committee be directed to segregate  the unserviceable properties and equipment from those properties which are still serviceable but no longer needed by the agency and enter the same  in the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) pursuant to Section 59, Volume I of LGU NGAS, Manual;
The Municipal Accountant should record as debit to Other Assets (251) and credit to the appropriate PPE accounts the value of obsolete and unserviceable assets including property and equipment no longer used in the agency operation but still serviceable pursuant to Section 140 of LGU NGAS Manual, Volume 3; and
The Municipal Treasurer should apply for the disposal of said unserviceable properties for approval by the Municipal Mayor and Sangguniang Bayan and forward the duly approved IIRUP to the Auditor for his appropriate action pursuant to Section 379 of RA 7160.
observations.managementAction: No action from Management.
observations.reason: Not complied by Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to conduct a thorough verification of the dormant accounts recorded in the books and take necessary action to resolve the issue.
observations.managementAction: Still unreconciled.
observations.reason: Not complied by Management. Reiterated in CY 2020 AAR.

observations.recommendation:
Required the Municipal Accountant to thoroughly analyze long overdue reconciling items appearing in the Bank Reconciliation Statements of General, Special Education and Trust Fund and take up in the books those valid items through the issuance of Journal Entry Voucher duly supported by the appropriate documents. 
Further, the Municipal Accountant should request the concerned depository bank to adjust their books of the unrecorded transfer of Bank Account No. 014C-1130-021668 of ₱31, 832.00 and the overstatement in recording the checks No. 18311285 dated May 27, 2009 and Check No. 1831442 dated June 24, 2009 in the amount of ₱500.00 and ₱272.01, respectively.  
Moreover, require the concerned Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to adhere strictly to the provisions of Section 59 of LGU NGAS Manual, Volume I.  Likewise, the Municipal Accountant should make the necessary Journal Entries on the stale checks in accordance with Section 61 of same manual.
observations.managementAction: Still to be verified by the Municipal Accountant.
observations.reason: Not complied by Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended management to stop immediately paying employer’s share to the Fund in excess of what is required by law and instead comply strictly with the above regulations effective immediately to avoid disallowance in audit.   Also, coordinate with Pag-ibig on the possible application of the over remittance of employer’s (government) share for succeeding dues on monthly contribution, otherwise, the excessive remittance shall be disallowed in audit pursuant to Section 103 of P.D. 1445.
observations.managementAction: None
observations.reason: Not complied by Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the construction materials totaling ₱1, 036,304.00 purchased for the improvement of the Auction Area of the Municipal Abattoir and Roof Deck of the Public Market be capitalized to fairly present the Property, Plant and Equipment account in the financial statements. The Municipal Accountant must be reminded that any improvements, alteration, replacements, major repairs, etc., when it extends the life, increase the capacity or improve the efficiency or safety of the property must be capitalized.
observations.managementAction: None
observations.reason: Not complied by Management.

observations.recommendation: Refund the amount and refrain from disbursing honoraria not in accordance with Budget Circular No. 2004-5 dated March 23, 2004.
observations.managementAction: None
observations.reason: Not complied by Management.

observations.recommendation:
The Municipal officials concerned should refund immediately the disallowed honorarium amounting to ₱106, 052.50.
Appropriate sanctions must be filed against the officials who deliberately collected the honorarium and those who authorized the collection thereof despite their knowledge of its irregularity.
observations.managementAction: None
observations.reason: Not complied by Management.

observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor, together with the key personnel of the LGU like the Municipal Accountant and the MPDC, were directed to convene with the SMWD officers together with the respective resident Auditors.  Clarify the intention of both parties as regards the transfer of the waterworks/sewerage facilities, including the water rights of the LGU’s Water System, to the SMWD in exchange of the latter’s payment of P5,000,000.00.  Amend the provisions in the project study and the MOA that are found conflicting with the established intentions of the parties.  Ensure that the municipality and the water district should have a uniform interpretation of the provision/stipulation stated in the project study and MOA.  Adjust the PPE account of the LGU based on the established/clarified intentions of the parties and in accordance with the accounting standard set in IPSAS No. 1.
observations.reason: Not Implemented

observations.managementAction: The MENRO was directed to formulate and comply the regular collection of non-recyclable and special wastes in the barangays as mandated in Section 10 of R.A. No. 9003; The MENRO also agreed to include in their budget an amount for the procurement of protective equipment such as, but not limited to gloves, masks and boots to be used by the garbage collectors and to follow-up the purchase of the vehicle with the prescribed features for the collection and transportation of wastes  as prescribed in Sections 23 and 24 of the Act; The Mayor agreed, with authority from the Sangguniang Bayan, to enter into a Memorandum of Agreement with the Provincial Government of Surigao del Norte to accept their proposal to utilize the vacant lot they offered as temporary dumping site; The MENRO agreed to exert more effort in locating possible sanitary landfill sites and  to expedite the establishment of the same; and faithfully adhere to the provisions of R.A. No. 9003 particularly Sections 10, 23, 24, 37, 40 and 41 thereof on the collection and disposal of solid waste materials.
observations.reason: Not Implemented

observations.managementAction: We recommended and the property custodian agreed to initiate the construction or maintenance of a secured shelter or room where to properly store the unserviceable property and waste materials awaiting the disposal thereof; The Inventory and Disposal Committee agreed, through its Team Leader, and with the assistance of the property custodian, to spearhead the planning of the disposal procedures that the LGU will adopt in the divestment of its unserviceable property and waste materials in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 89-296 dated January 27, 1989.  Implement the divestment procedures and prepare the necessary documents for such disposal as required in the said COA Circular; Remind the Inventory and Disposal Committee and/or the property custodian that the appraisal of property can be done by the municipal engineer or by the provincial appraiser from the provincial government if the latter cannot perform the job; and strictly observe Sections 2 and 79 of P.D. No. 1445 in the utilization and disposal of LGU property.
observations.reason: Not Implemented

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer in
coordination with the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer (MSWDO)
to submit to the Audit Team the SACs, IDs, photographs of the recipients during the
actual payout, and other supporting documents of the aforementioned 22 SAP
recipients.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and
the Municipal Treasurer to have their records updated and reconciled and
subsequently make the necessary adjustments for the discrepancies in their
respective records.

Furthermore, require the Municipal Accountant to:

a. continue exerting efforts in retrieving relevant documents to reconcile the
unaccounted amounts in the BRSs and the difference between the GL and SL
totals of the CIB accounts in the TF; and

b. make the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts relative to the
unrecorded reconciling items in the BRSs and errors in disbursements of CY
2020.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Treasurer to require the MCs to remit their collections daily.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Accountant to retrieve relevant documents relative to the recordings made on the
respective accounts and reconcile the accounts. Prepare the necessary
adjustments where appropriate.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to
ensure that all cash advances are liquidated at the end of each year. Furthermore,
instruct the concerned officials/employees/accountable officer to immediately
liquidate the remaining cash advances. Otherwise, implement the withholding of
their respective salaries.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant
to:

a. continue exerting effort in locating relevant documents that will support the
reconciliation and the determination of complete details and breakdown of the
accounts’ balances;

b. make the necessary adjustments pertaining to errors in the respective
accounts; and

c. henceforth, ensure that all transactions are properly recorded.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to
provide the details or breakdown of the unidentified/unaccounted Due from Officers
and Employees account of P997,775.71. We also recommended to adjust/refund to
employees the over-deduction of loan payments in salary payrolls after verification
of supporting documents.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:

a. Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Assessor to exert effort in providing
the breakdown or details of the recorded land account amounting to
P2,091,433.00 or reconcile the same with the unrecorded lots owned by the
Municipality;

b. Municipal Treasurer/Municipal Assessor in locating documents to establish the
amount and mode of acquisition of the unrecorded lots owned by the
Municipality or prioritize the creation of the Appraisal Committee for the
valuation of the unrecorded lands, titled and untitled, which will be used as the
basis of the Municipal Accountant in recording the unrecorded parcels of
land/adjusting the Land account balance; and

c. Municipal Assessor to facilitate the titling of all untitled lots owned by the
Municipality and ensure that all titles are in the custody and safekeeping of the
Municipal Treasurer.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Inventory Committee to
conduct/complete the physical count of PPE and submit to the Audit Team copies
of complete RPCPPE and RPCLRN in forms prescribed in the existing rules and
regulations and ensure that all PPE owned/controlled by the Municipality are
included in the physical count and presented per class.

Moreover, require the Inventory Committee and the Municipal Accountant to
reconcile the results of the physical count with the accounting records and make
the necessary adjustments, if any.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to
coordinate with the Municipal Engineer in providing the details or breakdown of the
unidentified CIP amounting to P513,625.01 and the supporting documents of the
non-moving CIP amounting to P4,042,700.44 of CY 2009. Subsequently, make the
appropriate adjustments in the books of accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to
continue his effort in retrieving all relevant documents such as SORDs for all funds
received from the National Government to establish the details or breakdown of the
aforementioned unidentified balances and make necessary adjustments, if any.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to
continue gathering all relevant documents regarding the reconciliation of the Due
to LGUs account and make the necessary adjustments; and reclassify the DPWH’s
share in building permit fees from Due to LGUs account to Due to NGAs account.
Consequently, require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to
remit the amount due to the respective LGUs/agency.

Henceforth, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant properly maintain the
subsidiary ledgers of the Due to LGUs account pertaining to the Provincial
Government and Barangays of the Municipality.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Accountant to retrieve relevant documents regarding the reconciliation of the
abovementioned accounts and make the necessary adjustments thereof.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Accountant continue
their efforts in retrieving the unsubmitted DVs in CY 2017 and oblige the former
Municipal Accountant to comply with its submission to the Audit Team for
verification and post audit. We also recommended to consider appropriate legal
action, if warranted.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Treasurer to submit to the Audit Team the Monthly Report of Official Travels and
Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption for each motor vehicle duly supported with
Driver’s Trip Tickets issued during the month for review and evaluation. Henceforth,
see to it that the Driver’s Trip Tickets were properly accomplished.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Bids and
Awards Committee Secretariat to submit to the Audit Team the copies of the
contracts and purchase orders as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2009-001 together
with its supporting documents.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Engineer/Bids and Awards Committee Chairperson to:

a. immediately submit to the SA and the ATL the list of all on-going PPAs of the
Municipality and those that are to be implemented in CY 2021. Henceforth, to
submit the same reports at the beginning of the succeeding years;

b. inform the Audit Team within ten days after the award of PPAs or before its start
that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices were already
posted for validation; and

c. regularly submit the monthly monitoring report.

1.14 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a. Reconcile the balance per books with the balance per bank for the Cash in
Bank – Local Currency, Current Account, trace the details of the
differences, and record the necessary adjusting entries;
b. Request the Municipal Mayor to make representation with depository
banks for the timely release of bank statements;
c. Prepare the monthly BRS regularly and submit the same to the Audit
Team; and
d. Prepare the GLs and SLs for the Cash in Bank – Local Currency, Current
Account containing complete significant information and history of the
transactions related to the account.

2.14 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
1. Municipal Accountant and Property Custodian to maintain subsidiary
ledger cards and property cards, respectively, and reconcile periodically;
2. Inventory Committee to conduct periodic physical count of PPEs and
prepare RPCPPE with complete data for each item, as basis for
reconciliation with the SLCs and PCs. Also, furnish the Audit Team a copy
of the duly accomplished RPCPPE not later than January 31 of each year in
accordance with the GAM for LGUs; and
3. Municipal Accountant to secure the documents that will support the
balances of the PPEs and provide annual depreciation as prescribed in the
GAM for LGUs to present fairly the financial statements.

3.11 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
1. Municipal Accountant and the Property Custodian to maintain supplies
ledger cards and stock cards, respectively, and reconcile periodically; and
2. Inventory Committee to conduct periodic physical count of Inventories and
prepare RPCI with complete data for each item, as basis for reconciliation
with the SLCs and SCs. Also, furnish the Audit Team a copy of the duly
accomplished RPCI not later than January 31 of each year in accordance
with the GAM for LGUs.

4.15 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant
to:
a. Prepare and certify a monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of
LDRRMF;
b. Transfer immediately the cash corresponding to the unexpended balance
as of year-end of LDRRMF for QRF and MOOE from General Fund to
Trust Fund account;
c. Verify the variances between the Report on the Utilization of DRRMF and
the amount presented in the Notes to Financial Statements and to make the
necessary corrections in the balances; and
d. Provide adequate disclosure in the Notes to FS for amount and details of
the unexpended balance of LDRRMF.

5.21 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Require the Members of the Sangguniang Bayan to enact an ordinance for
the augmentation/realignment of funds and a supplemental budget for any
changes in the Annual Budget to cover new PPAs from realigned funds or
available funds from new revenue source as certified by the Municipal
Treasurer;
b. Require the Municipal Accountant to ensure completeness of
documentation attached to all disbursements, and submit the lacking
supporting documents;
c. Require BAC to be updated on the related issuances of GPPB particularly
on the posting requirements for procurements; and
d. Require the GSO in charge and the Inspection Officer to complete the
details of the AIR to effect a correct system on delivered goods vis-a-vis
with the contract/PO.

6.13 We recommended that local government officials concerned to read and follow
guidelines that are created/issued for the implementation of the ESP thereby,
enabling the measures adopted to respond to the crisis brought by the COVID-
19 pandemic and become effective and efficient.

7.11 We recommended that the officials concerned to read and follow the guidelines
created for the proper implementation of the selection, utilization and/or paying
of the cash assistance of the program.

8.17 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
1. Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to strictly comply with the posting and
submission requirements of the GPPB to provide full information and
transparency to the public;
2. Sangguniang Bayan to henceforth pass an ordinance on the creation of a
special account in the general fund (SAGF) for the BGCM to facilitate the
monitoring of the fund’s utilization;
3. Supply and/or Property Officer and Inspecting Officer to fill out the date and
other necessary details in the Purchase Order and Acceptance and
Inspection Report to assure that the items received are in accordance with
the contract; and
4. Municipal Accountant to ensure completeness of supporting documents for
all disbursement so as to ascertain the propriety of the transactions; submit
the disbursement voucher for the procured relief goods; and submit all the
lacking supporting documents in disbursement vouchers.

9.8 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Budget
Officer, Municipal Planning and Development Officer, and Municipal
Engineer to fully utilize the 20% Development Fund thereby, optimizing the
allocation for various Programs/Projects/Activities to achieve desirable socio-
economic development and environmental outcomes.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) comply with the mandatory posting of the updated APP, NOA, and other information relative to the Emergency Procurement under the Bayanihan Act in the GPPB Online Portal as mandated in GPPB Circular No. 1-2020 and should always be updated with the issuances and advisories of the GPPB by visiting their website regularly to avoid non-compliance in the future.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) submit justification as to the non-compliance with GPPB Circular No. 01-2020 and to be always updated with the issuances of the GPPB as the governing board for all the procurement activities of the government.

We recommended that Management send demand letters to the ineligible beneficiaries for the refund of the illegally received assistance totaling ₱1,140,000.00 and ensure that these illegally disbursed funds are collected and returned to DSWD.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit monthly Bank Reconciliation Statement in a timely manner, in compliance with the existing rules and regulations to properly substantiate the Cash in Bank balance reflected in the accounting records.

We recommended, henceforth, that Management submit the required financial transaction documents on or before the 10th day of the ensuing month for prompt verification purposes pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-006. We also recommended that Municipal Accountant submit justification on the noted lapses.

Since we are required to submit the PPA report to the Office of the Regional Director, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Office of the Municipal Engineer for the timely submission of the Quarterly Report on PPAs on or before the 10th day of the month following the end of each quarter.
We recommended also that the Municipal Mayor, as the head of the agency, to impose administrative disciplinary action to the responsible officials every time the reports due to our office are not submitted within the required time.

We recommended that the concerned municipal official be directed to refund the amount of honoraria received to avoid disallowance in audit.
We also recommended that, henceforth, strict compliance with the provisions of Section 95 of RA 7160 and COA Circular No. 2013-003 and other pertinent laws is enjoined to avoid personal liability.

We recommended that the management direct the Municipal Accountant to compute and record the depreciation in the books of accounts in order to present fairly the value of the Property, Plant & Equipment and other affected accounts in the financial statements.  The following entry is proposed:

We recommended that the management take appropriate actions to cause for the immediate resolution of these deficiencies.  Henceforth, we advised management to enforce accounting controls so as to prevent the incurrence of dormant account balances in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant review the Due to NGAs account and reclassify the completed projects to its appropriate PPE account so that this could be subjected to depreciation. We also recommended that management immediately implement the programs/projects funded by the National Government Agencies in order not to delay further the benefits expected from the implementation of those PPAs.

We recommended that management direct the Municipal Accountant to review and verify these reciprocal accounts from the source documents and effect the appropriate adjustments to correct & reconcile these accounts.

We recommended that the Accounting Office continue with the reconciliation of the Due to BIR balance to come up with the details of the overdue amount and ensure that these are fully remitted to the BIR. We further recommended that, henceforth a monthly reconciliation of remittances against amounts withheld be conducted to prevent the recurrence of unreconciled/unremitted balances.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to provide disclosures on the face of the SCBAA and/or in the Notes to the Financial Statements the a) changes between the original and final budget; b) material discrepancies between the budget and actual amounts; and c) reconciliation of actual amounts per SCBAA and per financial statements, to enhance the transparency of the Municipality’s financial statements.

We recommended that the Management:
Issue a demand letter to all accountable officers who are not already connected with the LGU for the immediate liquidation/ settlement of their unliquidated cash advances and in case of non-compliance, find any legal remedies to compel the liquidation/settlement of cash advances; and
Make necessary actions for the proper reporting of dormant accounts by submitting request for write-off and its underlying supporting documents to prove that the accounts are no longer collectible.

We recommended that the Management instruct the Inventory Committee to conduct actual count of all its inventories as at year-end, to present a reliable and accurate Inventory accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Management instruct the Inventory Committee to conduct a physical inventory of all the local road networks and reconcile the local road inventory with the records of the accounting office and strictly follow the provisions of COA Circular 2015-008.

We recommended that Management stop charging ineligible expenses to 5% LDRRMF, allocate additional funds for regular operation expenses of the LDRRM and EOC Office, and follow strictly the regulation in the utilization of the 5% Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund as provided under Section 5.1 of the Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 of DBM and DILG dated March 25, 2013.

We recommended that Management submit the required supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor immediately. Henceforth, all disbursement should be supported with complete documentation to establish the validity and regularity of transactions.
We recommended to the Management to stop the practice of procuring goods to non-eligible suppliers pursuant to Section 53 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184 in the negotiated procurement of goods.

We recommended that Management to direct the Municipal Engineering Office and the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC), through its secretariat, to submit all contracts, together with all the supporting documents forming part thereof, within 5 days from its perfection to the Auditor for auditorial review and technical evaluation and to ascertain the regularity of the transactions as required in COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to withhold the amount of P1,070,646.88 during the payment of the contract and instruct the Municipal Engineer to strictly adhere to the guidelines in the preparation of the Approved Budget of the Contract set forth in DPWH Department Order No. 197 series of 2016.

We recommended that the management, through the Municipal Local School Board to maximize the utilization of the Special Education Fund by full implementation of all planned programs and projects in a year in order to attain the objectives for which the fund was intended and to provide benefits for the students.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive conduct regular monitoring of the implementation of GAD Plan and Budget to ensure the full utilization of the fund. Also, submit the Annual GAD Accomplishment Report and its supporting documents within 5 days from end of January of the preceding year.

We recommended that the Management, through the Municipal Peace and Order Council, to maximize the utilization of the POPs Fund by full implementation of all planned programs and projects in a year in order to attain the objectives for which the fund was intended.

We recommended that Management instruct the implementing office to fast track the implementation of the programs/projects so that targeted beneficiaries can enjoy the intended benefits may derived therefrom.
We recommended that the Management provide updated property numbers for these equipment.
We recommended that the Management issue proper documents upon issuance of the PPE or semi-expandable items and keep a file for monitoring.

We recommended that the Management direct the concerned accountable officers to accomplish the necessary report/s and/or procedure in property disposal as provided in the Manual on Property and Supply Management and use the reports (IIRUP) as the basis of the de-recognition of the property from the inventory.

We recommended that the Management submit the Report on Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment for CY 2020 and adhere to the prescribed date of its submission for CY 2021 and succeeding years.

We recommended that the Management strengthen the GAD Focal Point System of the LGU by (1) providing enough proficient personnel to support the Focal Person; (2) require the Focal Person together with her team to: (a) develop and maintain GAD database containing gender statistics and sex-disaggregated data, gathered systematically, among the LGU population and clients; (b) regularly update these data; (c) conduct gender analysis on these data for planning, programming and policy formulation; (d) implement the identified PPAs; and (e) closely monitor the progress of activities to meet the objectives and realize the performance indicators and targets; (3) ensure that the gender issues are clearly stated in the GAD Plan and design and formulate its PPAs according to the specific identified gender issues to address and resolve the same, supported with valid analyses.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to remit immediately to the BIR the withheld taxes balance as of
December 31, 2020 in the amount of ₱590,583.32 to avoid incurring penalties and surcharges; and Adhere strictly to Section 4.114.C of Revenue Regulation (RR) No. 2-98 in the withholding and remittance of taxes to the BIR.
We recommended and the Management agreed to submit to COA the documents required in the subject Notices of Suspension and to settle the amount stated in the Notices of Disallowance.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to have their records reconciled and make the necessary adjustments for the discrepancies. Furthermore, require the Municipal Accountant to: a. exert efforts in retrieving relevant documents in preparing some of the BRS; b. submit to the Audit Team copies of the BRS prepared; and c. make the necessary adjusting entries pertaining to the errors identified in the post audit of CY 2019 transactions.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant is still retrieving/locating related documents needed to reconcile their records.
See Observation No. 2, Part II of this AAR.
The Municipal Accountant was able to prepare and submit copies of the BRSs prepared in CY 2020.
The adjusting entries pertaining to the errors identified in the post audit of CY 2019 transactions were already made in CY 2020.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant is still retrieving/locating related documents needed to reconcile their records.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to retrieve relevant documents relative to the recordings made on the respective accounts and reconcile the accounts. Prepare the necessary adjustments where appropriate.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant was able to retrieve some of the relevant documents needed in the preparation of the reconciliation of the RPT/SET receivables and payables accounts.
Adjustments to correct the set-up of RPT/SET receivables and payables accounts of CY 2019 was already made in CY 2020.
observations.reason: Adjustments to correct the set-up of RPT/SET receivables and payables accounts of CY 2019 was already made as relevant documents were not yet retrieved/located in CY 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to retrieve relevant documents regarding the reconciliation of the abovementioned accounts and make the necessary adjustments thereof.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant committed to continue the retrieval of relevant documents and reconciliation of the reciprocal accounts.
observations.reason: The reconciliation of the abovementioned reciprocal accounts is on-going as well as the retrieval and review of supporting documents.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all cash advances are liquidated at the end of each year. Furthermore, instruct the concerned officials/employees/accountable officer to immediately liquidate the remaining cash advances. Otherwise, implement the withholding of their respective salaries.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to: a. continue exerting effort in locating relevant documents that will support the reconciliation and the determination of complete details and breakdown of the accounts’ balances; b. make the necessary adjustments pertaining to errors in the respective accounts; and c. henceforth, ensure that all transactions are properly recorded.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant was able to retrieve some of the relevant documents and was able to provide schedules of the aforementioned receivables. He committed to continue retrieval of relevant documents and to make necessary adjustments where appropriate.
See Observation No. 5, Part II of this AAR.
observations.reason: There are still cash advances that were not liquidated in CY 2020 due to non-submission of liquidation reports to the Accounting Office and under-liquidation of some cash advances. The Municipal Accountant still has to locate other relevant documents and to inform the concerned officials/employees/accountable officers of their cash advance balances prior to withholding of their salaries.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to provide the details or breakdown of the account to ensure its accuracy and validity. We also recommended to adjust/refund to employees the over-deduction of loan payments in salary payrolls after verification of supporting documents.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant was able to identify some amounts in the Due from Officers and Employees account amounting to P98,641.61 which pertains to salary loan balances of employees. He committed to continue retrieval of supporting documents and to make adjustments/refunds after verification.
observations.reason: As of year-end, Due to Officers and Employees of P997,775.71 with no details or breakdown remain. Over deductions of loan payments were included in the account balance as of year-end which affected the accuracy and validity of the said account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the: a. Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Assessor to exert effort in providing the breakdown or details of the recorded land account amounting to P2,304,909.00 or reconcile the same with the unrecorded lots owned by the Municipality; b. Municipal Treasurer/Municipal Assessor in locating documents to establish the amount and mode of acquisition of the unrecorded lots owned by the Municipality or prioritize the creation of the Appraisal Committee for the valuation of the unrecorded lands, titled and untitled, which will be used as the basis of the Municipal Accountant in recording the unrecorded parcels of land/adjusting the Land account balance; and c. Municipal Assessor to facilitate the titling of all untitled lots owned by the Municipality and ensure that all titles are in the custody and safekeeping of the Municipal Treasurer.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Assessor identified ten parcels of land with acquisition costs totaling P11,013,476.00 which reduced the balance of the land account without details. He committed to continue the titling of the remaining untitled lots and will request for its budgetary requirements.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Assessor still need to locate relevant documents to provide the breakdown or details of the recorded land account and to establish the amount and mode of acquisition of the unrecorded lots. Titling of some untitled lots is on-going.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Inventory Committee to: a. ensure that all PPE owned/controlled by the Municipality are included in the physical count; b. henceforth prepare the RPCPPE presented per class; and c. submit to the Audit Team copies of the complete RPCPPE and RPCLRN in the forms prescribed in the existing rules and regulations. Moreover, require the Inventory Committee and the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the results of the physical count with the accounting records and make the necessary adjustments, if any.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Assessor as the head of the Inventory Committee agreed to conduct/complete the physical count, reconcile the results with the accounting records and make necessary adjustments, if necessary.
observations.reason: The Municipality was not able to conduct/complete the physical count in CY 2020; hence the RPCPPE and RPCLRN were not prepared and not submitted to the Audit Team. Reconciliation with accounting records was not made due to non-conduct of physical count.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Municipal Engineer in providing the details or breakdown of the unidentified CIP amounting to P513,625.01 and the supporting documents of the non-moving CIP amounting to P4,042,700.44 of CY 2009. Subsequently, make the appropriate adjustments in the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant was able to adjust some of the CIP accounts in CY 2020 and provided its schedule. He committed to coordinate with the Municipal Engineer and retrieve supporting documents for the non-moving CIP amounts.
observations.reason: CIP amounting to P513,625.01 was still not provided with details or breakdown. CIP totaling P4,042,700.44 remain non-moving or unadjusted since CY 2009 due to lack of supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to: a. reconcile the loans balances in the general ledger and the statement of accounts provided by EWBC and make the necessary adjustments thereof; and b. henceforth, ensure that all transactions are properly recorded.
observations.managementAction: Some of the loans balances in the general ledger were reconciled with the statement of accounts provided by EWBC and adjustments were made. Loan transactions were already properly recorded.
observations.reason: The loans’ balances in the general ledger were reconciled with the statement of accounts provided by EWBC and adjustments were made. Proper recording of funds received from NGAs was still being established for some items.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to exert effort in retrieving all relevant documents such as Statements of Receipts and Disbursements (SORD) for all funds received from the National Government to establish the details/breakdown of the account. Consequently, require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to remit the amount due to the respective LGUs/agency. Henceforth, ensure the proper recording of funds received from the National Government in the Due to NGAs account to monitor utilization and liquidation with the source agencies.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant committed to continue retrieval of SORDs and relevant documents and to reconcile and adjust the Due to NGAs where necessary.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant is still locating all the SORDs and other relevant documents needed to establish the correct breakdown of some of the funds, to complete the reconciliation and adjustment of the Due to NGAs account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to continue gathering all relevant documents regarding the reconciliation of the Due to LGUs account and make the necessary adjustments; and reclassify the DPWH’s share in building permit fees from Due to LGUs account to Due to NGAs account. Consequently, require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to remit the amount due to the respective LGUs/agency. Henceforth, properly maintain the subsidiary ledgers of the Due to LGUs account pertaining to the Provincial Government and Barangays of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant committed to continue gathering relevant documents and to properly maintain subsidiary ledgers and to make necessary adjustments. Some remittances were still pending.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant is still locating relevant supporting documents for the reconciliation of the Due to LGUs account; remittance of some amounts due to the Provincial Government and the Barangays were not yet made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustment thereof and henceforth ensure that all transactions are properly recorded.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant made the necessary adjustments and correcting entries pertaining to the errors in recording of prior years’ interest expenses and ensured subsequent proper recording.
observations.reason: Adjustments were already made by the Municipal Accountant and interest transactions are now properly recorded.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to: a. make the necessary adjustments pertaining to the errors in recording of prior year’s expenses and henceforth, ensure that all transactions are properly recorded; b. coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer in locating the missing checks identified in the sequence of checks issued and submit to the Audit Team a report supported with the necessary documents indicating its status whether the said checks were cancelled, unrecorded or others; c. propose internal controls, subject to the approval of the Municipal Mayor, that will improve document flow and organization of disbursements of the Municipality to produce complete, accurate and reliable financial information; and d. exert efforts in retrieving the un-submitted DVs in CY 2017 and submit the same to the Audit Team for verification and post audit.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant made adjustments to correct prior year errors and the Municipal Treasurer located some of the missing checks and the Municipality improved the document flow and collation of DVs. The Municipal Mayor and Municipal Accountant requested a meeting with the former Municipal Accountant to assist in retrieval of the un-submitted DVs of CY 2017, but the former could not attend due to COVID-19 restrictions.
observations.reason: Despite efforts, the retrieval and submission of the un-submitted DVs of CY 2017 were not completed in CY 2020 as the former Municipal Accountant was not able to attend the meeting arranged for retrieval due to COVID-19 health protocols.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to require the MCs to remit their collections daily and ensure that the RCDs are sequentially numbered to conform with the prescribed form.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer endorsed a letter to the Municipal Mayor requesting issuance of a memorandum of compliance to the MCs involved in the observation. The RCDs were sequentially numbered in CY 2020.
observations.reason: The practice of accumulating collections for two or more days before remittance to the Municipal Treasurer was still observed in CY 2020 despite the sequential numbering of RCDs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC Chairperson to ensure that all concerned officials or end-users in the procurement shall state the details, manner and schedule of their respective PPAs’ procurement plan in their respective PPMPs and submit it to the BAC to facilitate consolidation and preparation of the APP.
observations.managementAction: The BAC Secretariat reported that copies of the Contracts were submitted to the Audit Team and that they are accumulating the necessary documents and assured submission of all documents necessary for contract review and evaluation.
observations.reason: While copies of Contracts were submitted to the Audit Team in CY 2020, the supporting documents required for technical evaluation and full contract review were not included in the submission.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to submit to the Audit Team the Monthly Report of Official Travels and Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption for each motor vehicle duly supported with Driver’s Trip Tickets issued during the month for review and evaluation. Henceforth, ensure that the Driver’s Trip Tickets are properly accomplished.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer expressed commitment to comply by vigilantly checking all trip tickets attached in the vouchers and processing payments only with complete information. She also communicated the submission requirement to the Office of the Municipal Engineer and other offices for consolidation.
observations.reason: No consolidated monthly report was submitted in CY 2020. The Driver’s Trip Tickets were still not properly accomplished as distance traveled during each trip was not indicated.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the MENRO to complete the conduct of WACS and subsequently secure the approval of the 10-year SWM Plan from the NSWMC. Furthermore, continue their efforts in establishing the SLF.
observations.managementAction: The MENRO assured compliance and provided updates such as the memorandum signed by the Municipal Mayor and landowner allowing the use of land/lot as SLF. Data gathering for WACS was affected by COVID-19 but preparatory activities were undertaken.
observations.reason: WACS was not completed in CY 2020 so approval of the 10-year SWM Plan was not secured. Establishment of the SLF remained in process pending acquisition and compliance with MGB-DENR requirements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to: a. revert the unutilized LDRRMF from CY 2014 and make it available for other social services upon enactment by the Sangguniang Bayan; b. reclassify the remaining balance of the financial assistance to Trust Liabilities-DRRMF account; c. reconcile the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF and the general ledger and make necessary adjustments; d. transfer the P2,000.00 savings to the TF; and e. prepare and submit to the Audit Team on a monthly basis the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF. Furthermore, instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to make the necessary cash transfers to the LDRRMF account in the TF.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer reverted CY 2014 unutilized balance, transferred unutilized LDRRMF of CY 2019 from GF to TF, and transferred cash from GF to TF for unexpended balances of prior years to settle reciprocal Due to/Due from Other Funds. The Municipal Accountant reclassified financial assistance to TL-DRRMF in CY 2021 and made adjusting journal entries. A check was issued for transfer of P2,000.00 savings and adjustments were made for some items. He assured submission of monthly reports thereafter.
observations.reason: Some adjustments and transfers were made in CY 2020 and CY 2021 but reconciliation of the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF with the general ledger for CY 2017 and other items were still in process. The P2,000.00 transfer check was issued but subject to verification.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary correcting entries in the GF for the reverted DRRMF of CYs 2014 and 2015.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant made the necessary correcting entries in CY 2021 as shown in the Journal Entry Voucher attached to his letter dated June 14, 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MPDC to continue the collection of SDDs and establish a GAD database that will be the basis of the GAD programs to be implemented. Furthermore, require the GAD Focal Person to ensure that the Municipality continually adheres with the provisions of the JMC.
observations.managementAction: The MPDC committed to continue the collection of SDDs and establishment of the GAD database. He initiated orientation on CBMS and planned activities to fast track completion of the CBMS to establish the GAD database. Some SDDs were obtained during facilitation of Social Amelioration Program documentation and survey.
observations.reason: The Municipality is still in the process of establishing the GAD database and needs to gather all the SDDs through completion of their Community-Based Monitoring System (CBMS).

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: a. Municipal Accountant to continue his efforts in gathering all relevant documents regarding the reconciliation of the aforementioned Inter-Agency Payables in order to establish the breakdown of the unidentified/unaccounted amounts and make the necessary adjustments for the errors in recording; b. Municipal Treasurer to consequently remit the amounts due to the aforementioned government agencies; and c. Municipal Accountant to properly maintain the subsidiary ledgers of the Due to BIR, Due to GSIS, Due to Pag-IBIG and Due to PhilHealth accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant made some correcting entries in CY 2021 to adjust errors pertaining to Due to GSIS and Due to PhilHealth accounts and committed to continue gathering relevant documents to reconcile and remit the unidentified/unaccounted balances. The Municipal Treasurer was requested to remit identified amounts.
observations.reason: Some correcting entries were made in CY 2021 but other unidentified/unaccounted amounts remained and supporting documents were still being gathered to complete reconciliation and remittance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and concerned officials take the necessary actions to settle the notices of suspension and disallowance and to provide supporting documents to the Audit Team.
observations.managementAction: Records were provided in Annex 10 and the Municipality committed to follow-up actions to settle suspensions and disallowances.
observations.reason: Some items remained unsettled as of year-end.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Bids and Awards Committee Secretariat to submit to the Audit Team the copies of the contracts and purchase orders as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2009-001 together with its supporting documents.
observations.managementAction: The BAC Secretariat mentioned they are currently accumulating the aforementioned documents and assured submission of all necessary documents for contract review and evaluation.
observations.reason: Contracts were submitted via electronic mail but supporting documents required under RA No. 9184 and the checklist of the Circular were not included; hence timely review was precluded.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer/BAC Chairperson to: a. immediately submit to the SA and the ATL the list of all on-going PPAs of the Municipality and those that are to be implemented in CY 2021. Henceforth, to submit the same reports at the beginning of the succeeding years; b. inform the Audit Team within ten days after the award of PPAs or before its start that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices were already posted for validation; and c. regularly submit the monthly monitoring report.
observations.managementAction: The BAC Chairperson committed to comply with the audit recommendations as soon as possible.
observations.reason: The list of on-going PPAs and monthly monitoring reports were not submitted at the beginning of the year; only the last quarter report was submitted. The Audit Team was not informed within the required timeline regarding project signboards and public notices.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to: a. reclassify the financial assistance from various Municipalities, Provincial Government of Quezon, and National Government to TL-DRRMF account; b. reconcile the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF with the general ledger balance of the account in CY 2017 and make the necessary adjustments; c. transfer the P2,000.00 savings to the TF; and d. prepare and submit to the Audit Team on a monthly basis the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF. Furthermore, instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to make the necessary cash transfers to the LDRRMF account in the TF.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant reclassified some financial assistance to TL-DRRMF in CY 2021 and made adjustments. A check was issued for transfer of the P2,000.00 savings to the TF and Journal Entry Vouchers were presented for adjustments made. He assured monthly reports will be submitted thereafter.
observations.reason: Some reclassifications and adjustments were made but reconciliation of the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF with the general ledger for CY 2017 and other items remained to be completed; the P2,000.00 transfer was issued but verification pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary correcting entries in the GF for the reverted DRRMF of CYs 2014 and 2015.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant made the necessary correcting entries in CY 2021 as shown in the Journal Entry Voucher attached to his letter dated June 14, 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MPDC to continue the collection of SDDs and establish a GAD database that will be the basis of the GAD programs to be implemented. Furthermore, require the GAD Focal Person to ensure that the Municipality continually adheres with the provisions of the JMC.
observations.managementAction: The MPDC committed to adhere and provided updates and planned activities to fast track the completion of the CBMS to fully establish their GAD database.
observations.reason: To fully establish the GAD database, the Office of the MPDC needs to gather all the SDDs through completion of their Community-Based Monitoring System (CBMS).

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MENRO to complete the conduct of WACS and subsequently secure the approval of the 10-year SWM Plan from the NSWMC. Furthermore, continue their efforts in establishing the SLF.
observations.managementAction: The MENRO provided updates that materials needed in the WACS survey for the 16 barangays were prepared but data collection was not completed due to COVID-19. Efforts regarding compliance with MGB-DENR recommendations for the SLF were ongoing and an agreement regarding land acquisition was nearing completion pending funding.
observations.reason: WACS completion and NSWMC approval were not achieved in CY 2020 and the SLF establishment remained pending due to acquisition and compliance issues.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: a. Municipal Accountant to continue his efforts in gathering all relevant documents regarding the reconciliation of the aforementioned Inter-Agency Payables in order to establish the breakdown of the unidentified/unaccounted amounts and make the necessary adjustments for the errors in recording; b. Municipal Treasurer to consequently remit the amounts due to the aforementioned government agencies; and c. Municipal Accountant to properly maintain the subsidiary ledgers of the Due to BIR, Due to GSIS, Due to Pag-IBIG and Due to PhilHealth accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant made necessary correcting entries in CY 2021 to adjust the errors pertaining to the Due to GSIS and Due to PhilHealth accounts and assured continuation of efforts to gather supporting documents for reconciliation and remittance. Some remittances were made in CY 2021.
observations.reason: Some correcting entries and remittances were made in CY 2021 but other unidentified/unaccounted amounts remained subject for reconciliation and supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee ensure compliance with RA 9184 by determining the appropriate mode of procurement to be used based on the condition of each of procurement.  Further, submit a valid explanation on why the shopping mode of procurement was used in the procurement of services or rental of sounds and lights instead of public bidding.
observations.managementAction: Submitted explanation and stopped the practice.
observations.reason: Complied by Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant refrain from setting up the Accounts Payable account at year-end based on the obligation requests and see to it that valid claim with supporting documents are present.
observations.managementAction: Stopped the practice.
observations.reason: Complied by Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the designated inspector prepare the pre and post inspection report using the prescribed form and ensure that all the necessary information of the motor vehicles/equipment repaired were complete.
observations.managementAction: Inspection Reports complied completely.
observations.reason: Complied by Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the following adjusting journal entries to correct the error in recording and submit the corresponding Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) within ten days upon receipt of the AOM. Henceforth, we recommended further that the Municipal Accountant review the Journal Entry Vouchers and the supporting documents to ensure that the  entries were correctly made and presented in the Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: Adjusting entries were made.
observations.reason: Complied by Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Engineer and other concerned officials be made to explain on the non-imposition of liquidated damages and the failure to monitor the completion of the project. We further recommended that liquidated damages be imposed and collected for the construction beyond the contract period.
observations.managementAction: Contract Time Extensions, Contract Time Suspensions and Temporary Work Suspensions totaling 330 days were submitted to the audit team to support claim of no delay incurred.
observations.reason: Complied by Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the following journal entries in the General Fund to correct the error in the posting to the general ledger and submit the corresponding Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) within ten days upon receipt of the AOM. Henceforth, due diligence should be exercised in the preparation of the recapitulation report and review should be made before posting the balances in the general ledger to avoid errors that will result to misstatements of the accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Adjusting entries was prepared.
observations.reason: Complied by Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the following journal entries in the General Fund to correct the error in the recording of the advance payments to suppliers/contractors and submit the corresponding Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) within ten days upon receipt of the AOM. Moreover, the Municipal Accountant should review all the journal entries made in the recording of advance payments to suppliers and contractors to ensure that these transactions are properly recorded and correctly classified.
observations.managementAction: Adjusting entries were prepared.
observations.reason: Complied by Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the following journal entries in the General Fund to correct the double recording of the adjusting entry and submit the corresponding Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) within ten days upon receipt of the AOM. Further, require the Municipal Accountant to review and analyze the Loans Payable – Domestic account to ensure that the recorded balance of the account is accurate, complete and fairly presented in the financial statements. Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant should ensure that all journal entries made are properly posted in the general ledger.
observations.managementAction: Adjusting entries were made.
observations.reason: Complied by Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management submit the aforementioned reports to avoid disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: Submitted and trip tickets completely accomplished.
observations.reason: Complied by Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to exhaust all legal remedies against EM Paule Construction and Trading and Poiel Engineering and Management Services and impose / collect the necessary liquidated damages for the failure to complete and deliver the said project on time. Further, consider the idea of terminating the contract due to the default of the contractor.
observations.managementAction: Terminated the contract with EM Paule Construction and Trading and Poiel Engineering and Management and awarded it to a new contractor.
observations.reason: Complied by Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant verify the Construction in Progress and other affected accounts and effect the necessary adjustments in order to reflect the correct balances in the Financial Statements. Also, require the maintenance of the Registry of Public Infrastructures and monitor accounts/assets not recorded in the books of accounts and cause the preparation of the subsidiary ledgers to substantiate the recorded balances in the Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: Completed projects were adjusted to its corresponding asset accounts.
observations.reason: Complied by Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC Municipal Accountant to maintain a separate record for each type of loans of the Municipality to facilitate the recording, monitoring and review thereof.  Also, make representation to the bank and secure data / records of the loans and reconcile the same with the records of the Municipality and ensure that the accrued interest for the loans is properly taken up in the books. We recommended further that the OIC Municipal Accountant prepare the adjusting entry to correct the entry erroneous entry made in recording the procurement of motor vehicle.
observations.managementAction: Subsidiary Ledger was prepared for monitoring of loans payable.
observations.reason: Complied by Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management submit proof of obligation or explanation for the non-obligation of prior year claims.
