Further, we  recommend  that  the  Municipal  Accountant  and  Budget  Officer  should  discontinue  the  practice  of  charging  prior  year’s  expenditure  to  current  year  appropriation  and  that  proper  matching  of  expenses  against  appropriation  should always be observed pursuant to the above-cited regulations.
observations.managementAction: Properly justified by Management.
observations.reason: Complied by Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management strictly observe the guidelines provided under DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017 and ensure that only development projects will be charged to the fund.
observations.managementAction: Stopped the practice.
observations.reason: Complied by Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management strictly adhere to the rules and regulations on the utilization of the disaster risk and reduction management fund especially in the procurement of items not included in the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund Investment Plan to avoid disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: Stopped the practice.
observations.reason: Complied by Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to make representation to the National Telecommunication Commission Region VII on the status of the donation of the motor vehicle subjected to repair and provide the OIC Municipal Accountant with such documents if any as basis for recording in the books.  Further, stop the practice of splitting the purchase requisition or contract pursuant to COA Circular 76-41 and require the Bids and Awards Committee to submit written explanation on the splitting of the contract on the repairs of the subject motor vehicle.
observations.managementAction: Donations already presented to the audit team.
observations.reason: Complied by Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer exert effort to collect the rentals including penalties, interests and surcharges and business taxes from delinquent market stall renters/lessees and business owners to generate additional income that the agency can use for the implementation of its projects/programs.
observations.managementAction: Collections were properly made.
observations.reason: Complied by Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer furnish the OIC Municipal Accountant with the Real Property Tax Account Register (RPTAR) as basis in the establishment and booking up of the RPT and SET Receivables at the beginning of the year in order to reflect the actual receivables.
observations.managementAction: RPT and SET Receivables properly established at the beginning of the year.
observations.reason: Complied by Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management issue demand letters to the concerned officers and employees to immediately settle their outstanding cash advances.  Otherwise, cause the withholding of their salary pursuant to the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002.
observations.managementAction: Current cash advances are properly liquidated.
observations.reason: Complied by Management.

observations.recommendation: We required management to explain why the procurement was made in two separate contracts instead of one time purchase and its preference to procure the same from a local supplier.
observations.managementAction: Justification submitted and properly evaluated.
observations.reason: Complied by Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the concerned Municipal Mayor see to it that financial assistance released to the Liga which is sourced from the development fund be duly supported with specific projects/programs for proper utilization and implementation thereof. There should be a resolution from the Sanggunian Bayan for the Mayor to enter into contract with the Liga for the implementation of the projects pursuant to G.R. No. 175527 of the Supreme Court.
observations.managementAction: Stopped the practice.
observations.reason: Complied by Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to see to it that all expenditures for repairs be supported with complete documentation and ensure proper observance of the procedures and to avoid incurrence of excessive expenditures. Further, require the personnel in charge at the motor pool to initiate the maintenance of the individual records of the equipment and adopt measures to protect the equipment and motor vehicles of the municipality.
observations.managementAction: Stopped the practice.
observations.reason: Complied by Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Accountant to make the necessary adjustments to correct the erroneous recording of the above transactions. The NGAS Chart of Accounts should always be used as the basis in the proper use of accounts and in the recording of transactions. Proper care should always be exercised in the recording of financial information to reflect a fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Adjusting entries was prepared.
observations.reason: Complied by Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to transfer the unexpended balance of the DRRM Fund in the Trust Liabilities-DRRMF account in the Trust Fund. Also, require the Municipal Accountant to include in the Notes to Financial Statement the DRRM Fund disclosure in accordance with COA Circular 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: Unexpended balance of LDRRM fund was transferred but no disclosure was made in the Notes to FS.
observations.reason: Partially complied by Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management direct the members of the local finance committee of the Municipality of San Francisco, Cebu, to refund honoraria they received as members of said committee for CY 2012.  Henceforth, the giving of honoraria to the members of the local finance committee be discontinued.
observations.managementAction: Stopped the practice of giving honoraria to the local finance committee.
observations.reason: Paid honoraria were not yet refunded.

observations.managementAction: The management has created an inventory team. The team has started to do the physical count of the PPE. The property officer is under the treasury department still and now in process of reconstructing documents/records relative to property control.
observations.reason: Partially Implemented

observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant was directed to reclassify the above-enumerated financial transactions to their appropriate/correct account and to do the same in the succeeding transactions with reference to the requirements set forth in Sections 4.8 and 112 of P.D. No. 1445, paragraph 7 of IPSAS No. 1, and COA Circular No. 2015-009.
observations.reason: Fully Implemented

observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant was directed to present distinctively in the CY 2019 Statement of Financial Position the current and non-current portion of the Loans Payable-Domestic Account of P2,596,348.49 through proper reclassification thereof in the amount of P1,005,038.28 and P1,591,310.21, respectively, as required in Section 112 of P.D. No. 1445 and paragraph numbers 70 and 80 of IPSAS No. 1.
observations.reason: Fully Implemented

observations.managementAction: The Management was directed to stop utilizing the LDRRMF for maintenance and operating expenses and capital outlay.  These should be charged against the general fund of the LGU.  Submit to COA the justification on the payments made amounting to P156,749.46 which shall be evaluated on whether to issue Notice of Suspension or Disallowance on the transactions as circumstances may warrant; Instruct the MDRRMO to carefully plan and include only in the LDRRMFIP the PAPs that are considered very important and urgent which need to be realized in a particular year, with due consideration of the scarce resources/allocation and to implement the same optimally; Require the MBO to prepare and furnish COA the Reports on the Receipt and Utilization of Cash Donations in the manner prescribed in Section V.A (1) of COA Circular 2014-002 and as shown in the sample form provided in Annex I thereof; Advise the MDRRMO to timely submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to COA on or before the 15th day after the end of each month as required in Item 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002; and ensure compliance to the provisions of Section 2 of R.A. No. 10121, Item 4 of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1, Item 4.3 of NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC JMC No. 2014-1, Section V.A (1) of COA Circular No. 2014-002 and Item 5.1.12 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 in the budgeting, utilization and reporting of the LDRRMF.
observations.reason: Partially Implemented

observations.managementAction: The HRMO-designate was directed to execute a standard form contract with the Job Orders as prescribed in the CSC Resolution No. 021480 bearing the necessary information as required therein. These contracts should bear two (2) witnesses and which a copy thereof should be issued to and received by the concerned employee.
observations.reason: Fully Implemented

observations.managementAction: The Management was directed to direct the GFPS to always formulate the LGU GPB based on the GAD Agenda or identified priority gender issues which were derived from the information gathered from the sources mentioned in Sections 4.1.C.2.1 and 4.1.C.1.4 of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01; The MSWDO agreed to ensure implementation of all the GAD activity or activities that will resolve GAD issues and concerns; Formulate and include only GAD PAPs that would support government’s commitment to promote equality among men and women to resources and opportunities and which specifically addressed gender issues and concerns; and strictly adhere to the provisions set forth in R.A. 9710 and PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 in the budgeting and implementation/utilization of GAD fund.
observations.reason: Partially Implemented

observations.managementAction: We recommended and the SB agreed that through its presiding officer, the Vice-Mayor, enact an ordinance promulgating rental fee rates to be used in the assessment by the Treasury and which to be paid by the clients to utilize the municipal gymnasium, municipal women’s Center and various chairs and tables, taking into consideration the objectives of the facilities.  The Municipal Mayor agreed to formulate operating procedures, with the assistance of the municipal treasurer and engineering office, covering the utilization of the said facilities.  These procedures must include the accomplishment by the client of an application/booking form before the properties will be used and which shall be the basis also by the treasury for the issuance of OR.  The procedures will also determine who will clean the venue before and after use, the municipal personnel to maintain the cleanliness and order of the amenities, grievance measures to follow for any untoward incidents while using the venue and other procedures to safeguard the properties against loss and damage and to ensure full economic gain therefrom.
observations.reason: Partially Implemented

observations.managementAction: We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed that, with the assistance from the accounting, treasury and budget, execute a comprehensive set of guidelines/policies, as well as the MOA governing the fund transfers for operational fund to the barangays.  These guidelines must clearly provide the legal basis of the financial assistance, the objective thereof, the duties and responsibilities of the municipality with regard to the funds, the duties and responsibilities of the barangays in receiving and utilizing the funds, the penalties to incur when a party departed from the agreements, etc.  Meanwhile, the MOA must contain supplemental provisions from the guidelines/policies which are integral for the grant, utilization and reporting of the financial assistance paid; The municipal accountant agreed to follow-up and ensure that the operational funds granted to the barangays must be liquidated at the end of the year; and faithfully adhere to the provisions of Section 2 of P.D. No. 1445 and Item 3.0 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 on the grant of operational funds to the barangays.
observations.reason: Partially Implemented

observations.managementAction: We recommended that Management submit to COA the justification on payments without the Contract/MOA, SB Resolution and Accomplishment Reports.  The justification must be accompanied with the letter alleged made to cover the availment of the dental services.  Failure to do so or after due evaluation but unsatisfied with the legal basis/justification or the documents received, the Audit Team may cause the issuance of Notice of Suspension or Disallowance for the total amount paid as circumstances may warrant.  Strictly adhere to Sections 4(6) and 4(7) of P.D. No. 1445 in the payments of services.
observations.reason: Fully Implemented

observations.managementAction: We recommended and the Municipal Engineer and MDRRMO agreed to provide the facilities/amenities required to be existing in an evacuation center. These basic necessities should be addressed first to provide the needed care, service and assistance to the evacuees; The MDRRMO and the Budget Officer agreed to appropriate a portion of the 70% of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (LDRRMF) to provide additional fund for the construction of the required facilities of an evacuation center; and adhere to Section 2 (p) of R.A. No. 10121 and Section 2 of P.D. 1445 in the implementation and operation of the Evacuation Center.
observations.reason: Fully Implemented

observations.managementAction: Management enjoin the Local School Board (LSB) to implement all the programs, projects and activities (PAPs) embodied in the LSB annual budget. The inclusion of the PAPs in the budget was because these were already considered by the board as important, urgent, and therefore priority and adhere to the declared policy of the State mentioned in R.A. No. 5447 and Section 2 of P.D. No. 1445 in the utilization of SEF.
observations.reason: Partially implemented

observations.managementAction: Advise the MSWDO to ensure implementation of all the LCPC PPAs so that the fund is optimally utilized; remind the MBO to correctly charge/obligate every financial transaction against its corresponding appropriation; follow-up with the DOLE on the status of the implementation of the project Construction of the Social Protection Center in the municipality to establish whether there is still a need to appropriation an amount from the LCPC fund as an LGU equity thereof; send a letter-inquiry to the City Government of Surigao on the amount to be paid for availing the services of the Bahay Pag-asa. This may avoid penalties and additional charges that may incur for non-payment or late settlement of accounts; and adhere faithfully to Section 2 of P.D. No. 1445 and Section 2 of R.A. No. 9344 in the utilization of LCPC fund or implementation of its PAPs.
observations.reason: Partially implemented

observations.managementAction: Require the BAC, including the BAC Secretariat and the TWG, to submit to the Audit Team the lacking documents and to explain to the latter the presence of procurement deficiencies/ irregularities. Non-compliance hereof may cause the suspension of the transactions and adhere strictly with the provisions of R.A. 9184 and its IRR, COA Circular Nos. 2009-001 and 2012-001 on the procurement of goods, infrastructures and services.
observations.reason: Not implemented

observations.managementAction: Direct the DTI-BuB Focal Person to supervise and/or implement the project Nito Production in accordance with the approved plan and its objectives as provided in the Item 2 of the MOA; instruct the Organization Treasurer, through the DTI-BuB Focal Person, to diligently prepare and maintain correct, proper and complete financial records and statements to show the financial operations and condition of the project. Register with the BIR so that OR can be used by the organization. Prepare vouchers too or any document showing an authorized payment so that the utilization of the fund is controlled. The DTI-BuB Focal Person is enjoined to submit to COA the CYs 2016-2018 financial statements/ reports of the project; advise the organization, through the DTI-BuB Focal Person, to set aside fund for the construction of a production area. This will house the weaving machineries, utensils and other equipment which shall be procured by the LGU as mentioned in the MOA. Create also a production timeline/schedule; and instruct the DTI-BuB Focal Person and other concerned personnel to regularly monitor the condition and performance of the project as well as the other PAPs under DTI. Monitoring includes ocular inspection, confirming compliance to the provisions in the MOA. These ensure immediate action to the rising problems which will result to the achievement of the project’s primary objective as well as guarantee profitability and sustainability of the same.
observations.reason: Partially implemented

observations.managementAction: Direct the DTI-BuB Focal Person to supervise and/or implement the project Establishment of Bigasan ng Barangay in accordance with the approved plan and its objectives as provided in the Item 2 of the MOA; instruct the Organization Treasurer, through the DTI-BuB Focal Person, to diligently prepare and maintain correct, proper and complete financial records and statements to show the financial operations and condition of the project; register the business with the BIR so that OR can be used by the organization. Prepare vouchers too or any document showing an authorized payment so that the utilization of the fund is controlled. The DTI-BuB Focal Person is enjoined to submit to COA the CYs 2016-2018 financial statements/ reports of the project; advise the organization, through the DTI-BuB Focal Person, to refrain from selling the grain rice on credit. Revenue-wise, these commodities shall be sold on a per kilo basis; direct the association, through the DTI-BuB Focal Person to utilize the supplies and equipment as intended. The group must appoint among its members a capable and dependable individual to man the project; and instruct the DTI-BuB Focal Person and other concerned personnel to regularly monitor the condition and performance of the project as well as the other PAPs under DTI. These ensure immediate action to the rising problems which will result to the achievement of the project’s primary objective as well as guarantee profitability and sustainability of the same.
observations.reason: Partially implemented

observations.managementAction:
Advise the association, through the DTI-BuB Focal Person, to utilize the amount of P32,362.00 to purchase the starter kit. These equipment and supplies will be used to produce more outputs, even the absence of a production room and to utilize only, for the meantime, the available spaces in the house of each member of the group. More productions will give more income to the group. This income will eventually be utilized to complete the building which will serve as the production area and display center; instruct the Organization Treasurer, through the DTI-BuB Focal Person, to prepare and maintain financial records and statements to show the financial operations and condition of the project. Register with the BIR so that OR can be used by the organization. The ORs can be utilized as control to properly and completely monitor the receipt of funds by the association. Prepare vouchers too or any document showing an authorized payment so that the utilization of the fund is controlled. Create also a production timeline/ schedule. This schedule will
provide the number of products to make in a given period, how many members needed to produce the products and who will produce thereof vis-à-vis the target sales of the same period. The presence of the production timeline will ensure synchronized, uniform and quality outputs; and instruct the DTI-BuB Focal Person and other concerned personnel to regularly monitor the condition and performance of the project as well as the other PAPs under DTI. Monitoring includes ocular inspection, confirming compliance to the provisions in the MOA. These ensure immediate action to the problems which will result to the achievement of the project’s primary objective as well as guarantee profitability and sustainability of the same.
observations.reason: Partially implemented

observations.managementAction: Revisit the MOA and the business plan of the project. Together with the officers and members of the organization, reevaluate whether the project, with the present circumstances, is totally not feasible to operate. Identify the presence of remedial actions or possible solutions to the identified problems like planting a variety of coconut trees suitable for sap production only. They can ask opinion/idea from the Philippine Coconut Authority on this matter. Requesting reasonable rate from the coconut farmers to get saps from tall coconut trees could be other alternative. These coconut farmers should be explained that the benefits of the project would redound also to their end in the future; initiate a meeting together with the officers of the organization with the DOLE, if the foregoing recommendation is not viable. Discuss in the meeting the possible business alternatives which are feasible in the locality considering its available resources, labor and market and whether these indeed will benefit the people. Consult the end-users concerning thereof; and instruct the Project Focal Person to regularly monitor the condition and performance of the project as well as the other PAPs under DOLE. Monitoring includes ocular inspection, confirming compliance to the provisions in the business plan and agreements. These ensure immediate action to the rising problems which will result to the achievement of the project objectives as well as guarantee profitability and sustainability of the same.
observations.reason: Partially implemented

observations.managementAction: Secure a copy of the MOA from source Agencies to cover each of the financial assistance received; make a representation before the source Agency that every financial assistance granted to the LGU should be covered with a MOA; ensure that financial assistance received must be credited to  Liability/Payable account instead of Income account to maintain accountability; and faithfully adhere to the provisions of Section 2 of P.D. No. 1445 and Item 3.0 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 in the receipt of financial assistance/aids from various sources.
observations.reason: Partially implemented

observations.managementAction: Revisit the provisions of the project.  Discuss with the DA on how the problem be solved with due consideration of the fact that, to date, there had been no suppliers found for the native chicken in which the procurement thereof would not violate the provisions of the procurement law.  However, if the project will not materialize, what other alternative courses of action be done so that the fund allocated thereof could still be used optimally.   Make a suggestion to the DA that projects for implementation in partnership with the Municipality should run through proper discussion and consultation.  This way, the need of the people will be rightfully provided/answered.  Thus, the fund intended thereof will be effectively utilized; faithfully adhere to the provisions of Section 2 of P.D. No. 1445 in the implementation of the LGU’s various projects, programs and activities with a view that the resources of the government must be safeguarded against loss or wastage; and submit to COA the MOA covering the implementation of the project.
observations.reason: Partially implemented

observations.managementAction: Advise the barangay officials and civil society organization representatives, if there are any, that in identifying priority projects in the unit to be funded from whatever source, select the projects that are necessary, doable and readily implementable. Non-compliance hereof would result to wastage and possible loss of government resources; remind the Local Poverty Reduction Action Team (LPRAT) that before submitting to the DILG RO the list of priority projects for funding, that these should be reviewed carefully whether the same are necessary, doable and readily implementable; ensure compliance to the requirements set forth in Item 5.8 of DBM-DILG-DSWD-NAPC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 7 dated November 3, 2015 in case there is revision/changes in the project; and suggest to the LPRAT and the barangay officials of Barangay Oslao that if possible, to find an amount to be used in the opening and development of the road planned to be concreted so that the latter activity/project will commence as soon as possible.
observations.reason: Partially implemented

observations.managementAction:
Partially implemented

The Mayor had already talked with the SB Secretary, suggesting their end to invite the owner of the land to one of their sessions and request from him to surrender the title of the land.  Other audit recommendations are still uncomplied.  No additional development had been made to date.
observations.reason: Partially implemented

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the newly assumed Municipal Accountant to expedite the retrieval of relevant documents that substantiate the account balance, otherwise remit the amount to the National Treasury in compliance with Executive Order No. 431, s. 2005 of the President of the Philippines.
observations.reason: See Observation No. 11, Part II of this AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the newly-assumed Municipal Accountant to expedite the retrieval of the relevant documents after which, transfer the Construction in Progress account to its appropriate asset accounts to reflect the correct balance of the accounts.
observations.reason: The CY 2019 year-end balance of the Construction in Progress account was not reliable because no details or breakdown were provided. See Observation No. 9, Part II of this AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Not Implemented that the Municipal Mayor fully institutionalize the LDRRMO by filling up the statutory/mandatory positions required under NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2014-1 to assure implementation of actions and measures related to disaster risk reduction and management.

We further recommended that whoever is appointed to perform physical count shall undergo a training on Property and Supply Management System (PSMS), to gain adequate understanding and working knowledge of the systems, processes, policies and other regulations pertaining to property supply and management.

We recommended Not Implemented that the Local Chief Executive should: 1. Compel the Municipal Accountant to review the details of the Due from NGAs, Due from LGUs and Other Receivable accounts and exert all efforts to identify the recipients of the fund transfers; 2. Direct the Municipal Accountant to comply with the reportorial requirements on funds transferred to local or national government agencies or other debtors in order to avoid accumulation of unliquidated balances of the accounts.

We recommended Not Implemented that the Municipal Mayor direct the members of the BAC and the Municipal Engineer to comply with procurement procedures and ensure that all necessary documentary requirements are present before effecting payments in order to validate the projects and disbursements.

We recommended Not Implemented that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Engineer to submit all documents that would warrant the non-imposition of liquidated damages to the Auditor; require the Municipal Engineer to prepare a report on the unperformed portions of the infrastructure projects based on their respective completion dates.

We recommended Not Implemented that the Local Chief Executive consider creating and organizing an Internal Audit Services (IAS) to identify potential errors, risks or irregularities and to provide assurance for a sound and adequate internal control system in the operations of the Municipality.

We recommended Not Implemented that the concerned LGU personnel submit justifications and documents explaining the procurement mode and direct that final and terminal reports be submitted with GIS generated maps and photographs to validate accomplishments.

We recommended Not Implemented that in future similar projects, the LCE, BAC and the Municipal Engineer should ensure funds are available and complete detailed engineering is undertaken before projects are implemented to avoid delays; and to contact the contractor to continue construction to avoid further delays.

We recommended Not Implemented that the Local Chief Executive provide financial and technical assistance to barangays for establishment of MRFs, enjoin barangays to implement projects in the Annual Investment Program and undertake wide dissemination of information on source reduction, recycling and composting.

We recommended Not Implemented that tax collection efforts be intensified, billing notices be sent, delinquent properties published and remedies such as auction, levy and distraint of personal property be pursued.

We recommended Not Implemented that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to follow NGAS accounting procedures regarding maintenance of special accounts and subsidiary ledgers and to report on sources and utilization of LDRRMF.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Assessor and General Services Officer to fast track the processing of the Certificate of Titles of all parcels of land acquired by the municipality pursuant to Section 39 of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Assessor will process the Transfer of Titles for land still not under the name of the LGU.
observations.reason: Still on the processing of Certificate of Transfer of Titles.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: 1. Instruct the GAD Focal Person to submit the required supporting documents of GAD AR and to coordinate with the Municipal Budget Office for the reconciliation of actual amount utilized; and 2. Maximize the utilization of the GAD fund for the year so that wider scope of intended beneficiaries can be benefited of the GAD programs, projects and activities, and objective of the fund can be fully achieved during the year.
observations.managementAction: The management will collate and submit the required documents per Joint Circular No. 2004-1 dated April 5, 2004 of the DBM, NEDA and NCRFW. The utilization amount is still being reconciled between the two departments.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and
the Municipal Treasurer to have their records reconciled and make the necessary
adjustments for the discrepancies. Furthermore, require the Municipal Accountant
to:
a. exert efforts in retrieving relevant documents as basis in preparing accurate
BRS;
b. consequently, submit to the Audit Team copies of the BRS prepared; and
c. make the necessary adjusting entries pertaining to the errors identified in the
post audit of CY 2019 transactions.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to
retrieve relevant documents relative to the recording made on the respective
accounts and reconcile the accounts. Prepare the necessary adjustments where
appropriate.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to
retrieve relevant documents regarding the reconciliation of the abovementioned
accounts and make the necessary adjustments thereof.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to:
a. exert effort in locating relevant documents that will support the reconciliation of
the discrepancies and the determination of complete details and breakdown of
the accounts balances;
b. make the necessary adjustments pertaining to errors in the respective
accounts; and
c. henceforth, ensure that all transactions are properly recorded.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to
ensure that all cash advances are liquidated at the end of each year. Furthermore,
instruct the concerned officials/employees/accountable officer to immediately
liquidate the remaining cash advances. Otherwise, implement the withholding of
their respective salaries.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to
provide the details or breakdown of the account to ensure its accuracy and validity.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a. Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Assessor to exert effort in providing the
breakdown or details of the recorded land account amounting to P2,304,909.00 or
reconcile the same with the unrecorded lots owned by the Municipality;
b. Municipal Treasurer/Municipal Assessor in locating documents to establish the
amount and mode of acquisition of the unrecorded lots owned by the
Municipality or prioritize the creation of the Appraisal Committee for the
valuation of the unrecorded lands, titled and untitled, which will be used as the
basis of the Municipal Accountant in recording the parcels of land/adjusting the
Land account balance; and
c. Municipal Assessor to facilitate the titling of all untitled lots owned by the
Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Inventory Committee to:
a. ensure that all PPE owned/controlled by the Municipality are included in the
physical count;
b. henceforth prepare the RPCPPE presented per class; and
c. submit to the Audit Team copies of the complete RPCPPE and RPCLRN in the
forms prescribed in the existing rules and regulations.

Moreover, require the Inventory Committee and the Municipal Accountant to
reconcile the results of the physical count with the accounting records and make
the necessary adjustments, if any.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to
coordinate with the Municipal Engineer in providing the details or breakdown of the
accounts and make the necessary adjustments.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to:
a. reconcile the loans balances in the general ledger and the statement of accounts
provided by EWBC and make the necessary adjustment thereof; and
b. henceforth, ensure that all transactions are properly recorded.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to
exert effort in retrieving all relevant documents regarding the SORDs of the funds
received and its reconciliation with the subsidiary ledger to establish the correct
balances of the Due to NGAs account and make the necessary adjustments.
Henceforth, ensure the proper recording of funds received from the National
Government in the Due to NGAs account to monitor the utilization and liquidation
with the source agencies.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to
gather all relevant documents regarding the reconciliation of the Due to LGUs
account and make the necessary adjustments. Consequently, require the Municipal
Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to remit the amount due to the Provincial
Government and the Barangays.

Henceforth, we recommended the Municipal Accountant to properly maintain the
subsidiary ledgers of the Due to LGUs account pertaining to the Provincial
Government and Barangays of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to
make the necessary adjustment thereof and henceforth ensure that all transactions
are properly recorded.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to:
a. make the necessary adjustments pertaining to the errors in recording the prior
year’s expenses and henceforth ensure that all transactions are properly
recorded;
b. coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer in locating the missing checks
identified in the non-chronological sequence of checks issued and submit to the
Audit Team a report supported with the necessary documents indicating its
status whether the said checks were cancelled, unrecorded or others;
c. propose internal controls, subject to the approval of the Municipal Mayor, that
will improve document flow and organization of disbursements of the
Municipality to produce complete, accurate and reliable financial information;
and
d. exert efforts in retrieving the unsubmitted DVs in CY 2017 and submit the same
to the Audit Team for verification and post audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to
require the MCs to remit their collections daily. Furthermore, ensure that the RCDs
are sequentially numbered to conform with the prescribed form.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC Chairperson to ensure
that all concerned officials or end-users shall state the details, manner and
schedule of their respective PPAs’ procurement plan in their PPMPs and submit it
to the BAC to facilitate consolidation and preparation of the APP.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Treasurer to submit to the Audit Team the Monthly Report of Official Travels and
Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption for each motor vehicle duly supported with
Driver’s Trip Tickets issued during the month for review and evaluation. Henceforth,
see to it that the Driver’s Trip Tickets were properly accomplished.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Bids and
Awards Committee (BAC) Secretariat to submit to the Audit Team the copies of the
contracts and purchase orders as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2009-001 together
with its supporting documents.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Engineer/Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Chairperson to:
a. immediately submit to the SA and the ATL the list of all on-going PPAs of the
Municipality and those that are to be implemented in CY 2020. Henceforth, to
submit the same reports at the beginning of the succeeding years;
b. inform the Audit Team within ten days after the award of PPAs or before its start
that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices were already
posted for validation; and
c. regularly submit the monthly monitoring report.

We recommended that the Management send demand letter to the concerned Barangays, through the Municipal Accountant, for the immediate submission of liquidation report/fund utilization and accomplishment report of the abovementioned funds transfers, especially those fund transfers that were granted from CY 2014 to CY2018. In the case that the Barangay continuously ignore the demand, find any legal remedies and/or strategies to compel them for the immediate liquidation for the funds transferred, if necessary.
It is also recommended to refrain from granting two fund transfers at the same time. Download another fund to barangays once the previous funds transfer was fully liquidated.  In addition, verify and monitor the implementation of projects funded out of the transferred funds especially for long outstanding downloaded funds and demand return of the fund if found not utilized or used.

We recommended that the Management continue sending demand letters for the liquidation of the outstanding fund transfer and making follow ups to all NGOs/POs concerned. In the event that the concerned POs continue to ignore the demand, legal remedies pursuant to existing laws, rules and regulations applicable for violation of the provision of COA Circular No. 2007-001 should be imposed as provided in Item 9 of the said circular.

We recommended that the Management:
Issue a demand letter to all accountable officers who are not already connected with the LGU for the immediate liquidation/ settlement of their unliquidated cash advances and in case of non-compliance, find any legal remedies to compel the liquidation/settlement of cash advances; and
Make necessary actions for the proper reporting of dormant accounts by submitting request for write-off of dormant accounts and its underlying supporting documents to prove that the accounts are no longer collectible.

We recommended that the Municipality use the maximum monthly compensation of P5,000.00 per month as basis in computing the employer share as provided in RA 9679 or a maximum of P100.00 per month regardless of the amount that the employee contributes for the personal share, in conformity to the IRR of RA 9679.

We recommended that the Management instruct the Municipal Engineer to furnish the Office of Auditor with copies of suspension orders duly supported with the required documents for its immediate determination if the grounds for suspension is valid or not. Strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 80-101C dated July 28, 1980.

We recommended that the Management direct those involved in the implementation of the projects to see to it that the installation/posting of project signboard should be made as soon as the award has been made and installed project signboard should be completely filled up and updated data to promote transparency of governance.
It is also recommended that the Management inform the Office of the Auditor when project signboard was already installed as prescribed under Section 3.1 of COA Circular 2013-004.

We recommended that the Management require the Sanggunian concerned that authorization for all realignment and/or augmentation of funds shall be through a passage of an Appropriation Ordinance as prescribed under Section 336 of RA 7160. Also, instruct the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant that no disbursements will be made from realigned and/or augmented funds unless supported with an appropriation ordinance.

We recommended that the Management carefully examine the surety bonds submitted by the contractors. Also, infrastructure projects shall only be accepted after one year from its completion to make use of the defects liability period covered by the surety bonds.

We recommended that the Management submit the Annual GAD Accomplishment Report and its supporting documents within 5 days from end of January of the preceding year. Also, the Local Chief Executive conduct regular monitoring of the implementation of GAD Plan and Budget to ensure the full utilization of the fund at the end of the year.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to  withhold the excess amount during the payment of the contract, otherwise, a Notice of Disallowance will be issued on the same amount.
We further recommended to:
Compute and impose liquidated damages equivalent to 8CD delayed of completion of the project Community-Based Rehabilitation (Perimeter Fence);
Monitor the minor scaling noted for Municipal Road Concreting at Apitong Street. If it may get worsen, an immediate rectification/maintenance shall be applied
Instruct the Municipal Engineer, the Bids and Awards Committee and the Technical Working Group to meticulously review all the documents and the basis of the Approved Budget of the Contract before proceeding to the procurement process.
Instruct the concerned officials and employees to keep updated with DPWH guidelines and standards which shall be the basis in preparation of the ABC and in the implementation of infrastructure projects.

We recommended that the Management to:
Refrain from charging expenses to Motorpool that is not directly attributable to its operation;
Allocate fund from General Proper for the expenses incurred in using the equipment in the delivery of its basic services; and
Open separate bank account for every economic enterprise so that its income will not be interchangeable used by other economic enterprise.
It is also recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan and the Local Finance Committee revisit the operations of the said economic enterprise and ascertain the viability and sustainability of the economic enterprise by finding alternative ways on how to generate more income to cover the funding requirement for the year. Otherwise, dissolution of these economic enterprise will be recommended if it still continue to incur losses in the future.

We recommended that the Management instruct the Property Custodian to update the AREs every three years and in case of transfer of property from one to another official or employee.
It is also recommended to file the AREs and ICS separately and in alphabetical order for easy tracing. Further, AREs should be pre-numbered and important information should be filled up completely.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the Management:
Charge the payment of wages of LGUs personnel, assistance to barangays, schools & POs, supplies and materials, electric bills, fuel & oil, TEVs, catering services and other expenses in the MOOE and/or Capital Outlay in the General Fund Proper or in other appropriate fund/programs of the concerned offices; and
Refrain from using the appropriations for Peace and Order for purposes other than those authorized under Joint Circular (JC) No. 2015-01 and the Policies and Guidelines of DILG MC 99-65.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant and Budget officer to discontinue the practice of charging prior year’s expenditures to current year appropriations. Follow strictly Section 11 of PD 1445 and Section 350 of RA 7160.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the former BAC and TWG to submit written explanation why said supplier were allowed to transact with the Municipality and not subjected to automatic disqualification.
It is also recommended to disqualify bidders which are related to the HOPE and other officials involved in the procurement within the third civil degree of consanguinity or affinity and follow strictly Section 47 of the RIRR of RA 9184.

We recommended that the Management require the contractors to rectify the deficiencies noted on the said projects, otherwise, refund of the equivalent amount should be collected from the contractors.
It is also recommended to deduct the amount of liquidated damages in the contractor’s billing if there is any on-going project implemented by the concerned contractors, otherwise, refund should be collected from them.
Henceforth, the implementation of the projects should be closely monitored by giving emphasis on the quality of all on-going and upcoming projects to avoid recurrence of the said deficiencies so that comfort of the populace at large can be provided.

We recommended that Management ensure the optimum utilization of the 20% Development Fund by full implementation of programmed development PPAs during the year and monitor the implementation thereof in order to attain the desired benefits as envisioned in Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 of DILG and DBM. In the event that the Barangays were not yet qualified to be downloaded funds for the implementation of the barangay projects, it is recommended that the LGU will be the one to undertake the implementation of the projects.
We further recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Development Council to assess the feasibility study of the unimplemented projects and/or unutilized balances prior to realignment and continue to implement those projects that are still found to be feasible. Also, the MPDO shall properly monitor the implementation of the projects especially those that are funded from previous years to avoid accumulation of unimplemented projects and/or unutilized appropriation from 20% Development Fund.
It is also recommended to submit the required documents for the inspection of the above-mentioned completed projects.

We recommended that the Management ensure the implementation of all PPAs in the LDRRMFIP, thus preventing or lessening the terrible effects of disaster that may occur anytime in the municipality. If possible, allocate additional budget for flood control project which can minimize the risk of flood during heavy rains and/or can lessen the damage dealt by an occurring calamity. For those LDRRMF which remained unutilized for two years or more, Management may reprogram such PPAs to other important LDRRM related PPAs which are readily implementable.

2.8	We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, together with the key personnel of the LGU like the Municipal Accountant and the MPDC, convene with the SMWD officers together with the respective resident Auditors.  Clarify the intention of both parties as regards the transfer of the waterworks/sewerage facilities, including the water rights of the LGU’s Water System, to the SMWD in exchange of the latter’s payment of P5,000,000.00.  Amend the provisions in the project study and the MOA that are found conflicting with the established intentions of the parties.  Ensure that the municipality and the water district should have a uniform interpretation of the provision/stipulation stated in the project study and MOA.  Adjust the PPE account of the LGU based on the established/clarified intentions of the parties and in accordance with the accounting standard set in IPSAS No. 1.

3.6	We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to reclassify the above-enumerated financial transactions to their appropriate/correct account and to do the same in the succeeding transactions with reference to the requirements set forth in Sections 4.8 and 112 of P.D. No. 1445, paragraph 7 of IPSAS No. 1, and COA Circular No. 2015-009.

4.7	We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to present distinctively in the CY 2019 Statement of Financial Position the current and non-current portion of the Loans Payable-Domestic Account of P2,596,348.49 through proper reclassification thereof in the amount of P1,005,038.28 and P1,591,310.21, respectively, as required in Section 112 of P.D. No. 1445 and paragraph numbers 70 and 80 of IPSAS No. 1.

5.5	We recommended and the Management agreed to stop utilizing the LDRRMF for maintenance and operating expenses and capital outlay.  These should be charged against the general fund of the LGU.  Submit to COA the justification on the payments made amounting to P156,749.46 which shall be evaluated on whether to issue Notice of Suspension or Disallowance on the transactions as circumstances may warrant; Instruct the MDRRMO to carefully plan and include only in the LDRRMFIP the PAPs that are considered very important and urgent which need to be realized in a particular year, with due consideration of the scarce resources/allocation and to implement the same optimally; Require the MBO to prepare and furnish COA the Reports on the Receipt and Utilization of Cash Donations in the manner prescribed in Section V.A (1) of COA Circular 2014-002 and as shown in the sample form provided in Annex I thereof; Advise the MDRRMO to timely submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to COA on or before the 15th day after the end of each month as required in Item 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002; and ensure compliance to the provisions of Section 2 of R.A. No. 10121, Item 4 of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1, Item 4.3 of NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC JMC No. 2014-1, Section V.A (1) of COA Circular No. 2014-002 and Item 5.1.12 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 in the budgeting, utilization and reporting of the LDRRMF.

6.5	We recommended and the MENRO agreed to formulate and comply the regular collection of non-recyclable and special wastes in the barangays as mandated in Section 10 of R.A. No. 9003; The MENRO also agreed to include in their budget an amount for the procurement of protective equipment such as, but not limited to gloves, masks and boots to be used by the garbage collectors and to follow-up the purchase of the vehicle with the prescribed features for the collection and transportation of wastes  as prescribed in Sections 23 and 24 of the Act; The Mayor agreed, with authority from the Sangguniang Bayan, to enter into a Memorandum of Agreement with the Provincial Government of Surigao del Norte to accept their proposal to utilize the vacant lot they offered as temporary dumping site; The MENRO agreed to exert more effort in locating possible sanitary landfill sites and  to expedite the establishment of the same; and faithfully adhere to the provisions of R.A. No. 9003 particularly Sections 10, 23, 24, 37, 40 and 41 thereof on the collection and disposal of solid waste materials.

7.6	We recommended and the HRMO-designate agreed to execute a standard form contract with the Job Orders as prescribed in the CSC Resolution No. 021480 bearing the necessary information as required therein. These contracts should bear two (2) witnesses and which a copy thereof should be issued to and received by the concerned employee.

8.6	We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the GFPS to always formulate the LGU GPB based on the GAD Agenda or identified priority gender issues which were derived from the information gathered from the sources mentioned in Sections 4.1.C.2.1 and 4.1.C.1.4 of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01; The MSWDO agreed to ensure implementation of all the GAD activity or activities that will resolve GAD issues and concerns; Formulate and include only GAD PAPs that would support government’s commitment to promote equality among men and women to resources and opportunities and which specifically addressed gender issues and concerns; and strictly adhere to the provisions set forth in R.A. 9710 and PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 in the budgeting and implementation/utilization of GAD fund.

9.6	We recommended and the SB agreed that through its presiding officer, the Vice-Mayor, enact an ordinance promulgating rental fee rates to be used in the assessment by the Treasury and which to be paid by the clients to utilize the municipal gymnasium, municipal women’s Center and various chairs and tables, taking into consideration the objectives of the facilities.  The Municipal Mayor agreed to formulate operating procedures, with the assistance of the municipal treasurer and engineering office, covering the utilization of the said facilities.  These procedures must include the accomplishment by the client of an application/booking form before the properties will be used and which shall be the basis also by the treasury for the issuance of OR.  The procedures will also determine who will clean the venue before and after use, the municipal personnel to maintain the cleanliness and order of the amenities,   grievance measures to follow for any untoward incidents while using the venue and other procedures to safeguard the properties against loss and damage and to ensure full economic gain therefrom.

10.6	We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed that, with the assistance from the accounting, treasury and budget, execute a comprehensive set of guidelines/policies, as well as the MOA governing the fund transfers for operational fund to the barangays.  These guidelines must clearly provide the legal basis of the financial assistance, the objective thereof, the duties and responsibilities of the municipality with regard to the funds, the duties and responsibilities of the barangays in receiving and utilizing the funds, the penalties to incur when a party departed from the agreements, etc.  Meanwhile, the MOA must contain supplemental provisions from the guidelines/policies which are integral for the grant, utilization and reporting of the financial assistance paid; The municipal accountant agreed to follow-up and ensure that the operational funds granted to the barangays must be liquidated at the end of the year; and faithfully adhere to the provisions of Section 2 of P.D. No. 1445 and Item 3.0 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 on the grant of operational funds to the barangays.

11.5	We recommended and the property custodian agreed to initiate the construction or maintenance of a secured shelter or room where to properly store the unserviceable property and waste materials awaiting the disposal thereof; The Inventory and Disposal Committee agreed, through its Team Leader, and with the assistance of the property custodian, to spearhead the planning of the disposal procedures that the LGU will adopt in the divestment of its unserviceable property and waste materials in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 89-296 dated January 27, 1989.  Implement the divestment procedures and prepare the necessary documents for such disposal as required in the said COA Circular; Remind the Inventory and Disposal Committee and/or the property custodian that the appraisal of property can be done by the municipal engineer or by the provincial appraiser from the provincial government if the latter cannot performed the job; and strictly observe Sections 2 and 79 of P.D. No. 1445 in the utilization and disposal of LGU property.

12.6	We recommended that Management submit to COA the justification on payments without the Contract/MOA, SB Resolution and Accomplishment Reports.  The justification must be accompanied with the letter alleged made to cover the availment of the dental services.  Failure to do so or after due evaluation but unsatisfied with the legal basis/justification or the documents received, the Audit Team may cause the issuance of Notice of Suspension or Disallowance for the total amount paid as circumstances may warrant.  Strictly adhere to Sections 4(6) and 4(7) of P.D. No. 1445 in the payments of services.
13.2	We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to immediately remit the taxes withheld promptly to the BIR to avoid incurrence of penalties and charges.

15.6	We recommended and the Municipal Engineer and MDRRMO agreed to provide the facilities/amenities required to be existing in an evacuation center. These basic necessities should be addressed first to provide the needed care, service and assistance to the evacuees; The MDRRMO and the Budget Officer agreed to appropriate a portion of the 70% of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (LDRRMF) to provide additional fund for the construction of the required facilities of an evacuation center; and adhere to Section 2 (p) of R.A. No. 10121 and Section 2 of P.D. 1445 in the implementation and operation of the Evacuation Center.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor create a committee that will conduct physical count of PPE, after which, require the Municipal Treasurer to prepare the property Card for each category of assets, in compliance with Sections 119 and 124 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I, to ascertain existence and valuation of the property and establish accountability thereon.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality was able to submit their RPCPPE for CY 2019.
observations.reason: The submitted RPCPPE was not complete and was presented per accountable person who has the custody of the asset instead of per class. See Observation No. 8, Part II of this AAR.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the: a. Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their records regularly; and b. Municipal Accountant to continue validating previous years’ transactions and effect adjustments to arrive at the correct account balance.
observations.managementAction: The ending balances of the Cash accounts as of year-end of CY 2019 remain unreconciled due to various reasons, including non-preparation of Bank Reconciliation Statements and errors in recording. The Municipal Accountant continues to exert effort in the validation of previous years’ transactions.
observations.reason: The ending balances of the Cash accounts as of year-end of CY 2019 remain unreconciled due to various reasons, including non-preparation of Bank Reconciliation Statements and errors in recording. See Observation No. 1, Part II of this AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require: a. the Municipal Treasurer to liquidate/settle her cash advances in compliance with existing regulation, otherwise, we will be constrained to file appropriate audit administrative/legal action against her; b. other accountable officers to settle their cash advances by deducting the corresponding supporting details and breakdown provided by the Municipal Accountant and the General Ledger of the Advances to Officers and Employees account. Both documents must be reconciled first before the complete implementation of the recommendation; c. henceforth, see to it that all cash advances are liquidated at the end of the year for fair presentation of financial statements; and d. the Municipal Accountant to submit the Schedule of Due from Officers and Employees for further review and verification in compliance with Section 112 of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant was able to provide the Subsidiary Ledger of the said accounts.
observations.reason: Discrepancies were found during the verification of the cash advances and its related accounts which must be reconciled first before the complete implementation of the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the newly assumed Municipal Accountant to exert effort in determining the composition of the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts to facilitate reconciliation of both accounts. Also, effect the necessary adjustments in the books once reconciled. Moreover, maintain Subsidiary Ledger for the accounts in compliance with COA Circular No. 2015-009.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant was able to provide the Subsidiary Ledger of the said accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer to monitor the RPT and SET remittances for CY 2019 and to remit the same to the 16 concerned barangay treasurers within five days after the end of each quarter, in compliance with Section 271 (d) of RA No. 7160 and Section 36 of COA Circular No. 92-382.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer monitored by the Municipal Treasurer. See Observation No. 12, Part II of this AAR.
observations.reason: Verification of the Due to LGUs account disclosed various issues which casted doubt in the accuracy of the account, hence the balance was not fully remitted in CY 2019. See Observation No. 12, Part II of this AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to submit to the Audit Team the Monthly Report of Official Travels and Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption for each motor vehicle duly supported with Driver’s Trip Tickets issued during the month for review and evaluation. Henceforth, see to it that the Driver’s Trip Tickets were properly accomplished.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer communicated the requirements to submit the Monthly Reports to the Office of the Municipal Engineer and other Offices; will consolidate the reports and the Municipal Accountant will submit the Consolidated Reports to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: The Monthly Report and Driver's Trip Tickets were not properly submitted/completed; reports remain to be consolidated and submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor refrain from procuring through reimbursement to its officials and employees, ensure strict implementation of RA No. 9184 and limited, if not totally prohibited, the payment of disbursements through reimbursement.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor made a strict implementation of RA. Totally prohibited, from the payment of disbursements through reimbursement.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the former OIC-Municipal Accountant in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer to submit to the Audit Team the 28 DVs for CY 2018 and 20 DVs for CY 2017 together with their supporting documents within five working days from approval thereof.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor is committed; in CY 2019, all of the unsubmitted DVs from CY 2018 and ten from CY 2017 were submitted to the previous Audit Team.
observations.reason: Ten DVs from CY 2017 remain unsubmitted as of year-end of CY 2019. See Observation No. 14, Part II of this AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Secretariat to submit to the Audit Team the copies of the contracts and purchase orders as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2009-001 together with its supporting documents within five working days from approval thereof. Also, instruct the BAC to strictly adhere and comply with the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001 on the prompt submission of perfected contracts and its supporting documents, to ensure timely review and evaluation thereof.
observations.managementAction: The BAC Secretariat implied acceptance of the audit recommendation and assured the submission of the required documents to facilitate its timely review.
observations.reason: The Municipality was still not able to submit the purchase orders and contracts in CY 2019; some documents were later provided during post-audit.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer to: a. submit to the Supervising Auditor (SA) and Audit Team Leader (ATL) at the beginning of the year, the list of all on-going government PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year; b. inform the Auditors within ten days after the award of the project or before the start of the program/activity that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices were already posted for validation; and c. regularly submit the monthly monitoring report in compliance with the above-cited regulations.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer notified the department heads regarding the submission of their respective list of PPAs, including their PPMPs on or before July 15, 2020 and assured compliance with the audit recommendations.
observations.reason: The Municipality was not able to inform the SA and ATL within the prescribed period; monthly monitoring reports for CY 2019 remain unsubmitted. See Observation No. 19, Part II of this AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to: a. transfer the cash corresponding to the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF for CYs 2017 and 2018 to a special trust fund account and to revert the unexpended balance of P3,265,513.75 to the Unappropriated Surplus of the GF after enactment of the Sanggunian in compliance with COA Circular No. 2012-002; b. transfer the cash corresponding to the unutilized balance of the LDRRMF for CYs 2014 to 2016 to a special trust fund account in compliance with the same Circular to avoid misapplication of fund and to ensure that activities therein are accomplished.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant was able to make the necessary transfer/adjustments of the said fund.
observations.reason: The corresponding cash for the Trust Liability-LDRRMF as of year-end of CY 2019 was still not transferred as of the aforementioned cut-off date. See Observation No. 20, Part II of this AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit to the Audit Team the Monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of DRRMF on or before the 15th day after the end of each month through the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council and Local Development Council in compliance with Item No. 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality was able to submit to the Audit Team a copy of the quarterly Report.
observations.reason: The Report was submitted on a quarterly basis instead of monthly submission. See Observation No. 20, Part II of this AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality, through the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board, secure the approval of its 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan from the NSWMC and make provisions in their plan for the closure or eventual phase out of the controlled dump sites within the period specified under the framework and develop and operate a sanitary landfill site as final disposal site pursuant to the provisions under the above-cited regulations.
observations.managementAction: The MENRO explained the procedures and constraints due to COVID-19 pandemic and mentioned continuing efforts in establishing the SLF by providing allocations for procurement of the sanitary landfill site in CY 2021.
observations.reason: The Municipality is still preparing the Waste Analysis and Characterization Study (WACS) and is yet to complete acquisition of the proposed site and to comply with recommendations in the geological assessment report. See Observation No. 22, Part II of this AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Planning and Development Officer, with the assistance of the Municipal GAD Focal Point System to: a. institutionalize the GAD database with adequate and systematically gathered sex-disaggregated data which will provide the bases for gender analysis; b. undertake gender analysis to identify existing gender issues and ensure that the PPAs are responsive to the said issues and use them in the formulation of the GAD Plan and Budget; c. revisit Annex B of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 which identifies the data requirements for particular gender issues; d. include in the GAD Plan and Budget activities which pertain to the development of GAD personnel and GAD database; e. require the Sangguniang Bayan to formulate and pass a local GAD Code or ordinances with the assistance of the Municipal GAD Focal Point System.
observations.managementAction: The MPDC and MSWDO are establishing the GAD database by gathering data relevant with GAD from 16 Barangays, setting up its database, incorporating SDDs obtained during Social Amelioration Program documentation and survey; software for GAD database was established in the first quarter of CY 2020; committed to continually adhere with the provisions.
observations.reason: The Municipality is still in the inception of establishing its GAD database; however, GAD Code was enacted in CY 2019 through SB Resolution No. 44-2019. See Observation No. 21, Part II of this AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to remit all taxes withheld within the prescribed period; remit to the National Treasury the unsubstantiated previous years’ balance liability; and comply with the pertinent BIR issuances.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor committed to adhere with all the audit recommendations, most especially in gathering all relevant documents regarding the withheld taxes as they were certain there are still unrecorded remittances to the BIR; they discovered and adjusted an amount of P8,092,849.56 for unrecorded remittances. Consequently, they will remit whatever amount proved to be unremitted.
observations.reason: Reconciliation of the Due to BIR account is ongoing due to possible unrecorded remittances; remittances will be made for proven unremitted amounts. See Observation No. 23, Part II of this AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Fully Implemented that the Municipal Mayor cause the submission of the AAPSI to the Audit Team for the timely evaluation of the actions taken by Management.

We recommended Partially Implemented that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to exert effort in enforcing the immediate settlement of audit disallowances and, ensure that all employees who are retiring or transferring to other agencies shall first settle the disallowances and charges for which they are liable in accordance with Section 7 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

We recommended Fully Implemented that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to promptly turn-over documents, reports, and journals under their possession to the Municipal Accountant on the prescribed time, who shall in turn, submit the documents to the Audit Team for audit which shall be duly covered by transmittal letters.
We recommended Fully Implemented that the Legislative Body legislate an authority for the grant of communication allowance to selected employees of the LGU.

We recommended Partially Implemented that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to obtain snap shots of bank statements from the depository banks so Bank Reconciliation Statements can be prepared and effect corrections both in the book of accounts and the bank, if any, be effected. Also, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive: a. Cause the creation of a task force that will take charge in the preparation of the BRS for the nine LBP accounts setting a time-frame for its completion; b. Make written representations with the depository banks to ensure prompt submission of the Bank statements; c. Compel the Municipal Accountant to maintain an updated subsidiary ledger.

We recommended Partially Implemented that the Local Chief Executive: a. Compel the Municipal Accountant to send within ten (10) days before the expiration of the prescribed period a written reminder under signature of the LCE or his duly authorized representative enjoining officials and employees concerned to settle their cash advance; b. Direct the Municipal Accountant not to sign claims for additional cash advance until the previous cash advance is settled; c. Compel the Municipal Accountant to cause the immediate withholding of salaries and claims of officers and employees who failed to settle their cash advances within the prescribed period after being sent notice and demand; d. Require the Municipal Accountant to stop the practice of offsetting the amount due to the accountable officer (for reimbursement) against his other outstanding cash advances; e. Require the Municipal Accountant to furnish all department heads listings of all unliquidated cash advances of employees under their charge to enhance efforts to effect liquidation.

We recommended Partially Implemented that the Local Chief Executive: a. Compel the Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant a duly certified list showing the names of the taxpayers and the amounts due and collectible from them for the current and prior years; b. Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare journal entries to record the uncollected RPT and SET of the current and prior years, and henceforth, to account for the receivables annually, including discounts granted and penalties imposed; and, c. Direct the Municipal Assessor to clean up the Assessment Role to establish a realistic and accurate RPT and SET Receivable.

We recommended Partially Implemented for the Local Chief Executive direct the Supply Officer to require all drivers of official vehicles of the Municipality to prepare and submit duly accomplished and serially numbered Driver’s Trip Tickets for each trip to the Supply Officer who in turn will prepare the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption for submission to COA.

We recommended Partially Implemented that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Engineer to facilitate the immediate completion of the construction of the Municipal Bus Terminal with Market Stalls and ensure that the operation of the water system and bus terminal be fast-tracked so that the projects will be operational and be able to generate sufficient revenues to pay their loan amortizations and related costs.

We recommended Partially Implemented that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Budget Officer to ensure that only projects aligned with the purpose/objectives of the 20% Development Fund be funded from such appropriation and that the Municipal Accountant should see to it that expenses charged against the 20% DF are in accordance with prescribed guidelines.

We recommended Partially Implemented that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council to prepare a risk reduction and management plan in accordance with the NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 dated March 25, 2013; compel the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council to formulate and implement a comprehensive and integrated Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Plan; and request the Sangguniang Bayan to issue a resolution to reprogram items in the Annual Budget and to prepare a revised Annual Investment Plan.

We recommended Partially Implemented that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer and other concerned to fast-track implementation of priority programs funded by the 20% DF, BUB and LGSF to provide beneficiaries the intended benefits.

We recommended Fully Implemented that the Local Chief Executive require the officers and employees to refund the amount collected in the absence of legal basis and desist from granting such benefits unless in pursuance of a lawful appropriation and/or other statutory authority.

We recommended Partially Implemented that the Local Chief Executive: 1. Require the Municipal Accountant to make sure that GSIS premium/ contribution remittances and loan amortizations are properly reconciled and remitted; 2. Require the Municipal Treasurer to remit withheld amounts to GSIS in full within 10 days following the end of the month of withholding; 3. Require reconciliation with GSIS records.

We recommended Partially Implemented that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer to identify the employees with unremitted contributions to enable the Government to settle its obligations and that the Municipal Accountant regularly reconcile amounts withheld and remitted to Pag-IBIG.

We recommended Partially Implemented that the Local Chief Executive through the GAD Council prioritize the creation of a GAD database to serve as basis for the creation of GAD database for inputs to planning, programming and policy formulation.

We recommended Partially Implemented that the Municipal Mayor through the SWM Focal Person consider appropriate means of communication to inform the public, implement feasible projects, and provide necessary financial and technical support to barangays to establish and operate Materials Recovery Facilities.

We recommended Partially Implemented that the LCE cause the creation of an Inventory Committee and instruct them to commence the conduct of the physical inventory of all LGU properties and require the Municipal Accountant to prepare PPE Ledger Cards (PPELC) for each class of PPE listing acquisition, description, custody, estimated life, depreciation, disposal and other information.
