We recommended Partially Implemented that the Municipal Mayor and concerned officials fast-track implementation of projects funded under the 20% DF to provide constituents the intended benefits.

We recommended Partially Implemented that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the corresponding disbursement vouchers together with the related supporting documents and ensure reports submitted are complete and intact.

We recommended Fully Implemented that the Municipal Vice Mayor exercise prudence in the usage of government funds and make representations that provincial seminars be conducted in the province to minimize costs and send participants only to seminars relevant to their functions.

We recommended Fully Implemented that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to submit accounts regularly on every 10th day of the succeeding month and all accountable officers to turn over documents to the accounting division within five (5) days after the end of each month.

We recommended Fully Implemented that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to ensure expenditure charged to the 20% Economic Development Fund are in accordance with prescribed guidelines and adjust the budget to attain intended objectives.

We reiterated previous recommendations for strict compliance to Sections 74 and 111 of PD 1445 and creation of a task force to prepare BRS for LBP accounts; make written representations with depository banks; maintain updated subsidiary ledgers; and reconcile negative balances and effect adjustments.

We recommended Partially Implemented that management require contractors to rectify deficiencies and that municipal officials ensure projects are inspected and completed according to plans and that procurement procedures under RA 9184 be complied with before processing claims.

We recommended Partially Implemented that the Local Chief Executive discontinue use of open dump sites and enjoin the Sangguniang Bayan to pass ordinances for 'No Open Burning' and 'No Littering' policies.

We recommended various remedial actions as indicated in prior recommendations including strengthening controls, speeding procurement and implementation, reconciling accounts, establishing MRFs and recovering unremitted obligations.

We recommended that Management instruct collectors to remit their collection within the prescribed period and require them to strictly adhere to the provisions mentioned in the foregoing to prevent misappropriation or incurrence of loss of government funds.
observations.managementAction: Most of the collectors have remitted the collection on time. New schemes will be promulgated by the management to ensure the strict observance of prompt remittance of collections.
observations.reason: There are still collectors with delayed remittances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management: a. Issue a demand letter to all accountable officers for the immediate liquidation/ settlement of their unliquidated cash advances and in case of non-compliance, withhold from their salaries the total amount due for those who are still active and find any legal remedies to compel the liquidation of cash advances for those who are not already connected with the LGU; b. Ensure that all cash advances are fully liquidated as soon as the purpose of the cash advance has been served; c. Make necessary actions for the proper reporting of dormant accounts by submitting request for write-off of dormant accounts and its underlying supporting documents to prove that the accounts are no longer collectible.
observations.managementAction: The management will continue to send demand letters to officers with balances.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management exert extra effort by making follow-ups and continue sending demand letters. Further, find any legal remedies and/or strategies to compel them for the immediate liquidation for the funds transferred, if necessary. It is also recommended not to download another fund to barangays unless previous funds transfer were fully liquidated. In addition, verify and monitor the implementation of projects funded out of the transferred funds especially for long outstanding downloaded funds and demand return of the fund if found not utilized or used.
observations.managementAction: Funds will not be downloaded to BLGUs with balances. Demand letters will be sent for unliquidated balances.
observations.reason: Substantial decrease. Reiterated in this report for funds downloaded that remained unliquidated ranging 1-5 years.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management initiate action for the liquidation of the outstanding fund transfer by sending demand letters and making follow ups to all NGOs/POs concerned and demanding return of the fund if found not utilized or has been used not in accordance with Memorandum of Agreement and follow strictly Section 5.4 of COA Circular No. 2007-001 on the granting of fund transfer to NGOs/POs.
observations.managementAction: Demand letters will be sent to the responsible NGOs/POs.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the officials concerned to prepare a physical inventory report of Biological Assets and submit to the accounting office for reconciliation. Confirm the existence thereof and effect necessary adjustments in the books, if warranted.
observations.managementAction: Deeds of Donation were already executed with the Donees for the said Biological Assets.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management stop the practice of engaging consultancy services without conducting public bidding and/or undergo the regular procurement process as provided under Section 10 of RIRR of RA 9184. It is also recommended to submit the approved consultancy contract together with the supporting documents for the determination of the reasonableness of the consultancy cost.
observations.managementAction: Required documents were submitted by the management. The management will refrain from engaging consultancy services without conducting public bidding and/or undergo the regular procurement process as provided under Section 10 of RIRR of RA 9184.
observations.reason: Payment of consultancy was stop immediatly after the exit conference in February 2019

observations.recommendation: We recommended that henceforth, any payment of claims for catering services should be supported with activity’s documentation and menu list to establish the validity and regularity of transactions.
observations.managementAction: All disbursements involving catering services are supported with proper documentation on such as photo and the menu list.
observations.reason: Claims for catering services were not supported with Inspection and Acceptance Report and Minutes of Meeting/ Narrative Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management instruct the BAC and its Secretariat: 1. To include the copy of the posting of the NOA and NTP in the PhilGEPS when they submit contracts and bidding documents; 2. To require all winning bidders to submit construction safety and health program duly approved by the DOLE; 3. To intensify the conduct of post qualification so that only qualified contractors will be awarded with contracts; and 4. To strictly adhere with pertinent provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: The BAC will observe to include the documents stated in recommendation no. 1 & 2, and to increase the effort for post qualification so that only qualified contractors will be awarded with contracts; and to strictly adhere with pertinent provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184.
observations.reason: There are still deficiencies noted in its procurement activities.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: 1. Stop charging ineligible expenses to 5% LDRRMF; 2. Allocate additional funds for regular operation expenses of the LDRRM Office; 3. Instruct the MDRRMO to consider the timing in the purchase of perishable goods; and 4. Follow strictly the regulation in the utilization of the 5% Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund as provided under Section 5.1 of the Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 of DBM and DILG dated March 25, 2013. It is also recommended that purchases of goods and supplies in bulk should be recorded to its proper inventory account upon purchase and eventually recorded to its proper expense account upon consummation or distribution. Further, accountable officer is also required to prepare a Monthly Report of Supplies and Materials Issued (MRSMI) and submit the said report, together with the copy of distribution list properly received by the beneficiaries, to the Accounting Office for recording purposes in the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: The MDRRMO will refrain from charging ineligible expenses to 5% LDRRMF and as much as possible adhere to the Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 of DBM and DILG. The MDRRMO also considers timing in the purchase of goods and non-food items. Prepositioning of commodities is also considered to somehow address other emergency situations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, as the Chair of Municipal Solid Waste Management Board shall provide for the schemes for volume reduction of solid waste by requesting the Sangguniang Bayan to pass an ordinance in refraining the use of non-environmentally accepted packaging for all commercial establishments within the locality after proper consultation and hearing with the stakeholders or with the sectors concerned.
observations.managementAction: The management will continue to recommend to the Office of the Sangguniang Bayan the passage of an ordinance that will minimize the carbon footprint specially from plastics. In addition, the management will instill in the Barangay management to adhere to the mandate of RA 9003.
observations.reason: Construction of MRFs were reiterated in this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan and the Local Finance Committee revisit the operations of the said economic enterprise and ascertain the viability and sustainability of the economic enterprise by finding alternative ways on how to generate more income to cover the funding requirement for the year.
observations.managementAction: The management will devise policies in minimizing the cost in operating the local economic enterprises. The management together with the Sangguniang Bayan will also assess the viability of the said enterprises whether such are self-sustainable.
observations.reason: Out of 4 economic enterprises that incur losses in CY2018, 3 economic enterprises continue to incur losses in CY2019; thus the need to reiterate in this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct those involved in planning and implementation to coordinate and monitor closely and hasten the implementation of the projects and optimize the utilization of 20 percent Development Fund through a) proper planning of project including timing of execution; b) coordinated action from LGU officials for better result; and c) strict monitoring in project implementation to ensure accomplishment of the project by setting time frames and target dates so that the constituents could avail of the benefits of the program.
observations.managementAction: The management will exert more effort in order to hasten the implementation of projects under the 20% MDF and to submit the required contract documents on time.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the implementing office to fast track the implementation of the projects so that targeted beneficiaries can enjoy the intended benefits. Further, instruct the BAC and its secretariat to set time frames in its procurement process so that the implementing office can start their implementation early. Also, furnish the Audit Team of the certified copies of suspension orders and its supporting documents for its immediate determination if the grounds for suspension is reasonable or not.
observations.managementAction: The management will employ mechanisms to decrease or avoid the delay in the procurement process so that the implementation of funds from NGAs will be hastened. Further, instruct the BAC and its secretariat to set time frames in its procurement process so that the implementing office can start also their implementation as early as possible. Also, furnish the Audit Team certified copies of suspension orders and its supporting documents for its immediate determination if the grounds for suspension is reasonable or not.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management maximize the utilization of LDRRMF for disaster preparedness, prevention and mitigation pursuant to DILG DBM Joint Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013. It is also recommended that Management prioritize the implementation of infrastructure projects which can minimize the risk of flood during heavy rains and/or can lessen the damage dealt by an occurring calamity.
observations.managementAction: The management will prioritize the implementation of infrastructure projects and hasten the utilization of such fund.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this report.

observations.recommendation: Instruct the members of the IDC to submit to the Audit Team immediately and without incurring more delays the RPCI and RPCPPE pursuant to Section 124 of NGAS Manual for LGU. The Committee shall also provide copies thereof to the Supply Officer and the Accountant for record purposes; advise the Municipal Accountant to maintain Supplies, PPEs and other properties Ledger Cards. It is very elementary that ALL the accounts in the financial statements is supported with ledgers. Reconcile the accounting records with the reports being kept by the Supply Officer and/or with the IDC at least quarterly; direct the Storekeeper to maintain ALL/completely the issued ARE. These will be used to verify PPEs being issued to the end users; and adhere to Sections 119, 120 and 124 of the NGAS Manual, Volume 1 and other pertinent laws, rules and regulations governing the conduct of physical count of properties, maintenance of records and submission of reports thereof to the Auditor.
observations.managementAction: Comply with the audit recommendations
observations.reason:
Not implemented
All the audit recommendations remained not implemented as of validation date.

observations.recommendation: Convene with the SMWD officers together with the respective resident Auditors. Clarify the intention of both parties as regards the transfer of the waterworks/sewerage facilities including the water rights of the LGU’s Water System to the MSWD in exchange of the latter’s payment of P5,000,000.00. Amend the provisions in the project study and the MOA that are found conflicting with the established intentions. Ensure that both parties should have a uniform interpretation of the provision/ stipulation stated in the project study and MOA. Adjust the PPE account of the LGU based on the established/clarified intentions of the parties and in accordance with the accounting standard set in PPSAS No. 1.
observations.managementAction: Comply with audit recommendations
observations.reason:
Not implemented
The Audit Team still did not observe compliance by the Management to the audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation:
Direct the MDRRMO to allocate 30% QRF separate and distinct from the 70% allocation in the 5% LDRRM; stop utilizing the LDRRMF for administrative expenses like establishment & maintenance of MDRRM/ CCA Office. Submit to COA the justification on the payments made which shall be evaluated on whether to issue Notice of Suspension or Disallowance on the transactions a circumstances may warrant; instruct the MDRRMO to carefully plan and include only in the LDRRMFIP the PAPs that are considered very important and urgent which need to be realized in a particular year. Follow-up/coordinate with the PPDO and PDRRMO from time to time with regard to the CBMS and the Dredging of Silted Rivers projects which implementation of the same by the LGU were
dependent with the Province; remind the Budget Officer to furnish COA the Reports on the Receipt and Utilization of Cash Donations; advise the MDRRMO to prepare and submit monthly reports on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF to COA in a manner prescribed in Item 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002. The MDRRMO may request from the Mayor manpower compliment to assist him in the MDRRM works; and ensure compliance to the provisions of Section 2 of R.A. No. 10121, Section 4.3 of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG-CSC JMC No. 2014-1, Item 4 of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1, Section V.A (1) of COA Circular No. 2014-002 and Item 5.1.16 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 on the utilization and reporting of the LDRRMF.
observations.managementAction: Comply the audit recommendations
observations.reason:
Partially implemented
The MDRRMO had already factored to modify the CY 2019 LDRRMF budget for the distinct allocation of the 30% and 70% separate from each other.

observations.recommendation: Management enjoin the Local School Board (LSB) to implement all the programs, projects and activities (PAPs) embodied in the LSB annual budget. The inclusion of the PAPs in the budget was because these were already considered by the board as important, urgent, and therefore priority and adhere to the declared policy of the State mentioned in R.A. No. 5447 and Section 2 of P.D. No. 1445 in the utilization of SEF.
observations.managementAction: Implement the SEF PAPs
observations.reason:
Partially implemented
The LSB alleged that there were some SEF PAPs that had already been implemented in CY 2019.  However, there was no documents submitted to COA to date as proof of the implementation.

observations.recommendation: Advise the MSWDO to ensure implementation of all the LCPC PPAs so that the fund is optimally utilized; remind the MBO to correctly charge/obligate every financial transaction against its corresponding appropriation; follow-up with the DOLE on the status of the implementation of the project Construction of the Social Protection Center in the municipality to establish whether there is still a need to appropriation an amount from the LCPC fund as an LGU equity thereof; send a letter-inquiry to the City Government of Surigao on the amount to be paid for availing the services of the Bahay Pag-asa. This may avoid penalties and additional charges that may incur for non-payment or late settlement of accounts; and adhere faithfully to Section 2 of P.D. No. 1445 and Section 2 of R.A. No. 9344 in the utilization of LCPC fund or implementation of its PAPs.
observations.managementAction: Comply the audit recommendations
observations.reason:
Partially implemented
The MBO remarked that his office is now undertaking extra steps to charge particular expenses to the corresponding appropriation.  Other audit recommendations remained not implemented.

observations.recommendation: Require the BAC, including the BAC Secretariat and the TWG, to submit to the Audit Team the lacking documents and to explain to the latter the presence of procurement deficiencies/ irregularities. Non-compliance hereof may cause the suspension of the transactions and adhere strictly with the provisions of R.A. 9184 and its IRR, COA Circular Nos. 2009-001 and 2012-001 on the procurement of goods, infrastructures and services.
observations.managementAction: Submit to COA the lacking documents and the explanation of the presence of procurement deficiencies
observations.reason:
Not implemented
The BAC still did not submit to COA the lacking bidding documents and the justification of the presence of procurement deficiencies.

observations.recommendation: Direct the DTI-BuB Focal Person to supervise and/or implement the project Nito Production in accordance with the approved plan and its objectives as provided in the Item 2 of the MOA; instruct the Organization Treasurer, through the DTI-BuB Focal Person, to diligently prepare and maintain correct, proper and complete financial records and statements to show the financial operations and condition of the project. Register with the BIR so that OR can be used by the organization. Prepare vouchers too or any document showing an authorized payment so that the utilization of the fund is controlled. The DTI-BuB Focal Person is enjoined to submit to COA the CYs 2016-2018 financial statements/ reports of the project; advise the organization, through the DTI-BuB Focal Person, to set aside fund for the construction of a production area. This will house the weaving machineries, utensils and other equipment which shall be procured by the LGU as mentioned in the MOA. Create also a production timeline/schedule; and instruct the DTI-BuB Focal Person and other concerned personnel to regularly monitor the condition and performance of the project as well as the other PAPs under DTI. Monitoring includes ocular inspection, confirming compliance to the provisions in the MOA. These ensure immediate action to the rising problems which will result to the achievement of the project’s primary objective as well as guarantee profitability and sustainability of the same.
observations.managementAction: Comply with audit recommendations
observations.reason:
Partially implemented
The LGU had initiated a dialogue with the concerned parties but the result thereof has yet to be communicated to COA. The project has started to operate normally.  Other audit recommendations remained not implemented.

observations.recommendation: Direct the DTI-BuB Focal Person to supervise and/or implement the project Establishment of Bigasan ng Barangay in accordance with the approved plan and its objectives as provided in the Item 2 of the MOA; instruct the Organization Treasurer, through the DTI-BuB Focal Person, to diligently prepare and maintain correct, proper and complete financial records and statements to show the financial operations and condition of the project; register the business with the BIR so that OR can be used by the organization. Prepare vouchers too or any document showing an authorized payment so that the utilization of the fund is controlled. The DTI-BuB Focal Person is enjoined to submit to COA the CYs 2016-2018 financial statements/ reports of the project; advise the organization, through the DTI-BuB Focal Person, to refrain from selling the grain rice on credit. Revenue-wise, these commodities shall be sold on a per kilo basis; direct the association, through the DTI-BuB Focal Person to utilize the supplies and equipment as intended. The group must appoint among its members a capable and dependable individual to man the project; and instruct the DTI-BuB Focal Person and other concerned personnel to regularly monitor the condition and performance of the project as well as the other PAPs under DTI. These ensure immediate action to the rising problems which will result to the achievement of the project’s primary objective as well as guarantee profitability and sustainability of the same.
observations.managementAction: Comply with audit recommendations
observations.reason:
Partially implemented
The LGU had initiated a dialogue with the concerned parties but the result thereof has yet to be communicated to COA. These concerned parties had started to rectify the noted deficiencies. Other audit recommendations remained not complied.

observations.recommendation:
Advise the association, through the DTI-BuB Focal Person, to utilize the amount of P32,362.00 to purchase the starter kit. These equipment and supplies will be used to produce more outputs, even the absence of a production room and to utilize only, for the meantime, the available spaces in the house of each member of the group. More productions will give more income to the group. This income will eventually be utilized to complete the building which will serve as the production area and display center; instruct the Organization Treasurer, through the DTI-BuB Focal Person, to prepare and maintain financial records and statements to show the financial operations and condition of the project. Register with the BIR so that OR can be used by the organization. The ORs can be utilized as control to properly and completely monitor the receipt of funds by the association. Prepare vouchers too or any document showing an authorized payment so that the utilization of the fund is controlled. Create also a production timeline/ schedule. This schedule will
provide the number of products to make in a given period, how many members needed to produce the products and who will produce thereof vis-à-vis the target sales of the same period. The presence of the production timeline will ensure synchronized, uniform and quality outputs; and instruct the DTI-BuB Focal Person and other concerned personnel to regularly monitor the condition and performance of the project as well as the other PAPs under DTI. Monitoring includes ocular inspection, confirming compliance to the provisions in the MOA. These ensure immediate action to the problems which will result to the achievement of the project’s primary objective as well as guarantee profitability and sustainability of the same.
observations.managementAction: Comply with audit recommendations
observations.reason:
Partially implemented
The LGU had initiated a dialogue with the concerned parties but the result thereof has yet to be communicated to COA. These concerned parties had started to rectify the noted deficiencies. Other audit recommendations remained not complied.

observations.recommendation: Revisit the MOA and the business plan of the project. Together with the officers and members of the organization, reevaluate whether the project, with the present circumstances, is totally not feasible to operate. Identify the presence of remedial actions or possible solutions to the identified problems like planting a variety of coconut trees suitable for sap production only. They can ask opinion/idea from the Philippine Coconut Authority on this matter. Requesting reasonable rate from the coconut farmers to get saps from tall coconut trees could be other alternative. These coconut farmers should be explained that the benefits of the project would redound also to their end in the future; initiate a meeting together with the officers of the organization with the DOLE, if the foregoing recommendation is not viable. Discuss in the meeting the possible business alternatives which are feasible in the locality considering its available resources, a business that will fit with the capability of the people operating the same; suggest to the officers of the organization to assess carefully and make a feasibility study whether the bakery business would indeed sustainable in the area. If positive, discuss the matter with the DOLE for the change of project. Execute a MOA to cover the new venture; examine and evaluate every PAPs offered by the national government agencies for the LGU’s implementation. Primary consideration should be made with the resources/raw materials needed, labor and market and whether these indeed will benefit the people. Consult the end-users concerning thereof; and instruct the Project Focal Person to regularly monitor the condition and performance of the project as well as the other PAPs under DOLE. Monitoring includes ocular inspection, confirming compliance to the provisions in the business plan and agreements. These ensure immediate action to the rising problems which will result to the achievement of the project objectives as well as guarantee profitability and sustainability of the same.
observations.managementAction: Comply with audit recommendations
observations.reason:
Partially implemented
The LGU had initiated a dialogue with the concerned parties but the result thereof has yet to be communicated to COA. Other audit recommendations remained not complied.

observations.recommendation: Create a GSO plantilla position and hire a person to fill therein subject to the financial capacity of the LGU.  If not feasible, designate a GSO in accordance with the provisions of Article 20 of R.A. 7160; instruct the inventory committee/team to spearhead, plan and conduct physical count of the Municipality’s supplies and properties and to prepare and submit to the Audit Team Report on Physical Count of PPE and other properties pursuant to Section 124 of NGAS Manual for LGU; advise the Municipal Accountant to maintain Supplies, PPEs and other properties Ledger Cards; direct the Storekeeper to maintain Supplies and Property Cards per category of materials, PPEs and other properties to reconcile balances thereof with the accounting records regularly; and adhere to Sections 119, 120 and 124 of the NGAS Manual, Volume 1 and other pertinent laws, rules and regulations governing the conduct of physical count of properties, maintenance of records and submission of reports thereof to the Auditor.
observations.managementAction: Create a plantilla position for GSO or designate one and conduct inventory
observations.reason:
Partially implemented
The LCE had already conducted a meeting with the department heads on the possibility of hiring a GSO or designating one.  No concrete compliance to the audit recommendations had been reached so far.

observations.recommendation: Convene with the SMWD officers and revisit the MOA.  Clarify with the SMWD whether the intent of the transaction was indeed a contract of sale.  If in affirmative, the LGU should drop from the books the Water System as part of the PPE without prejudice to the issuance of NS, ND or NC by this Office due to violation by the LGU of the provisions enshrined in COA Circular No. 89-296.  Amend the provisions of the MOA if necessary to conform with the true/correct intention of both parties.  Ensure that the parties should have a uniform interpretation of the provision/stipulation stated in the MOA; and adhere to the provisions and regulations governing disposal/divestment of government properties including its recording in the books.
observations.managementAction: Comply the audit recommendations
observations.reason:
Partially implemented
The Management had already initiated to talk with the SMWD to clear up provisions in the MOA and other documents.  Other audit recommendations were not complied to date.

observations.recommendation: Secure a copy of the MOA from source Agencies to cover each of the financial assistance received; make a representation before the source Agency that every financial assistance granted to the LGU should be covered with a MOA; ensure that financial assistance received must be credited to  Liability/Payable account instead of Income account to maintain accountability; and faithfully adhere to the provisions of Section 2 of P.D. No. 1445 and Item 3.0 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 in the receipt of financial assistance/aids from various sources.
observations.managementAction: Secure MOA with the source agencies
observations.reason:
Partially implemented
The LGU had initially contacted the source agencies signifying the former’s intention of forging a MOA for the financial assistance received from the latter.  There was no MOA submitted to COA to date.

observations.recommendation: Encourage every barangay or cluster of barangays through various information drives to establish an MRF where sorting, segregation, composting and recycling of biodegradable, compostable and reusable waste materials will take place as mandated in Section 10 of R.A. No. 9003; fast tract the negotiations with the authorized public official of the Government of Surigao City regarding the co-utilization of its sanitary land fill as temporary dumping site of the Municipality’s solid wastes; advise the Municipal Ecological Solid Waste Management Board (MESWMB) to formulate as soon as possible alternative solutions for disposal of non-recyclable and special wastes in accordance with the provisions of R.A. No. 9003; ensure compliance to Section 10 of the R.A. No. 9003 where the LGU will collect non-recyclable materials and special wastes from the barangays and other sources to be disposed in the designated dump sites; and faithfully adhere to the provisions of R.A. No. 9003 particularly Sections 10, 32 and 33 thereof on the segregation, collection, transportation and disposal of solid waste materials.
observations.managementAction: Comply the audit recommendations
observations.reason:
Partially implemented
The MENRO formulated integral policies in in relation to the provisions of RA 9003.  The LGU had also initially informed several barangays on the establishment of MRF.  Other audit recommendations remained unimplemented.

observations.recommendation: Appropriate an amount for GAD PAPs at least 5% of the total annual budget and not based from the regular income of the LGU; establish the LGU GFPS with the inclusion of the Executive Committee and the TWG and/or Secretariat as required in Section 4.1.A.1 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01.  This should be submitted to COA without delay; direct the GFPS to always formulate the LGU GPB based on the GAD Agenda or identified priority gender issues which were derived from the information gathered from the sources mentioned in Sections 4.1.C.2.1 and 4.1.C.1.4 of JMC No. 2013-01; submit to the COA Audit Team a copy of the GPB and AR in a manner prescribed in Item V of COA Circular No. 2014-001; and strictly adhere to the governing provisions set forth in the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 and COA Circular No. 2014-001 in the creation, utilization and reporting of GAD fund.
observations.managementAction: Appropriate GAD fund at least 5% of the appropriation.  Submit to COA the EO establishing the GFPS, formulate the LGU GPB based on the GAD Agenda or identified priority gender issues
observations.reason:
Partially implemented
The MBO had already considered in the CY 2019 municipal appropriation the GAD budget at least 5% of the annual budget.  Other audit recommendations remained uncomplied.

observations.recommendation: Revisit the provisions of the project.  Discuss with the DA on how the problem be solved with due consideration of the fact that, to date, there had been no suppliers found for the native chicken in which the procurement thereof would not violate the provisions of the procurement law.  However, if the project will not materialize, what other alternative courses of action be done so that the fund allocated thereof could still be used optimally.   Make a suggestion to the DA that projects for implementation in partnership with the Municipality should run through proper discussion and consultation.  This way, the need of the people will be rightfully provided/answered.  Thus, the fund intended thereof will be effectively utilized; faithfully adhere to the provisions of Section 2 of P.D. No. 1445 in the implementation of the LGU’s various projects, programs and activities with a view that the resources of the government must be safeguarded against loss or wastage; and submit to COA the MOA covering the implementation of the project.
observations.managementAction: Implement the project
observations.reason:
Partially implemented
The LGU had already taken steps like travelling to far places to look for possible source of the native chicken.  Nothing integral development had been reported to COA so far.

observations.recommendation: Advise the barangay officials and civil society organization representatives, if there are any, that in identifying priority projects in the unit to be funded from whatever source, select the projects that are necessary, doable and readily implementable. Non-compliance hereof would result to wastage and possible loss of government resources; remind the Local Poverty Reduction Action Team (LPRAT) that before submitting to the DILG RO the list of priority projects for funding, that these should be reviewed carefully whether the same are necessary, doable and readily implementable; ensure compliance to the requirements set forth in Item 5.8 of DBM-DILG-DSWD-NAPC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 7 dated November 3, 2015 in case there is revision/changes in the project; and suggest to the LPRAT and the barangay officials of Barangay Oslao that if possible, to find an amount to be used in the opening and development of the road planned to be concreted so that the latter activity/project will commence as soon as possible.
observations.managementAction: Realize the project
observations.reason:
Partially implemented
The Municipal Engineer had verbally and initially talked with the Oslao barangay officials to find remedy of the problem.  The LGU had also consulted the DILG on the right thing to do with regard to the project which is road concreting pending its road opening since the former cannot be implemented until the latter will be done. The road concreting had not been implemented as of to date.

observations.recommendation: Refrain from disbursing funds without corresponding appropriation.  Submit to COA the legal basis on the procurement of land in the amount of P700,000.00 without corresponding budget; stop the payments of claims without complete supporting documents.  Submit to COA a certified photocopy of the Transfer Certificate of Title (TCT) and Tax Declaration (TD) in the name of the procuring entity or previous owner with annotation of sale; coordinate with the previous land owner for the immediate transfer of the certificate of title in the name of the LGU, if such document has not been made/executed yet; revisit the provisions of the MOA or the project study, if there is any.  Implement the project without delay in accordance with the provisions thereof.  If the conduct of a meeting with the representatives of the NHA is warranted to commence the activity, do so immediately; submit to COA the MOA or the project study covering the implementation of the project; enjoin the MPDC to monitor the timely implementation of the various projects of the LGU; and adhere to Sections 2, 4(1) and 4(6) of P.D. No. 1445, Item 2 of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 and Item 13.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 in the implementation of projects which involves procurement of land.
observations.managementAction: Submit copies of the TCT and TD to COA
observations.reason:
Partially implemented
The Mayor had already talked with the SB Secretary, suggesting their end to invite the owner of the land to one of their sessions and request from him to surrender the title of the land.  Other audit recommendations are still uncomplied.  No additional development had been made to date.

observations.recommendation:
1.9 We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal
Accountant to strictly comply with the provisions of Section 74 of PD No.
1445 and regularly prepare the BRS to reconcile the cash in bank
balances between accounting and the bank records and make the
necessary adjustments where appropriate. For the bank reconciling items,
make representations with the concerned bank to effect the necessary
adjustments.

2.8 We further recommended that members of the Inventory Committee shall
undergo a training on Property and Supply Management System (PSMS),
to gain adequate understanding and working knowledge of the systems,
processes, policies and other regulations pertaining to property and
supply management.

• to send within ten (10) days before the expiration of the prescribed
period a written reminder under signature of the Mayor or his duly
authorized representative enjoining officials and employees concerned
to settle their cash advance;
• not to sign claims for additional cash advance until the previous cash
advance is settled;
• to cause the immediate withholding of salaries and claims of officers
and employees who failed to settle their cash advances within the
prescribed period after being sent notice and demand;
• to furnish all department heads listings of all unliquidated cash
advances of employees under their charge to enhance efforts to effect
liquidation.

4.8 We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal
Accountant to reclassify the recording of the share of income from the
diving site tourism to the Trust Fund-- such type of income does not come
from the regular source of the LGU, and further install the objectives for
which the Trust Fund was opened.

5.8 We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal
Treasurer to look further into the borrowing capacity requirements and
consider the effect of the remaining term of the incumbent LGU officials,
as well as the economic life of the projects funded under the loan
agreement to effectively manage the loan/borrowing entered into by the
LGU.

6.6 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal
Accountant to process the request for write-off of the aforementioned
accounts in accordance with COA Circular No, 2016-005 supported with
an aging of dormant receivables and fund transfers, indicating in the
remarks column the existence of the applicable conditions, as follows:
a. Absence of records or documents to validate/support the claim and/or
unreconciled reciprocal accounts;
b. Death of the accountable officer/employee/debtor;
c. Unknown whereabouts of the accountable officer/employee/debtor,
and that he/she could not be located despite diligent efforts to find
him/her;
d. Exhaustion of all possible remedies by the Management to collect the
receivables and to demand liquidation of cash advances and fund
transfers; and,
e. No pending case in court involving the subject dormant accounts.

7.8 We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal
Engineer and all municipal officials concerned to fast-tract the
implementation of the projects under the 20% Development Fund.

10.7 We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal
Treasurer take action on the imposed updates in the assessment of Local
Business Tax (LBT), Registration and Renewal of Business Permits and
Licenses, and the imposition of Local Taxes, Fees and Charges in order
to increase the collection of revenues and taxes of the municipality.

12.12 We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the GAD FPS to
adhere to the provisions of JMC Circular Nos. 2013-01 and 2016-01 in the
application of cost attribution of the annual expenditure to the annual
GADPB, in order to address efficiently and effectively the streamlined
gender issues of the locality.

13.6 We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal
Accountant to adapt monitoring mechanism to detect variances in the
amount to be remitted and withheld. Furthermore, we also recommend
that accounting records such as cash receipts journal, check
disbursement journal, cash disbursement journal, general and subsidiary
ledgers must be updated monthly in order to determine the exact amount
to be remitted to the Bureau of Internal Revenue.

14.7 We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal
Accountant to confirm with the GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth the
veracity of the balances and properly disclose in the Notes to Financial
Statements why these balances were long outstanding in the books.
Further, he should adapt monitoring mechanism to detect variances

15.7 We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal
Accountant to use the prescribed account names in recording completed
and partially paid infrastructure projects in consonance with the
provisions of Section 50 of the NGAS for LGUs by effecting the Adjusting
Journal Entries (AJE) for the correct account names of the partially and
fully paid infrastructure projects.

16.7 We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal
Assessor to facilitate the titling of untitled lots.

17.16 We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Health
Officer to apply for a license to operate from the DOH as required.

18.6 We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the officials
concerned to discontinue the subscription with the current internet service
provider and consider other available/responsive provider that can deliver
better connectivity for the key offices of the LGU.

19.7 We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to request the Sangguniang
Bayan to enact an appropriation ordinance authorizing the grant of
communication allowance to officials.

20.6 We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the officials
concerned to secure an SB resolution authorizing the use of lump sum
appropriation for the inaugural rites.

21.10 We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to fully institutionalize the
LDRRMO by filling up the statutory/mandatory positions required under
NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2014-
1 to assure implementation of actions and measures related to disaster
risk reduction and management.

22.11 We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal
Accountant cause the submission of the AAPSI to the Audit Team for the
timely evaluation of action taken by Management.

23.15 We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal
Accountant to send demand letters to those employees who have not yet
settled their disallowances, and strictly monitor the settlement of
suspension and disallowances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that tax collection efforts be intensified. Tax information campaign and rewards should be explored and pursued to encourage payment. Publication of delinquent properties and sending billing notices to defaulting taxpayers should be conducted.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure the establishment and operationalization of functional and standard-based MRFs in all barangays and provide support and wide dissemination of information on source reduction, recycling and composting programs in conformance with the IRR of RA 9003.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that tax collection efforts be intensified, remedies such as auction, levy and distraint of personal property be carried out to recover the tax delinquency, and that publication of delinquent properties and sending billing notices to defaulting taxpayers be conducted.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive exercise prudence in the usage of government funds: 1. Make representation with the PCL officials and request that seminars/trainings where participants come from the province should be conducted in the province in order to minimize cost; 2. Send participants only to seminars with agency that is in their area of concern.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to submit the accounts of the municipality regularly on every 10th day of the succeeding month. Likewise all accountable officers are to turn-over to the accounting division for recording purposes reports as well as source documents within five (5) days after the end of each month.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Budget Officer (MBO) to provide the necessary appropriation representing one percent (1%) of the IRA for programs, projects and activities for the protection of children.
