observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive, as the Chair of Municipal Solid Waste Management Board shall operate a sanitary landfill as its final disposal of wastes and to appropriate fund for the program as stipulated in Section 17 of RA 9003. Further, segregation of wastes at source must be strongly implemented and the collection thereof must be properly handled in consonance with Article 2 and 3 of RA 9003. The collection of fees and penalties shall be properly imposed to generate adequate revenue and to cover the costs of implementing solid waste management.
observations.managementAction: The Management has already found its prospect area for the establishment of sanitary landfill.
observations.reason: The LGU still do not operate a sanitary landfill.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor create a committee that
will conduct physical count of PPEs, after-which, require the Treasurer to prepare
the Property Card for each category of assets, in compliance with Sections 119 and
124 of the MNGAS for LGU, Volume 1, to ascertain existence and valuation of the
property and establish accountability thereon.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the:
 Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their
records regularly; and
 Municipal Accountant to continue validating previous years’
transactions and effect adjustments to arrive at the correct account
balance.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require:
a. the Municipal Treasurer to immediately liquidate/settle his cash advances in
compliance with existing regulation, otherwise, we will be constrained to file
appropriate administrative/legal action against him;
b. other accountable officers to settle their cash advances by deducting the
corresponding amounts from the money due them;
c. Henceforth, see to it that all cash advances are liquidated at the end of the
year for fair presentation of financial statements; and
d. The Municipal Accountant to submit the Schedule of Due from Officers and
Employees for further review and verification in compliance with Section
112 of PD 1445.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the newly
assumed Municipal Accountant to expedite retrieval of relevant documents to
substantiate the account balance, otherwise remit the amount to the National
Treasury in compliance with EO No. 431, s. 2005 of the President of the Philippines.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the newly
assumed Municipal Accountant to exert effort in determining the composition of the
Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts to facilitate reconciliation
of both accounts. Also, effect the necessary adjustments in the books once
reconciled. Moreover, maintain Subsidiary Ledger for the accounts in compliance
with COA Circular 2015-009.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the newly-assumed Municipal
Accountant to expedite the retrieval of the relevant documents afterwhich, transfer
the Construction in Progress account to its appropriate asset accounts to reflect the
correct balance of the accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and
the Municipal Treasurer to monitor the remittances of barangay shares from real
property taxes and to remit the same to the concerned barangay treasurers within
five days after the end of each quarter, in compliance with Section 271(d) of RA 7160
and COA Circular No. 92-382.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GSO-Designate to submit to
the Audit Team the Monthly Report of Official Travels and Monthly Report of Fuel
Consumption for each motor vehicle duly supported with Driver’s Trip Tickets
issued during the month in compliance with the provision of COA Circular No. 77-
61, for review and evaluation. Henceforth, see to it that the Driver’s Trip Ticket were
properly accomplished.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor refrain from procuring goods and
services through reimbursement and ensure strict implementation of Republic Act
No. 9184.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the OIC- Municipal Accountant
in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer to submit to the Audit Team the 28 DVs
for CY 2018 and 20 DVs for CY 2017 together with their supporting documents for
review and evaluation.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Bids and
Awards Committee (BAC) Secretariat to submit to the Audit Team the copies of the
contracts together with the supporting documents within five working days from
approval thereof. Also, instruct the BAC to strictly adhere and comply with the
pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001 on the prompt submission of
perfected contracts and its supporting documents, to ensure timely review,
evaluation and verification thereof.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer to:
 submit to the SA and ATL at the beginning of the year, the list of all ongoing
government projects/programs/activities and those that are to be
implemented during the year;
 inform the Auditors within 10 days after the award of the infrastructure
project or before the start of the program/activity that the appropriate project
signboards and/or public notices were already posted for validation; and
 regularly submit the monthly monitoring report in compliance with the
above cited regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC Municipal Accountant to maintain a separate record for each type of loans of the Municipality to facilitate the recording, monitoring and review thereof.  Also, make representation to the bank and secure data / records of the loans and reconcile the same with the records of the Municipality and ensure that the accrued interest for the loans is properly taken up in the books.

We recommended further that the OIC Municipal Accountant prepare the adjusting entry to correct the erroneous entry made in recording the procurement of motor vehicle.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee submit explanation as to the non-submission of the APP and PMR to GPPB. Also, we recommended that the Budget Office and the BAC revisit their functions and coordinate with each other to facilitate the preparation of the APP for all procurements and its inclusion in the Annual Budget. Henceforth, strictly follow the provisions in R.A. 9184 in the preparation and submission of the Annual Procurement Plan and the Procurement Monitoring Report.

We recommended that Management submit proof of obligation or explanation for the non-obligation of prior year claims. Further, we  recommend  that  the  Municipal  Accountant  and  Budget  Officer  discontinue  the  practice  of  charging  prior  year’s  expenditure  to  current  year  appropriation  and  that  proper  matching  of  expenses  against  appropriation  should always be observed pursuant to the above-cited regulations.
We recommended that Management submit the aforementioned reports to avoid disallowance in audit.
We recommended that Management submit proof that Rescue Reconnaisance I is an accredited training organization by the National Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council.

1.7 We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor create an
inventory committee to conduct physical count of all PPEs of the Municipality
and come up with the RPCPPE

1.8 We further recommended that whoever is appointed to perform physical count
shall undergo a training on Property and Supply Management System (PSMS),
to gain adequate understanding and working knowledge of the systems,
processes, policies and other regulations pertaining to property and supply
management.

2.4 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant
to obtain snap shots of bank statement from the depository banks of the
Municipality and prepare the BRS and effect corrections both in the book of
accounts and the bank, if any, be effected.

3.5 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant
to exhaust all efforts in the settlement of cash advances.

4.6 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the officials concerned to
implement the projects listed under the AIP so as to effect timely delivery of
services to the constituents.

5.6 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the LDRRM Focal Person
to implement the projects listed under the LDRRMFIP so as to effect timely
delivery of services to the constituents.

6.7 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Planning and
Development Officer (MPDO), Municipal Engineer, Municipal Agriculturist,
LDRRM Focal Person, and Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer
(MSWDO) to judiciously plan proposed projects and fast-track the
implementation of said programs, projects and activities funded by the BUB to
provide constituents of the maximum benefits due them.

7.5 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Assessor to
expedite the processing of documents for the titling of lands owned by the
Municipality. We further recommended that before any construction is made,
land ownership thru a certificate of title is obtained.

8.10 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Human Resources
Management Officer to adhere to the provisions of Section 77 of RA 7160.

9.8 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the SWM Focal Person to
consider the appropriate means of communication depending on the information
that it wishes to pass on to the public and its stakeholders. For instance, there
should be public notices on television and on the radio about who can access the
landfill, the working hours at the landfill to limit after-hours access and the
schedule for collection of wastes from households for the different zones.
Websites have become increasingly effective and economical in disseminating
information. Therefore it is important to have a specific link for Solid Waste
Management within the Municipality’s website so that it is easier for the public
to have access to information and policy on solid waste management as well as
any issue concerning solid waste management.

9.9 To ensure effective implementation of the solid waste management plan, a well-
designed, current and targeted community awareness program is required. The
targeted audience should be considered when determining how the information
should be given to the public. The urgency of the matter should also be
considered so that everyone is aware of the matter as soon as possible.

10.4 We recommended that a mechanism to measure the volume of waste and type of
waste should be placed at the site, which will provide baseline data to assist in
developing effective waste minimization strategies and future plans for waste
management.

11.7 We recommended that a proper gate at the entrance to the dumpsite be built so
that it is easier for the personnel to stop vehicles coming in, inspect the waste to
confirm the type of waste to be disposed then direct the vehicle to the
designated area depending on the type of waste already inspected. This daily
process will compensate for the non-segregation of waste at source and will
ensure that wastes being disposed at the landfill are properly separated.

11.8 We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the SWM Focal
Person to implement a color coding or appropriate means of identification to
ensure that wastes are properly segregated at the dumpsite.

12.5 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Solid Waste
Management Focal Person to maintain disposal site records at the dumpsite.

13.4 We recommended that the dumpsite be fenced with a secured gate at the
entrance to minimize illegal visitors or scavengers. This will also help address
the occupational health and safety issues by limiting access.

14.6 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Person to
train personnel manning the VAW Help Desks in the barangays to capacitate
them to address cases of violence against women in a gender-responsive
manner.

15.4 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Person to
establish a GAD Monitoring and Evaluation System.

16.5 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Accountant to make reconciliation of their records against that of the
GSIS. Furthermore, the municipal accountant shall also verify who among the
employees is deficient in paying its loan obligations so that an adjustment in
his/her deduction shall be made.

We recommended that Management instruct collectors to remit their collection within the prescribed period and require them to strictly adhere to the provisions mentioned in the foregoing to prevent misappropriation or incurrence of loss of government funds.
It is also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer monitor closely the remittances of collection of the collectors.

We recommended that Management exert extra effort by making follow-ups and continue sending demand letters. Further, find any legal remedies and/or strategies to compel them for the immediate liquidation for the funds transferred, if necessary.
It is also recommended not to download another fund to barangays unless previous funds transfer were fully liquidated.  In addition, verify and monitor the implementation of projects funded out of the transferred funds especially for long outstanding downloaded funds and demand return of the fund if found not utilized or used.

We recommended that Management initiate action for the liquidation of the outstanding fund transfer by sending demand letters and making follow ups to all NGOs/POs concerned and demanding return of the fund if found not utilized or has been used not in accordance with Memorandum of Agreement and follow strictly Section 5.4 of COA Circular No. 2007-001 on the granting of fund transfer to NGOs/POs.
It is also recommended to submit immediately the lacking supporting documents and strictly adhere to COA Circular No. 2012-001 pertaining to fund releases to NGO/PO requirements.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Assessor and General Services Officer to fast track the processing of the Certificate of Titles of all parcels of land acquired by the municipality pursuant to Section 39 of PD 1445.

We recommended that management require the officials concerned to prepare a physical inventory report of Biological Assets and submit to the accounting office for reconciliation. Confirm the existence thereof and effect necessary adjustments in the books, if warranted.

We recommended that management stop the practice of engaging consultancy services without conducting public bidding and/or undergo the regular procurement process as provided under Section 10 of RIRR of RA 9184.
It is also recommended to submit the approved consultancy contract together with the supporting documents for the determination of the reasonableness of the consultancy cost.
We recommended that henceforth, any payment of claims for catering services should be supported with activity’s documentation and menu list to establish the validity and regularity of transactions.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, as the Chair of Municipal Solid Waste Management Board shall provide for the schemes for volume reduction of solid waste by requesting the Sangguniang Bayan to pass an ordinance in refraining the use of non-environmentally accepted packaging for all commercial establishments within the locality after proper consultation and hearing with the stakeholders or with the sectors concerned.
We further recommended that the management direct the MENRO to:
Fast track the implementation of its programs and projects for improvement of its solid waste management;
Monitor the prioritization of the establishment of MRF and the constructed MRF of every barangay, the facility must be designed to accommodate efficiently and store recyclable wastes before transferring to disposal facility or sanitary landfill; and
Closely monitor the proper segregation of solid waste disposal by every household and establishments collected at the Materials Recovery Facilities as temporary dumpsite, and impose sanctions/penalties for every household and establishments which violated the proper segregation of solid waste in coordination with the Barangay Officials.
It is also recommended to mobilize the active participation of schools, private organization and barangay officials informing, educating and monitoring households in the proper waste disposal from source.

We recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan and the Local Finance Committee revisit the operations of the said economic enterprise and ascertain the viability and sustainability of the economic enterprise by finding alternative ways on how to generate more income to cover the funding requirement for the year.

We recommended that Management direct those involved in planning and implementation to coordinate and monitor closely and hasten the implementation of the projects and optimize the utilization of 20 percent Development Fund through a) proper planning of project including timing of execution; b) coordinated action from LGU officials for better result; and c)strict monitoring in project implementation to ensure accomplishment of the project by setting time frames and target dates so that the constituents could avail of the benefits of the programs and improve the living and economic condition of the people of the municipality.
It is also recommended to submit the perfected contracts and the supporting documents within the prescribed period. Also, installation/posting of project signboard should be made as soon as the award has been made to promote transparency of governance.

We recommended that Management instruct the implementing office to fast track the implementation of the projects so that targeted beneficiaries can enjoy the intended benefits may derived therefrom. Further, instruct the BAC and its secretariat to set time frames in its procurement process so that the implementing office can start also their implementation as early as possible. Also, furnish the Audit Team of the certified copies of suspension orders and its supporting documents for its immediate determination if the grounds for suspension is reasonable or not.
It is also recommended to always coordinate with other implementing agencies for every fund downloaded for a particular project so that same problem will not occur again that would result to duplication of project.

We recommended that Management maximize the utilization of LDRRMF for disaster preparedness, prevention and mitigation pursuant to DILG DBM Joint Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013.
It is also recommended that Management prioritize the implementation of infrastructure projects which can minimize the risk of flood during heavy rains and/or can lessen the damage dealt by an occurring calamity.

We recommended that Management:
Instruct the GAD Focal Person to submit the required supporting documents of GAD AR and to coordinate with the Municipal Budget Office for the reconciliation of actual amount utilized; and
Maximize the utilization of the GAD fund for the year so that wider scope of intended beneficiaries can be benefited of the GAD programs, projects and activities, and objective of the fund can be fully achieved during the year.

1.7	We recommended and the Management agreed to instruct the members of the IDC to submit to the Audit Team immediately and without incurring more delays the RPCI and RPCPPE pursuant to Section 124 of NGAS Manual for LGU.  The Committee shall also provide copies thereof to the Supply Officer and the Accountant for record purposes; advise the Municipal Accountant to maintain Supplies, PPEs and other properties Ledger Cards.  It is very elementary that ALL the accounts in the financial statements is supported with ledgers.  Reconcile the accounting records with the reports being kept by the Supply Officer and/or with the IDC at least quarterly; direct the Supply Officer to keep ALL/completely the issued ARE.  These will be used to verify PPEs being issued to the end users; and adhere to Sections 119, 120 and 124 of the NGAS Manual, Volume 1 and other pertinent laws, rules and regulations governing the conduct of physical count of properties, maintenance of records and submission of reports thereof to the Auditor.

2.8	We recommended that Management convene with the SMWD officers together with the respective resident Auditors.  Clarify the intention of both parties as regards the transfer of the waterworks/sewerage facilities including the water rights of the LGU’s Water System to the MSWD in exchange of the latter’s payment of P5,000,000.00.  Amend the provisions in the project study and the MOA that are found conflicting with the established intentions.  Ensure that both parties should have a uniform interpretation of the provision/stipulation stated in the project study and MOA.  Adjust the PPE account of the LGU based on the established/clarified intentions of the parties and in accordance with the accounting standard set in PPSAS No. 1.

3.5	We recommended that Management direct the MDRRMO to allocate 30% QRF separate and distinct from the 70% allocation in the 5% LDRRM; stop utilizing the LDRRMF for administrative expenses like establishment & maintenance of MDRRM/CCA Office.  Submit to COA the justification on the payments made which shall be evaluated on whether to issue Notice of Suspension or Disallowance on the transactions as circumstances may warrant; instruct the MDRRMO to carefully plan and include only in the LDRRMFIP the PAPs that are considered very important and urgent which need to be realized in a particular year.  Follow-up/coordinate with the PPDO and PDRRMO from time to time with regard to the CBMS and the Dredging of Silted Rivers projects which implementation of the same by the LGU were dependent with the Province; remind the Budget Officer to furnish COA the Reports on the Receipt and Utilization of Cash Donations; advise the MDRRMO to prepare and submit monthly reports on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF to COA in a manner prescribed in Item 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.  The MDRRMO may request from the Mayor manpower compliment to assist him in the MDRRM works; and ensure compliance to the provisions of Section 2 of R.A. No. 10121, Section 4.3 of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG-CSC JMC No. 2014-1, Item 4 of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1, Section V.A (1) of COA Circular No. 2014-002 and Item 5.1.16 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 on the utilization and reporting of the LDRRMF.

4.5	We recommended that Management enjoin the Local School Board (LSB) to implement all the programs, projects and activities (PAPs) embodied in the LSB annual budget.  The inclusion of the PAPs in the budget was because these were already considered by the board as important, urgent, and therefore priority and adhere to the declared policy of the State mentioned in R.A. No. 5447 and Section 2 of P.D. No. 1445 in the utilization of SEF.

5.5	We recommended and the Management agreed to advise the MSWDO to ensure implementation of all the LCPC PPAs so that the fund is optimally utilized; remind the MBO to correctly charge/obligate every financial transaction against its corresponding appropriation; follow-up with the DOLE on the status of the implementation of the project Construction of the Social Protection Center in the municipality to establish whether there is still a need to appropriation an amount from the LCPC fund as an LGU equity thereof; send a letter-inquiry to the City Government of Surigao on the amount to be paid for availing the services of the Bahay Pag-asa.  This may avoid penalties and additional charges that may incur for non-payment or late settlement of accounts; and adhere faithfully to Section 2 of P.D. No. 1445 and Section 2 of R.A. No. 9344 in the utilization of LCPC fund or implementation of its PAPs.

6.5	We recommended and the Management agreed to appropriate an amount for GAD PAPs at least 5% of the total annual budget; direct the GFPS to always formulate the LGU GPB based on the GAD Agenda or identified priority gender issues which were derived from the information gathered from the sources mentioned in Sections 4.1.C.2.1 and 4.1.C.1.4 of JMC No. 2013-01 and to create and maintain a GAD database which will be used to identify prevalent gender issues present in the Municipality; submit to the COA Audit Team a copy of the GPB and AR in a manner prescribed in Item V of COA Circular No. 2014-001; and strictly adhere to the governing provisions set forth in the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 and COA Circular No. 2014-001 in the creation, utilization and reporting of GAD fund.

7.4	We recommended that Management require the BAC, including the BAC Secretariat and the TWG, to submit to the Audit Team the lacking documents and to explain to the latter the presence of procurement deficiencies/irregularities.  Non-compliance hereof may cause the suspension of the transactions and adhere strictly with the provisions of R.A. 9184 and its IRR, COA Circular Nos. 2009-001 and 2012-001 on the procurement of goods, infrastructures and services.
8.2	We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to immediately remit the taxes withheld promptly to the BIR to avoid incurrence of penalties and charges.
9.1	We recommended and the Management agreed to submit to COA the documents required in the subject Notices of Suspension and to settle the amount stated in the Notices of Disallowance.

10.6	We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the DTI-BuB Focal Person to supervise and/or implement the project Nito Production in accordance with the approved plan and its objectives as provided in the Item 2 of the MOA; instruct the Organization Treasurer, through the DTI-BuB Focal Person, to diligently prepare and maintain correct, proper and complete financial records and statements to show the financial operations and condition of the project.  Register with the BIR so that OR can be used by the organization.  Prepare vouchers too or any document showing an authorized payment so that the utilization of the fund is controlled.  The DTI-BuB Focal Person is enjoined to submit to COA the CYs 2016-2018 financial statements/reports of the project; advise the organization, through the DTI-BuB Focal Person, to set aside fund for the construction of a production area.  This will house the weaving machineries, utensils and other equipment which shall be procured by the LGU as mentioned in the MOA.  Create also a production timeline/schedule; and instruct the DTI-BuB Focal Person and other concerned personnel to regularly monitor the condition and performance of the project as well as the other PAPs under DTI.  Monitoring includes ocular inspection, confirming compliance to the provisions in the MOA.  These ensure immediate action to the rising problems which will result to the achievement of the project’s primary objective as well as guarantee profitability and sustainability of the same.

11.6	We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the DTI-BuB Focal Person to supervise and/or implement the project Establishment of Bigasan ng Barangay in accordance with the approved plan and its objectives as provided in the Item 2 of the MOA; instruct the Organization Treasurer, through the DTI-BuB Focal Person, to diligently prepare and maintain correct, proper and complete financial records and statements to show the financial operations and condition of the project; register the business with the BIR so that OR can be used by the organization.  Prepare vouchers too or any document showing an authorized payment so that the utilization of the fund is controlled.  The DTI-BuB Focal Person is enjoined to submit to COA the CYs 2016-2018 financial statements/reports of the project; advise the organization, through the DTI-BuB Focal Person, to refrain from selling the grain rice on credit.  Revenue-wise, these commodities shall be sold on a per kilo basis; direct the association, through the DTI-BuB Focal Person to utilize the supplies and equipment as intended. The group must appoint among its members a capable and dependable individual to man the project; and instruct the DTI-BuB Focal Person and other concerned personnel to regularly monitor the condition and performance of the project as well as the other PAPs under DTI.  These ensure immediate action to the rising problems which will result to the achievement of the project’s primary objective as well as guarantee profitability and sustainability of the same.

12.6	We recommended and the Management agreed to advise the association, through the DTI-BuB Focal Person, to utilize the amount of P32,362.00 to purchase the starter kit.  These equipment and supplies will be used to produce more outputs, even the absence of a production room and to utilize only, for the meantime, the available spaces in the house of each member of the group.  More productions will give more income to the group.  This income will eventually be utilized to complete the building which will serve as the production area and display center; instruct the Organization Treasurer, through the DTI-BuB Focal Person, to prepare and maintain financial records and statements to show the financial operations and condition of the project.  Register with the BIR so that OR can be used by the organization.  The ORs can be utilized as control to properly and completely monitor the receipt of funds by the association.  Prepare vouchers too or any document showing an authorized payment so that the utilization of the fund is controlled.  Create also a production timeline/schedule.  This schedule will provide the number of products to make in a given period, how many members needed to produce the products and who will produce thereof vis-à-vis the target sales of the same period.  The presence of the production timeline will ensure synchronized, uniform and quality outputs; and instruct the DTI-BuB Focal Person and other concerned personnel to regularly monitor the condition and performance of the project as well as the other PAPs under DTI.  Monitoring includes ocular inspection, confirming compliance to the provisions in the MOA.  These ensure immediate action to the problems which will result to the achievement of the project’s primary objective as well as guarantee profitability and sustainability of the same.

13.6	We recommended and the Management agreed to revisit the MOA and the business plan of the project.  Together with the officers and members of the organization, reevaluate whether the project, with the present circumstances, is totally not feasible to operate.  Identify the presence of remedial actions or possible solutions to the identified problems like planting a variety of coconut trees suitable for sap production only.  They can ask opinion/idea from the Philippine Coconut Authority on this matter.  Requesting reasonable rate from the coconut farmers to get saps from tall coconut trees could be other alternative.  These coconut farmers should be explained that the benefits of the project would redound also to their end in the future; initiate a meeting together with the officers of the organization with the DOLE, if the foregoing recommendation is not viable.  Discuss in the meeting the possible business alternatives which are feasible in the locality considering its available resources, a business that will fit with the capability of the people operating the same; suggest to the officers of the organization to assess carefully and make a feasibility study whether the bakery business would indeed sustainable in the area.  If positive, discuss the matter with the DOLE for the change of project.  Execute a MOA to cover the new venture; examine and evaluate every PAPs offered by the national government agencies for the LGU’s implementation.  Primary consideration should be made with the resources/raw materials needed, labor and market and whether these indeed will benefit the people.  Consult the end-users concerning thereof; and instruct the Project Focal Person to regularly monitor the condition and performance of the project as well as the other PAPs under DOLE.  Monitoring includes ocular inspection, confirming compliance to the provisions in the business plan and agreements.  These ensure immediate action to the rising problems which will result to the achievement of the project objectives as well as guarantee profitability and sustainability of the same.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor create a committee that will conduct physical count of PPE, after-which, require the Treasurer to prepare the Property Card for each category of assets, in compliance with Sections 119 and 124 of the MNGAS for LGU, Volume 1, to ascertain existence and valuation of the property and establish accountability thereon.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer/GSO-Designate commented that they conducted the physical count of all PPEs of the Municipality in January 2019, however, due to loads of work assigned to her and time constraint, she was not able to finish the inventory. The Municipal Budget Officer will request to the Sangguniang Bayan for the creation of General Services Office and for the designation of a General Service Officer who will conduct the complete inventory of PPE and will be responsible thereof. Also, the Municipal Mayor agreed with and assured compliance of the audit recommendation.
observations.reason: There is no RPCPPE submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the:  OIC-Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their records regularly; and  OIC-Municipal Accountant to continue validating previous years’ transactions and effect adjustments to arrive at the correct account balance.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant both agreed and committed to reconcile their records. The Municipal Treasurer commented that the records of collection and Official Receipts are intact in their Office as well as the checks issued. The Municipal Accountant will start the reconciliation this CY 2019 and will reflect the adjustments in the books of accounts.
observations.reason: The Cash Book and Ledger balances still showed discrepancies.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the newly assumed Municipal Accountant to exert effort in determining the composition of the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts to facilitate reconciliation of both accounts. Also, effect the necessary adjustments in the books once reconciled. Moreover, maintain Subsidiary Ledger for the accounts in compliance with COA Circular 2015-009.
observations.managementAction: The OIC- Municipal Accountant exerted extra effort in retrieving relevant documents.
observations.reason: Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts still showed difference.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the OIC-Municipal Accountant to reconstruct the subsidiary ledgers for CY 2013 and below in compliance with existing regulations to facilitate validation of account balances.
observations.managementAction: The OIC Municipal Accountant already maintain Subsidiary Ledgers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to strictly comply with the requirements stated under COA Circular No. 92-389 to prevent the possible re-use of those documents for other claims of same nature as part of a sound internal control system in the disbursement of funds.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer had assigned one of her staff to help her ensure that all the disbursement vouchers and its supporting documents are stamped “Paid” beginning January 3, 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the OIC-Municipal Accountant to submit to the Audit Team all the required supporting documents as presented in Annex 6 in accordance with R.A. No. 9184 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 and for evaluation. Otherwise, the same may be suspended in audit.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant submitted the necessary supporting documents.
observations.reason: Not all the required supporting documents were submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the OIC-Municipal Accountant to submit to the Audit Team all the required supporting documents for evaluation. Otherwise, the same may be suspended in audit.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant submitted the necessary supporting documents.
observations.reason: Not all the required supporting documents were submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer to: • submit the list of all on-going government projects/programs/activities and those that are to be implemented during the year; • submit the monthly monitoring report in compliance with the above cited regulations; and • inform the Auditors within 10 days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices were already posted for validation.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer promised to regularly submit to the Audit Team the monthly monitoring report on publicized government projects/programs/activities starting CY 2019.
observations.reason: List of government PPAs that are to be implemented were not submitted to the Audit Team. No action was undertaken by the Municipality. The Municipal Engineer is not aware of the regulation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Budget Officer (MBO) to appropriate at least 1% of their IRA for LCPC and provide programs, projects and activities for the strengthening and implementation of the PPAs of LCPC as required under DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality already appropriated one percent of their Internal Revenue Allotment (IRA) for the strengthening and implementation of the PPAs of Local Council for the Protection of Children (LCPC) for CY 2018, as required under DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MPDO, with the assistance of the Municipal GAD Focal Point System (MGADFPS) to: • Institutionalize the GAD database with adequate and systematically gathered sex-disaggregated data which will provide the bases for gender analysis, planning, programming and policy formulation; • Undertake gender analysis to identify and confirm existing gender issues and ensure that the programs, projects and activities are responsive to the said issues; • Revisit Annex B of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 which identifies the data requirements for particular gender issues; and • Include in the GAD Plan and Budget the attendance to seminar and training of the personnel assigned in the GAD as well as the expenses related to development of GAD Database and GAD Code. Moreover, require the Sangguniang Bayan to formulate and pass a local GAD Code or ordinances with the assistance of the LGU GFPS, to support the localization of Magna Carta of Women and to ensure that GAD is mainstreamed in local policy-making and integrated in local development plans.
observations.managementAction: The MSWDO commented that they already requested to the Sangguniang Bayan to create and pass a GAD Code. Also, she mentioned they are started the gathering of data for sex-disaggregate database and for the establishment of GAD database last May 2019. She agreed with the audit recommendation and assured compliance.
observations.reason: The Sangguniang Bayan still not pass a GAD Code and the GAD database was still not maintained by the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the OIC - Municipal Accountant to remit all taxes withheld within the prescribed period in order for the Government to utilize the same and free the officials concerned of any liability in terms of surcharges that may be imposed by BIR.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer commented that she is regularly remitting the taxes withheld within the prescribed period. As to the unsubstantiated previous year’s balance, she mentioned that it was already remitted to the BIR, and it was recorded in her cashbook. She promised to provide the Municipal Accountant with the necessary documents/proof of remittance for proper recording/adjustments in the books.
observations.reason: See Observation No. 17 Part II of this AAR

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor take immediate action to fill up the position of the Municipal Accountant with qualified personnel so that efficiency and effectiveness in the discharge of the functions can be attained.  Further, observe the rules and regulations on designation as mandated by the Civil Service Commission.
observations.managementAction: Hired a qualified person for the position.
observations.reason: Complied by Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC Municipal Accountant to submit immediately the financial statements and supporting schedules otherwise, withhold the salaries of the concerned personnel.
observations.managementAction: Submitted the complete set of CY 2018 financial statements on September 10, 2020.
observations.reason: Complied by Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the OIC Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the Monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements and draw journal entry voucher to record all valid reconciling items to ensure the reliability of the Cash in Bank account balances, otherwise, withhold the salary of the concerned employees until the same have been complied.
observations.managementAction: Submitted Bank Reconciliation Statements as of December 31, 2018 on September 2, 2020.
observations.reason: Complied by Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the OIC Municipal Accountant reclassify the non-current portion of Loans Payable – Domestic for the proper presentation of the account in the Statement of Financial Position of the Municipality at year-end.
observations.managementAction: Presented the current and non-current portions of the loans payable-domestic account in the Statement of Financial Position as of December 31, 2018.
observations.reason: Complied by Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management submit the aforementioned reports to avoid disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: Submitted fuel consumption report.
observations.reason: Reiterated in CY 2018 AAR prepared on September 14, 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to exhaust all legal remedies against EM Paule Construction and Trading and Poiel Engineering and Management Services and impose / collect the necessary liquidated damages for the failure to complete and deliver the said project on time. Further, consider the idea of terminating the contract due to the default of the contractor.
observations.managementAction: Terminated the contract with EM Paule Construction and Trading and Poiel Engineering and Management and awarded it to a new contractor.
observations.reason: Contract with the previous contractor was terminated but no legal remedies were availed by the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management direct the members of the local finance committee of the Municipality of San Francisco, Cebu, to refund honoraria they received as members of said committee for CY 2012.  Henceforth, the giving of honoraria to the members of the local finance committee be discontinued.
observations.managementAction: Stopped the practice of giving honoraria to the local finance committee.
observations.reason: Paid honoraria were not yet refunded.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant verify the Construction in Progress and other affected accounts and effect the necessary adjustments in order to reflect the correct balances in the Financial Statements. Also, require the maintenance of the Registry of Public Infrastructures and monitor accounts/assets not recorded in the books of accounts and cause the preparation of the subsidiary ledgers to substantiate the recorded balances in the Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: Completed projects were adjusted to its corresponding asset accounts.
observations.reason: Registry of Public Infrastructures and subsidiary ledgers are still not prepared.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare monthly Bank statements for all banks to ensure prompt submission of the Bank statements; The Accountant shall draw journal vouchers to record all valid reconciling items that require adjustment and correction in the GL. The Duplicate and Quadruplicate copies of the BRS including the paid checks, original copies of debit/credit memos shall be submitted to the Auditor concerned for verification within ten days for receipt after the end of each month.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: 1)Direct the Municipal Inventory Committee to: conduct a complete physical count of PPE, physical count 2) Direct the Municipal Accountant to determine the validity of the PPE account through reconciliation of the accounting records and the actual inventory; 3)Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the PPE Ledger Card (PPELC) for each class of PPE listing therein the data as to its acquisition, description, custody, estimated life ,rate of depreciation, disposal, and other information about the property, plant and equipment, based on the source documents of the transactions and drop from the books of accounts the unserviceable properties after the Waste Material Report and the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property are duly approved and evaluated by the COA.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: a. Require the Municipal Accountant to send written reminder within ten (10) days before the expiration of the prescribed period a written reminder under signature of the LCE or his duly authorized representative enjoining officials and employees concerned to settle their cash advance; b. Direct the Municipal Accountant not to sign claims for additional cash advance until the previous cash advance is settled; c. Compel the Municipal Accountant to cause the immediate withholding of salaries and claims of officers and employees who failed to settle their cash advances within the prescribed period after being sent notice and demand; d. Require the Municipal Accountant to stop the practice of offsetting the amount due to the accountable officer (for reimbursement) against his other outstanding cash advances; e. Require the Municipal Accountant to furnish all department heads listings of all unliquidated cash advances of employees under their charge to enhance efforts to effect liquidation.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: a. Compel the Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant a duly certified list of the taxpayers showing the names and the amounts due from them for the current and prior years, b. Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare journal entries to record the uncollected RPT and SET of the current and prior years, and henceforth, to account for the receivables annually, including discounts granted and penalties imposed; and c. Direct the Municipal Assessor to clean up the Assessment Role to establish a realistic and accurate RPT and SET Receivable.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: a. Direct the MDRRM Council to prepare its risk reduction and management plan in accordance with the provision of Section 5.0 of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 dated March 25, 2013; b. Compel the Municipal Budget Officer to charge disbursement out of the 70% of LDRRMF only to those that specifically used to support the risk reduction and management activities of the LGU; c. Request the Sangguniang Bayan to issue a resolution to reprogram items in the Annual Budget and to prepare a revised Annual Investment Plan.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Engineer, Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer and Municipal Agricultural Officer to fast-track the implementation of projects to provide constituents the intended benefits and Make sure that the budget will be approved by Sangguniang Bayan on time and that available funds for BUB and LGSF funded projects will be utilized as intended.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive cause the creation of a Local Council for officer Protection of Children and compel an allocation for the Municipal Budget Officer to provide a budget allocation equivalent to 1% of LGU’s IRA for LCPC plans and program.
observations.reason: In 2019 budget, the budget made sure that there’s an allocation for the 1% LCPC.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Engineer to submit to the Office of the Auditor all suspension orders or any documents that would warrant the non-imposition of liquidated damages to the concerned contractors, otherwise, the Municipal Accountant shall compute the liquidated damages. We also recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Engineer to prepare a report on the unperformed portions of the aforementioned infrastructure projects based on their respective completion date.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the officers and employees to refund the amount collected in the absence of legal basis and that the management should desist and stop granting Personnel Benefits without legal basis.
observations.reason: Refunds of the amount granted to officers and employees were collected.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: 1. Require the personnel in-charge in the preparation of GSIS remittances and municipal accountant to conduct thorough reconciliation of their records with that of the GSIS; 2. Require the Municipal Treasurer that the amount withheld shall be remitted to GSIS in full within 10 days following the end of the month the withholding was made; 3. Require the Municipal Accountant to make sure that premium/contributions as well as employees and employer contributions are properly reconciled.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer to identify the employees with unremitted contributions to enable the Municipal Government to settle its obligations. The Municipal Accountant should also regularly reconcile the amounts withheld and remitted to Pag-IBIG in order to ascertain the accuracy and reliability of the balances of Due to Pag-IBIG account in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: 1. Direct the Human Resource Management Office to review the duties and functions of the Job Order Workers so as not to duplicate the duties and functions of the regular plantilla employees; 2. Direct the Municipal Budget Officer to adjust the rates of Job Order Workers in compliance to regulations.
observations.reason: The LGU stopped granting Personnel Benefits without legal basis.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for the Municipality to prioritize the implementation of the CBMS for the GAD database to serve as inputs or basis for planning, programming and policy formulation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor through the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board strictly implement feasible and functional programs and implement the solid waste management projects listed in the AIP. We also recommended that the Mayor provide the necessary financial and technical support and enjoin the barangays to establish their own Materials Recovery Facility.
observations.reason: Lack of funds / manpower

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive cause the creation of an Inventory Committee and instruct them to commence the conduct of the physical inventory of all LGU properties.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct: 1. The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer to observe the guidelines in the utilization of the 20% Economic Development Fund to attain the maximum benefit envisioned under DILG-DBM Joint Circular No. 2011-01; 2. The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to install appropriate controls over the proper utilization.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant immediately submit the corresponding disbursement vouchers together with related supporting documents pertaining to the one hundred seventy-three (173) checks totaling P4,144,389.63. Henceforth, they must ensure that the reports submitted are complete and intact.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive cause the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer to observe the guidelines in the utilization of the 20% Economic Development Fund and to install appropriate controls over the proper utilization.

We recommend that the Management:

1) demand from the officers and employees concerned for the immediate liquidation/settlement of their unliquidated cash advances through issuance of demand letters issued by the Municipal Accountant and noted by the Local Chief Executive, copy furnished the Audit Team. As to those that are no longer connected with the LGU, send demand letters to their last known address. Impose disciplinary actions against them. In case of non-compliance, direct the Municipal Accountant to withhold the payment of their salaries or any money due to them. Otherwise, filing of appropriate charges shall be dealt with them;

2) direct the officers and employees concerned to secure certification from the Municipal Accountant that they have no outstanding cash advance before claims for cash advance of travel expenses are processed; and

3) adhere strictly to COA Circular No. 97-002 to avoid accumulation of cash advances.
observations.managementAction: Management sent demand letters to officers and employees concerned. They continued to collect the refund for cash advances through payroll deduction.
observations.reason: Balances for CY 2018 decreases compared to previous years balance.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Local Chief Executive instruct the following officials of the Municipality to undertake the following measures:

a) perform periodic reconciliation of stock/property cards to ensure that discrepancies are immediately investigated, cleared and reconciled with the books of accounts. Also the Supply Officer should have a summary of total per account so that it would be easier to trace to what account contributes to the discrepancies noted;

b) prepare necessary journal entries to adjust the duly investigated reconciling items in order to reflect the correct account balances; and

c) instruct the Office of the Municipal Accountant should continue its effort to locate pertinent source documents to reduce the unidentified balances in the property ledger cards, if any, in order to render a complete and accurate financial report.
observations.managementAction: The Management was developing a New Property Information System to help monitor the PPEs and conducted the complete physical count.
observations.reason: RCPPE reconciled with Accounting Records

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to review the transactions and verify where the lumped balances originate and trace the details of the accounts and to immediately perform correcting entries in the affected accounts. Further, proper and regular monitoring must be conducted in the office concerned as to the posting of government transactions to facilitate fair presentation of financial reports.
observations.managementAction: The Management instituted policies that ensure the regular conduct of proper monitoring of the posting of the government transactions.

observations.recommendation: With the foregoing, it is recommended that the Local Officials, Municipal Budget Officer and Accountant be guided with the observation above in the succeeding disbursement of CF.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Peace and Order Council is tasked to make appropriate adjustments to ensure that activities funded by the CF are only those that are eligible in the program.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Budget Officer to follow the provisions stated in Joint Circular No. 2015-001 dated January 8, 2015, to ensure the submission on time the documentary requirements needed in the granting of cash advances for Confidential Fund.
observations.managementAction: The LCE, through the Municipal Administrator instructed the concerned Offices to submit the documents on time.
observations.reason: There are disbursement vouchers for CF not submitted within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We are reiterating our prior year’s recommendation that the Local Chief Executive directs the Municipal Accountant and other Head of Offices concerned to submit the accounts and other financial reports of the Municipality regularly, on every 10th day of the succeeding month.
observations.managementAction: The Management has submitted the Disbursement Vouchers and its supporting documents along the Financial reports on time.

observations.recommendation: We are reiterating our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive instruct the concerned officials to ensure the timely submission of copies of government contracts and each of all the documents forming part thereof within five (5) working days from execution pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009, to ensure timely review and evaluation of the same, so that the Management could be immediately notified of any defects/deficiencies noted thereon
observations.managementAction: Copies of the Executed contracts were furnished by the Management to the COA within the prescribed period.
observations.reason: There are still contracts submitted beyond the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to submit the necessary supporting documents as enumerated under Section 5.16 of COA Circular 2012-001 to determine the legality of granting Collective Negotiation Agreement Incentives to its employees. We further necessitate the submission of any law, circular or rules and regulations from competent authority that authorizes the Municipality to grant cash incentives to its officials and employees.

Moreover, instruct the Disbursing Officer to require the claimant to duly signed the payroll and liquidate in full the cash advance.
observations.managementAction: The management submitted documents to support the claim of CNA and additional incentives.
observations.reason: Submitted documents is not sufficient especially for the additional incentives claim.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to review all disbursements for GSIS, HDMF and PhilHealth to make sure that the monthly contributions and loan amortizations of all employees have been withheld; that no more employees have outstanding balances from the previous years; and that all of the contributions and loan amortizations withheld were remitted. Prompt remittance of these monthly contributions must be observed, in consonance of the aforementioned laws.
observations.managementAction: Withheld contributions were remitted to GSIS upon submission by employees of BP numbers.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that requisitions of supplies and materials be consolidated to determine the volume that is subject to bulk bidding by quarter. 

We further recommend to review the preparation of the Project Procurement Management Plan (PPMP) and the Annual Procurement Program (APP) on all the supplies and materials of the LGU, to serve as guide in the procurement activity. Proper planning in the procurement of supplies and materials should be made to avoid splitting of requisitions.
observations.managementAction: The BAC and GSO have conferred with the PPMP and APP for the Requisitions made by the LGU in order to utilize the appropriate procurement method specially with the supplies and materials to be used in the day to day operations.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that Management formulate a policy on the granting of financial assistance as to how much will be given to its constituents. 

The Management should instruct the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Office to require the applicants for assistance a complete submission of supporting documents as a basis of assessment and recommendation to make sure that only the deserving ones were provided with the necessary assistance to help them cope during crisis situations.

In view of the foregoing, we recommend that Management:

1) To strictly observe the provisions of Joint Circular (JC) No. 2015-01 and the Policies and Guidelines of DILG MC 99-65; and

2) To refrain from using the appropriations for Peace and Order for purposes other than those authorized under Joint Circular (JC) No. 2015-01 and the Policies and Guidelines of DILG MC 99-65.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Peace and Order Committee has already made adjustments to the Programs on Peace and Order. The Budget Officer is being keen on the compliance of obligations with JC No. 2015-01.
observations.reason: There are still items included in the POP which are not related.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to review all disbursements for the purchases of goods, whether expendable or non-expendable, to make sure that the required warranty was retained, in consonance with the provisions of Section 62 of RA 9184. Moreover, if the supplier for this project had already submitted the bank guarantee equivalent to what has been required; the Management are impelled to submit the said document. All of the other lacking original documents mentioned in this observation must also be submitted.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has included in all transactions pertaining to acquisition of goods to attach the warranties on the procured goods.
A new disbursing officer was instated for the task of payroll disbursement and made sure that all outstanding balances of previous personnel on the Cash Advances are liquidated or refunded.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to withhold the amount of Php58,472.20 and Php83,633.60 during the payment of the contract, otherwise, a Notice of Disallowance will be issued on the same amount.  Moreover, instruct the Municipal Engineer, the Bids and Awards Committee and the Technical Working Group to meticulously review all the documents and the basis of the Approved Budget of the Contract before proceeding to the procurement process. Further, require the concerned officials to update themselves with the rules and regulations relative to the new issuances issued by the DPWH on the implementation of the Infrastructure projects.
observations.managementAction: Demand letter was sent to the contractor concerned.
observations.reason: Not yet fully refunded

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management direct those involved in planning and implementation to monitor closely and hasten the implementation of the projects. Likewise, optimally utilized the 20% Development Fund allocated for the implementation of various projects, programs and activities that are envisioned to help in the attainment of social, economic and environmental development so that the intended beneficiaries can avail the maximum benefits that will be derived therefrom.
observations.managementAction: Some projects were already complete.
observations.reason: Not all projects were completed within the construction period.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management direct those involved in planning and implementation to monitor closely and to hasten the implementation of these projects/activities funded from National Government Agencies especially those releases in earlier years, so that the constituents can benefit from said projects. Likewise, please submit justification on the non-utilization of the funds.  Return to source agency any unused balance of grants/fund transfer upon completion of the project.
observations.managementAction: Most of the projects were already completed. Adjustments were made in the books to reflect the correct balances of the remaining on-going projects.
observations.reason: There are still projects not completed within the construction period.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Finance Committee and the Sangguniang Bayan review the provisions of the manual for local enterprises issued by DBM and undergo regular performance evaluation to monitor the financial status and self-sufficiency of the LEEs. There shall be also valid feasibility study conducted prior to procurement or construction of additional property, plant and equipment to ensure that these will generate satisfactory income. Further, separate financial reports for each LEE must be prepared annually or whenever needed.
observations.managementAction: Separate Financial Performance for each LEEs were provided and submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the management must submit the schedule containing therein the breakdown of the computed amount of the liquidated damages imposed against the contractor as a result of the apparent delay of the completion of the project without any extension in the contract time duly approved by the Head of the Procuring Entity.
observations.managementAction: Submitted suspension orders and disbursement vouchers reflecting the deducted liquidated damages.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Local Chief Executive:

a.) require the contractor to rectify the deficiencies of the project noted which shall still be inspected by auditor upon completion;

b.) adhere to the provisions of Section 62.2 of the IRR of RA 9184 which requires the procuring entity to observe the one-year defects liability period and imposition of warranty against structural defects; and

c.) direct those concerned in the implementation of the projects to closely monitor the same and to give emphasis on the quality of all on-going and upcoming projects so that comfort of the populace at large can be provided.
observations.managementAction: The AOM was communicated to the contractor for the project for which another rectification was promised to the Management
observations.reason: Some deficiencies was not yet rectified by the contractor.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Municipal Accountant to effect the adjustments/reconciling items in the respective books to present fairly the balances in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Effect the adjustments
observations.reason:
Partially implemented

The reconciling items had already been identified by the Accountant but remained unaffected in the books.

observations.recommendation: Create a GSO plantilla position and hire a person to fill therein subject to the financial capacity of the LGU.  If not feasible, designate a GSO in accordance with the provisions of Article 20 of R.A. 7160; instruct the inventory committee/team to spearhead, plan and conduct physical count of the Municipality’s supplies and properties and to prepare and submit to the Audit Team Report on Physical Count of PPE and other properties pursuant to Section 124 of NGAS Manual for LGU; advise the Municipal Accountant to maintain Supplies, PPEs and other properties Ledger Cards; direct the Storekeeper to maintain Supplies and Property Cards per category of materials, PPEs and other properties to reconcile balances thereof with the accounting records regularly; and adhere to Sections 119, 120 and 124 of the NGAS Manual, Volume 1 and other pertinent laws, rules and regulations governing the conduct of physical count of properties, maintenance of records and submission of reports thereof to the Auditor.
observations.managementAction: Create a plantilla position for GSO or designate one and conduct inventory
observations.reason:
Partially implemented

The LCE had already conducted a meeting with the department heads on the possibility of hiring a GSO or designating one.  No concrete compliance to the audit recommendations had been reached so far.

observations.recommendation: Convene with the SMWD officers and revisit the MOA.  Clarify with the SMWD whether the intent of the transaction was indeed a contract of sale.  If in affirmative, the LGU should drop from the books the Water System as part of the PPE without prejudice to the issuance of NS, ND or NC by this Office due to violation by the LGU of the provisions enshrined in COA Circular No. 89-296.  Amend the provisions of the MOA if necessary to conform with the true/correct intention of both parties.  Ensure that the parties should have a uniform interpretation of the provision/stipulation stated in the MOA; and adhere to the provisions and regulations governing disposal/divestment of government properties including its recording in the books.
observations.managementAction: Comply the audit recommendations
observations.reason:
Not implemented

The Team did not notice any compliance with the audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation: Secure a copy of the MOA from source Agencies to cover each of the financial assistance received; make a representation before the source Agency that every financial assistance granted to the LGU should be covered with a MOA; ensure that financial assistance received must be credited to  Liability/Payable account instead of Income account to maintain accountability; and faithfully adhere to the provisions of Section 2 of P.D. No. 1445 and Item 3.0 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 in the receipt of financial assistance/aids from various sources.
observations.managementAction: Secure MOA with the source agencies
observations.reason:
Partially implemented

The LGU had initially contacted the source agencies signifying the former’s intention of forging a MOA for the financial assistance received from the latter.  There was no MOA submitted to COA to date.

observations.recommendation: Direct the MSWDO to reassess its manpower vis-à-vis the duties and responsibilities and determine whether the task of overseeing the execution of the LCPC PPAs can be delegated to other qualified personnel in the office. If not, the MSWDO should adopt measures to personally ensure that the LCPC PPAs are implemented and the fund is optimally utilized; stop utilizing LCPC fund for expenses which have no direct effect in the promotion and protection of the children’s best interest; and adhere faithfully to Sections 2 and 15 of R.A. No. 9344 in the implementation of LCPC programs and activities that these shall serve its objectives.
observations.managementAction: Assess the manpower, ensure optimum utilization of the fund and refrain from utilizing the fund for non-LCPC expenses
observations.reason:
Partially implemented

The MSWDO had initially conferred with the Mayor regarding her concern in the office.  So far, there had been no disbursements from LCPC for non-LCPC expenses.

observations.recommendation: Encourage every barangay or cluster of barangays through various information drives to establish an MRF where sorting, segregation, composting and recycling of biodegradable, compostable and reusable waste materials will take place as mandated in Section 10 of R.A. No. 9003; fast tract the negotiations with the authorized public official of the Government of Surigao City regarding the co-utilization of its sanitary land fill as temporary dumping site of the Municipality’s solid wastes; advise the Municipal Ecological Solid Waste Management Board (MESWMB) to formulate as soon as possible alternative solutions for disposal of non-recyclable and special wastes in accordance with the provisions of R.A. No. 9003; ensure compliance to Section 10 of the R.A. No. 9003 where the LGU will collect non-recyclable materials and special wastes from the barangays and other sources to be disposed in the designated dump sites; and faithfully adhere to the provisions of R.A. No. 9003 particularly Sections 10, 32 and 33 thereof on the segregation, collection, transportation and disposal of solid waste materials.
observations.managementAction: Comply the audit recommendations
observations.reason:
Partially implemented

The MENRO had already consulted with COA on how to formulate policies in accordance with the provisions of RA 9003.  The LGU had also initially informed several barangays on the establishment of MRF.  Other audit recommendations remained unimplemented.

observations.recommendation: Appropriate an amount for GAD PAPs at least 5% of the total annual budget and not based from the regular income of the LGU; establish the LGU GFPS with the inclusion of the Executive Committee and the TWG and/or Secretariat as required in Section 4.1.A.1 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01.  This should be submitted to COA without delay; direct the GFPS to always formulate the LGU GPB based on the GAD Agenda or identified priority gender issues which were derived from the information gathered from the sources mentioned in Sections 4.1.C.2.1 and 4.1.C.1.4 of JMC No. 2013-01; submit to the COA Audit Team a copy of the GPB and AR in a manner prescribed in Item V of COA Circular No. 2014-001; and strictly adhere to the governing provisions set forth in the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 and COA Circular No. 2014-001 in the creation, utilization and reporting of GAD fund.
observations.managementAction: Appropriate GAD fund at least 5% of the appropriation.  Submit to COA the EO establishing the GFPS, formulate the LGU GPB based on the GAD Agenda or identified priority gender issues
observations.reason:
Partially implemented

The MBO had already considered in the CY 2019 municipal appropriation the GAD budget at least 5% of the annual budget.  Other audit recommendations remained uncomplied.

observations.recommendation: Implement the PPAs under the 20% DF as planned. Include only in the AIP the PPAs that are considered priority and realizable during the year; find other PPAs in the AIP or other infrastructure projects with deficient in appropriation so that the items in the DF with unused appropriation/savings could be utilized thereto through augmentation or realignment; and adhere faithfully to Section 2 of P.D. No. 1445 and DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 in the utilization of 20% DF.
observations.managementAction: Implement the PPAs under the 20% DF and include only PPAs in the AIP that are priority and realizable during the year
observations.reason:
Partially implemented

The LGU had already conducted a meeting with the concerned municipal heads to ensure implementation of the PPAs under the 20% DF.

observations.recommendation: Revisit the provisions of the project.  Immediately initiate a meeting with the DA to discuss remedies to the identified problems with due consideration of the fact that, to date, there had been no suppliers found for the delivery of the material Microns UV Plastic.  However, if the project will not be materialized, what other alternative courses of action be done so that the fund allocated thereof could still be used optimally; advise the School Principal to provide, whatever available means to them, minimum care to the project to avoid further loss and deterioration to the structure; make a suggestion to the DA that projects for implementation in partnership with the Municipality should run through proper discussion and consultation with the latter.  This way, the needed materials for the project could be easily/rightfully sourced or procured, among other concerns.  Thus, the fund intended thereof will be effectively utilized; faithfully adhere to the provisions of Section 2 of P.D. No. 1445 in the implementation of the LGU’s various projects, programs and activities with a view that the resources of the government must be safeguarded against loss or wastage; and submit to COA a copy of the Project Proposal of the program.
observations.managementAction: Comply the audit recommendations
observations.reason:
Partially implemented

The LGU had verbally advised the school head to provide the facility the protection against further loss to the project.  Construction activities has commenced.

observations.recommendation: Revisit the provisions of the project.  Discuss with the DA on how the problem be solved with due consideration of the fact that, to date, there had been no suppliers found for the native chicken in which the procurement thereof would not violate the provisions of the procurement law.  However, if the project will not materialize, what other alternative courses of action be done so that the fund allocated thereof could still be used optimally.   Make a suggestion to the DA that projects for implementation in partnership with the Municipality should run through proper discussion and consultation.  This way, the need of the people will be rightfully provided/answered.  Thus, the fund intended thereof will be effectively utilized; faithfully adhere to the provisions of Section 2 of P.D. No. 1445 in the implementation of the LGU’s various projects, programs and activities with a view that the resources of the government must be safeguarded against loss or wastage; and submit to COA the MOA covering the implementation of the project.
observations.managementAction: Implement the project
observations.reason:
Partially implemented

The LGU had already taken steps like travelling to far places to look for possible source of the native chicken.  Nothing integral development had been reported to COA so far.

observations.recommendation: Advise the barangay officials and civil society organization representatives, if there are any, that in identifying priority projects in the unit to be funded from whatever source, select the projects that are necessary, doable and readily implementable. observations.recommendation: Refrain from disbursing funds without corresponding appropriation.
