Implement the project without delay in accordance with the provisions thereof.  If the conduct of a meeting with the representatives of the NHA is warranted to commence the activity, do so immediately; submit to COA the MOA or the project study covering the implementation of the project; enjoin the MPDC to monitor the timely implementation of the various projects of the LGU; and adhere to Sections 2, 4(1) and 4(6) of P.D. No. 1445, Item 2 of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 and Item 13.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 in the implementation of projects which involves procurement of land.

Partially implemented

observations.recommendation: Create a plantilla for GSO or designate an employee to the position; Encourage the GSO to practice stocking of a three (3)-month supplies so that immediate needs of the Agency will be addressed; Advise the BAC to prepare the Abstract of Canvass and forward the same to the GSO for the preparation of the PO; Instruct the GSO to prepare the PO with proper and complete information and have it conformed with the supplier.  Remind the GSO to tell the supplier that delivery of the supplies procured must be made direct to the GSO and in accordance with the terms and conditions stated in the PO; Require the Inspectorate Team to inspect the items delivered and prepare IAR if found in order.  Otherwise, return the goods to the supplier if found defective.  The IAR must be given to the GSO for its signature and furnish it, together with the PO and delivery receipt to the Accounting for recording.  It may be allowed that the end-user will prepare the ObR and DV but its preparation must be made after the delivery of the goods and the information therein must be based from the PO, IAR and delivery receipt; Inform the GSO that the received supplies must be properly kept, segregated and tagged and issuance thereof to the end-user must be recorded in a manner provided in Section 122 of the NGAS Manual for LGU; and Faithfully adhere to Section 122 of the NGAS Manual for LGU, Volume I in resolving other matters relating to procurement of supplies and materials.
observations.managementAction: Follow audit recommendations
observations.reason:
Partially implemented

observations.recommendation: Provide at least 5% of the total annual budget for GAD PAPs as prescribed by the JMC; formulate and include only GAD PAPs that would support government’s commitment to promote equality among men and women to resources and opportunities and which specifically addressed gender issues and concerns; categorically include in the establishment of the GFPS the Executive Committee, Technical Working Group and/or Secretariat. observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the concerned officials to submit a written explanation on the failure to submit request for inspection of the property.  Further, submit all the disposal documents of subject building for review and evaluation.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC Municipal Accountant
to prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statements to establish accurate balances
of the Cash in Bank accounts of all the bank accounts of the municipality and submit the same for verification, otherwise cause the suspension of the
salaries of the concerned official and employees.

We recommend that Management strictly observe the guidelines provided under DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017 and ensure that only development projects will be charged to the fund.

We recommend that Management strictly adhere to the rules and regulations on the utilization of the disaster risk and reduction management fund especially in the procurement of items not included in the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund  Investment Plan to avoid disallowance in audit.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Budget Officer to submit written justification and legal basis for the 	grant of rice subsidy to all the employees of the municipality, otherwise the grant of rice subsidy will be disallowed in audit.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to make representation to the National Telecommunication Commission Region VII on the status of the donation of the motor vehicle subjected to repair and provide the OIC Municipal Accountant with such documents if any as basis for recording in the books.  Further, stop the practice of splitting the purchase requisition or contract pursuant to COA Circular 76-41 and require the Bids and Awards Committee to submit written explanation on the splitting of the contract on the repairs of the subject motor vehicle.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer exert effort to collect the rentals including penalties, interests and surcharges and business taxes from delinquent market stall renters/lessees and business owners to generate additional income that the agency can use for the implementation of its projects/programs.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer furnish the OIC Municipal Accountant with the Real Property Tax Account Register (RPTAR) as basis in the establishment and booking up of the RPT  and SET Receivables at the beginning of the year in order to reflect the actual receivables.
We recommend that henceforth the concerned municipal officials adhere strictly to the provisions of RA 7160 in the computation of PS limitation to avoid excess expenditures for Personal Services.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant to prepare monthly bank reconciliation statements for all bank accounts
maintained by the LGU.

Also, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a. Cause the creation of a task force that will take charge in the preparation of the
BRS for the nine LBP accounts setting a time-frame for its completion;
b. Make written representations with the depository banks to ensure prompt
submission of the Bank statements;
c. Compel the Municipal Accountant to maintain an updated subsidiary ledger.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
1) Direct the Municipal Inventory Committee to: conduct a complete physical
count of PPE,
2) Direct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to determine the
validity of the PPE account through reconciliation of the accounting records
and the actual inventory;
3) Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the PPE Ledger Card (PPELC)
for each class of PPE listing therein the data as to its acquisition, description,
custody, estimated life, rate of depreciation, disposal, and other information
about the property, plant and equipment, based on the source documents of the
transactions and drop from the books of accounts the unserviceable properties
after the Waste Material Report and the Inventory and Inspection Report of
Unserviceable Property are duly approved and evaluated by the COA.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a. Require the Municipal Accountant to send within ten (10) days before the
expiration of the prescribed period a written reminder under signature of the
LCE or his duly authorized representative enjoining officials and employees
concerned to settle their cash advance;
b. Direct the Municipal Accountant not to sign claims for additional cash advance
until the previous cash advance is settled;
c. Compel the Municipal Accountant to cause the immediate withholding of
salaries and claims of officers and employees who failed to settle their cash
advances within the prescribed period after being sent notice and demand;
d. Require the Municipal Accountant to stop the practice of offsetting the amount
due to the accountable officer (for reimbursement) against his other
outstanding cash advances;
e. Require the Municipal Accountant to furnish all department heads listings of
all unliquidated cash advances of employees under their charge to enhance
efforts to effect liquidation.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a. Compel the Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant a duly
certified list showing the names of taxpayers and the amounts due and
collectible from them for the current and prior years,;
b. Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare journal entries to record the
uncollected RPT and SET of the current and prior years, and henceforth, to
account for the receivables annually, including discounts granted and penalties
imposed; and
c. Direct the Municipal Assessor to clean up the Assessment Role to establish a
realistic and accurate RPT and SET Receivable.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive should:
1) Compel the Municipal Accountant to review the details of the Due from
NGAs, Due from LGUs and Other Receivable accounts and exert all efforts to
identify the purpose of the fund transfers;
2) Direct the Municipal Accountant to comply with the reportorial requirements
on funds transferred to local or national government agencies or and other
debtors in order to avoid accumulation of unliquidated balances of the
accounts.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive direct the Supply Officer to
require all drivers of official vehicles of the Municipality to prepare and submit duly
accomplished and serially numbered Driver’s Trip Tickets for each trip to the Supply
Officer who in turn will prepare the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption for
submission to COA.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Treasurer to formulate strategies to increase its income from the operation of its water
system and compel the Municipal Engineer to facilitate the immediate completion of
the construction of Municipal Bus Terminal with Market Stalls.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Budget Officer
to ensure that only projects aligned with the purpose/objectives of the 20%
Development Fund be funded from such appropriation. The Municipal Accountant
should likewise see to it that expenses charged against the 20% DF is in accordance
with prescribe guidelines.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive:
a. Direct the Local Risk Reduction Management Council to prepare its risk
reduction and management plan in accordance with the provision of Section
5.0 of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 dated
March 25, 2013;
b. Compel the Municipal Budget Officer to charge disbursement out of the 70%
of LDRRMF only to those that specifically used to support the risk reduction
and management activities of the LGU; and
c. Request the Sangguniang Bayan to issue a resolution to reprogram items in the
Annual Budget and to prepare a revised Annual Investment Plan.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Engineer, Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, Municipal Social
Welfare and Development Officer and Municipal Agricultural Officer to fast-track the
implementation of priority programs, projects and activities funded by the 20% DF,
BUB and LGSF to provide constituents of the benefits due themthem.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive cause the creation of a Local
Council for Protection of Children and compel the Municipal Budget Officer to
provide a budget allocation equivalent to 1% of LGU’s IRA for LCPC plans and
programs.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the members of the Bids
and Awards Committee and the Municipal Engineer to comply with the procurement
procedures prescribed under R.A. 9184 and its implementing rules and regulations. We
further recommend that before effecting payment complete documentation must be
observed.
We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive compel the Municipal
Accountant to form part of the inspectorate team for completed infrastructure project.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Engineer to submit to the
Office of the Auditor all suspension orders or any documents that would warrant the
non-imposition of liquidated damages to the concerned contractors, otherwise, the
Municipal Accountant shall compute the liquidated damages.

We also recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Engineer to prepare a
report on the unperformed portions of the aforementioned infrastructure projects
based on their respective expected completion date.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the officers and
employees to refund the amount collected in the absence of legal basis.

Management should desist and stop granting additional benefits unless in
pursuance of a lawful appropriation and/or other statutory authority.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
1. Require the Municipal Accountant to instruct the personnel in-charge in the
preparation of the remittance of employees and employer due to GSIS to
adhere strictly to the provisions of RA;
2. Require the Municipal Treasurer that the amount withheld shall be remitted to
GSIS in full within 10 days following the end of the month the withholding
was made or the withholding was accrued;
3. Require the concerned employee in-charge in the preparation of GSIS
remittances and municipal accountant to conduct thorough reconciliation with
its records with that of the GSIS.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive directs the Municipal
Accountant to coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer to identify the employees with
unremitted contributions to enable the Municipal Government to settle its obligations.
The Municipal Accountant should also regularly reconcile the amounts withheld and
remitted to Pag-IBIG in order to ascertain the accuracy and reliability of the balances
of Due to Pag-IBIG account in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
1. Direct the Human Resource Management Office to review the duties and
functions of the Job Order Workers so as not to duplicate the duties and
functions of the regular plantilla employees;
2. Direct the Municipal Budget Officer to adjust the rates of Job Order Workers
in compliance to regulations

The Local Chief Executive should consider creating and organizing an Internal
Audit Services, in compliance with R.A. No 4177 and AO 70 to give assurance for a
sound and adequate internal control system in the operations of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive through the GAD Council
prioritize the implementation of the CBMS for the creation of GAD database to serve
as inputs or basis for planning, programming and policy formulation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor through the Municipal Solid
Waste Management Board strictly implement feasible re-use, recycling, and
composting programs and other prescribed procedures of RA No. 9003. Also,
implement the projects, programs and activities listed in the AIP.

We also recommended that the Mayor provide the necessary financial and
technical support in enjoining the barangays to establish their own Materials Recovery
Facility.

We recommended that the Management:
Demand from the officers and employees concerned for the immediate liquidation/settlement of their unliquidated cash advances through issuance of demand letters issued by the Municipal Accountant and noted by the Local Chief Executive, copy furnished the Audit Team. As to those that are no longer connected with the LGU, send demand letters to their last known address. Impose disciplinary actions against them. In case of non-compliance, direct the Municipal Accountant to withhold the payment of their salaries or any money due to them. Otherwise, filing of appropriate charges shall be dealt with them;
Direct the officers and employees concerned to secure certification from the Municipal Accountant that they have no outstanding cash advance before claims for cash advance of travel expenses are processed; and
Adhere strictly to COA Circular No. 97-002 to avoid accumulation of cash advances.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the following officials of the Municipality to undertake the following measures:
Perform periodic reconciliation of stock/property cards to ensure that discrepancies are immediately investigated, cleared and reconciled with the books of accounts. Also the Supply Officer should have a summary of total per account so that it would be easier to trace to what account contributes to the discrepancies noted;
Prepare necessary journal entries to adjust the duly investigated reconciling items in order to reflect the correct account balances; and
Instruct the Office of the Municipal Accountant to continue its effort to locate pertinent source documents to reduce the unidentified balances in the property ledger cards, if any, in order to render a complete and accurate financial report.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to review the transactions and verify where the lumped balances originate and trace the details of the accounts and to immediately perform correcting entries in the affected accounts. Further, proper and regular monitoring must be conducted in the office concerned as to the posting of government transactions to facilitate fair presentation of financial reports.

With the foregoing, it is recommended that the Local Officials, Municipal Budget Officer and Accountant be guided with the projects, programs and activities to be included as part of the POP public safety plan to be guided in the succeeding disbursement of CF.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Budget Officer to follow the provisions stated in Joint Circular No. 2015-001 dated January 8, 2015, to ensure the submission on time the documentary requirements needed in the granting of cash advances for Confidential Fund.

We are reiterating our prior year’s recommendation that the Local Chief Executive directs the Municipal Accountant and other Head of Offices concerned to submit the accounts and other financial reports of the Municipality regularly, on every 10th day of the succeeding month.

We are reiterating our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive instruct the concerned officials to ensure the timely submission of copies of government contracts and each of all the documents forming part thereof within five (5) working days from execution pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009, to ensure timely review and evaluation of the same, so that the Management could be immediately notified of any defects/deficiencies noted thereon.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Budget Officer (MBO) to provide the necessary appropriation representing one percent (1%) of the IRA for programs, projects and activities for the protection of children.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to submit the necessary supporting documents as enumerated under Section 5.16 of COA Circular 2012-001 to determine the legality of granting Collective Negotiation Agreement Incentives to its employees. We further necessitate the submission of any law, circular or rules and regulations from competent authority that authorizes the Municipality to grant cash incentives to its officials and employees.
Moreover, instruct the Disbursing Officer to require the claimant to duly signed the payroll and liquidate in full the cash advance.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to review all disbursements for GSIS, HDMF and PhilHealth to make sure that the monthly contributions and loan amortizations of all employees have been withheld; that no more employees have outstanding balances from the previous years; and that all of the contributions and loan amortizations withheld were remitted. Prompt remittance of these monthly contributions must be observed, in consonance of the aforementioned laws.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, as the Chairman of Municipal Solid Waste Management Board shall operate a sanitary landfill as its final disposal of wastes and to appropriate fund for the program as stipulated in Section 17 of RA 9003. Further, segregation of wastes at source must be strongly implemented and the collection thereof must be properly handled in consonance with Article 2 and 3 of RA 9003. The collection of fees and penalties shall be properly imposed to generate adequate revenue and to cover the costs of implementing solid waste management.

We recommended that requisitions of supplies and materials be consolidated to determine the volume that is subject to bulk bidding by quarter.
We further recommended to review the preparation of the Project Procurement Management Plan (PPMP) and the Annual Procurement Program (APP) on all the supplies and materials of the LGU, to serve as guide in the procurement activity.

Management should formulate a policy on the granting of financial assistance as to how much will be given to its constituents.
The Municipality should instruct the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Office to require the applicants for assistance a complete submission of supporting documents for the assistance being requested as a basis of assessment and recommendation to make sure that only the deserving ones were provided with the necessary assistance to help them cope during crisis situations.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that Management:
To strictly observe the provisions of Joint Circular (JC) No. 2015-01 and the Policies and Guidelines of DILG MC 99-65; and
To refrain from using the appropriations for Peace and Order for purposes other than those authorized under Joint Circular (JC) No. 2015-01 and the Policies and Guidelines of DILG MC 99-65.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to review all disbursements for the purchases of goods, whether expendable or non-expendable, to make sure that the required warranty was retained, in consonance with the provisions of Section 62 of RA 9184. Moreover, if the supplier for this project had already submitted the bank guarantee equivalent to what has been required; the Management are impelled to submit the said document. All of the other lacking original documents mentioned in this observation must also be submitted.
1.5	We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to effect the adjustments/reconciling items in the respective books to present fairly the balances in the financial statements.

2.7	We recommended that Management create a GSO plantilla position and hire a person to fill therein subject to the financial capacity of the LGU.  If not feasible, designate a GSO in accordance with the provisions of Article 20 of R.A. 7160; instruct the inventory committee/team to spearhead, plan and conduct physical count of the Municipality’s supplies and properties and to prepare and submit to the Audit Team Report on Physical Count of PPE and other properties pursuant to Section 124 of NGAS Manual for LGU; advise the Municipal Accountant to maintain Supplies, PPEs and other properties Ledger Cards; direct the Storekeeper to maintain Supplies and Property Cards per category of materials, PPEs and other properties to reconcile balances thereof with the accounting records regularly; and adhere to Sections 119, 120 and 124 of the NGAS Manual, Volume 1 and other pertinent laws, rules and regulations governing the conduct of physical count of properties, maintenance of records and submission of reports thereof to the Auditor.

3.7	We recommended that Management convene with the SMWD officers and revisit the MOA.  Clarify with the SMWD whether the intent of the transaction was indeed a contract of sale.  If in affirmative, the LGU should drop from the books the Water System as part of the PPE without prejudice to the issuance of NS, ND or NC by this Office due to violation by the LGU of the provisions enshrined in COA Circular No. 89-296.  Amend the provisions of the MOA if necessary to conform with the true/correct intention of both parties.  Ensure that the parties should have a uniform interpretation of the provision/stipulation stated in the MOA; and adhere to the provisions and regulations governing disposal/divestment of government properties including its recording in the books.

4.5	We recommended that Management secure a copy of the MOA from source Agencies to cover each of the financial assistance received; make a representation before the source Agency that every financial assistance granted to the LGU should be covered with a MOA; ensure that financial assistance received must be credited to  Liability/Payable account instead of Income account to maintain accountability; and faithfully adhere to the provisions of Section 2 of P.D. No. 1445 and Item 3.0 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 in the receipt of financial assistance/aids from various sources.

5.5	We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit to COA the subsidiary ledger and detailed schedule of the Adjustment account and to prepare and maintain subsidiary ledgers and schedules of the financial accounts of the LGU to provide adequate information regarding the account balances in compliance to Sections 111(1), 112 and 114 of P.D. No. 1445.

6.5	We recommended that Management direct the MDRRMO to allocate 30% QRF separate and distinct from the other allocations in the 5% LDRRMF. This will serve as a standby fund integrating its PAPs in the LDRRMFIP; instruct the MDRRMO to plan and develop doable PAPs and implement the same with a view that optimum use of the fund to reduce disaster risks and build disaster-resilient communities will always be upheld; remind the Accountant/Budget Officer to furnish COA the reports required in Sections V.A(1) and V.B of COA Circular 2014-002 and as shown in the sample form provided in Annex H and I thereof; advise the MDRRMO and the Accountant to prepare and submit monthly reports on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF to COA in a manner prescribed in Item 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002; require the Accountant to disclose in the Notes to FS the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF as prescribed in Item 5.1.16 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 and as shown in the sample form provided in Annex D thereof; and ensure compliance to the provisions of Section 2 of R.A. No. 10121, Item 4 of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1, Sections V.A(1) and V.B of COA Circular No. 2014-002 and Item 5.1.16 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 on the utilization and reporting of the LDRRMF.

7.6	We recommended that Management direct the MSWDO to reassess its manpower vis-à-vis the duties and responsibilities and determine whether the task of overseeing the execution of the LCPC PPAs can be delegated to other qualified personnel in the office. If not, the MSWDO should adopt measures to personally ensure that the LCPC PPAs are implemented and the fund is optimally utilized; stop utilizing LCPC fund for expenses which have no direct effect in the promotion and protection of the children’s best interest; and adhere faithfully to Sections 2 and 15 of R.A. No. 9344 in the implementation of LCPC programs and activities that these shall serve its objectives.

8.5	We recommended that Management advise the  Municipal Treasurer to deposit immediately to the account of the SEF the collections accruing thereof so that this will be immediately available for the utilization/implementation of SEF PAPs; direct the LSB to include only in the budget the PAPs authorized under DECS-DBM-DILG J.C. No. 1 s.1998; and adhere faithfully to Sections 272, 9 and 4 of R.A. No. 7160, R.A. No. 5447 and DECS-DBM-DILG J.C. No. 1 s.1998, respectively, in the allocation and utilization of SEF.

9.5	We recommended that Management encourage every barangay or cluster of barangays through various information drives to establish an MRF where sorting, segregation, composting and recycling of biodegradable, compostable and reusable waste materials will take place as mandated in Section 10 of R.A. No. 9003; fast tract the negotiations with the authorized public official of the Government of Surigao City regarding the co-utilization of its sanitary land fill as temporary dumping site of the Municipality’s solid wastes; advise the Municipal Ecological Solid Waste Management Board (MESWMB) to formulate as soon as possible alternative solutions for disposal of non-recyclable and special wastes in accordance with the provisions of R.A. No. 9003; ensure compliance to Section 10 of the R.A. No. 9003 where the LGU will collect non-recyclable materials and special wastes from the barangays and other sources to be disposed in the designated dump sites; and faithfully adhere to the provisions of R.A. No. 9003 particularly Sections 10, 32 and 33 thereof on the segregation, collection, transportation and disposal of solid waste materials.

10.5	We recommended that Management seek an SB ordinance/resolution allowing the LGU to hire JOs and to incur obligations as a result of availing their services; submit to COA the legal bases on the hiring of JOs and payments of their wages. Non-compliance hereof may result to suspension or disallowance of the amount as circumstances may warrant; and adhere to the requirements stated in Section 77 of R.A. No. 7160 as regards the hiring of JOs and payments of wages thereof.

11.5	We recommended that Management appropriate an amount for GAD PAPs at least 5% of the total annual budget and not based from the regular income of the LGU; establish the LGU GFPS with the inclusion of the Executive Committee and the TWG and/or Secretariat as required in Section 4.1.A.1 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01.  This should be submitted to COA without delay; direct the GFPS to always formulate the LGU GPB based on the GAD Agenda or identified priority gender issues which were derived from the information gathered from the sources mentioned in Sections 4.1.C.2.1 and 4.1.C.1.4 of JMC No. 2013-01; submit to the COA Audit Team a copy of the GPB and AR in a manner prescribed in Item V of COA Circular No. 2014-001; and strictly adhere to the governing provisions set forth in the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 and COA Circular No. 2014-001 in the creation, utilization and reporting of GAD fund.
12.2	We recommended that the Municipal Accountant immediately remit the outstanding payables to avoid incurrence of penalties and charges.
13.1	We recommended that Management submit to COA the documents required in the subject Notices of Suspension and to settle the amount stated in the Notices of Disallowance.

14.5	We recommended that Management implement the PPAs under the 20% DF as planned. Include only in the AIP the PPAs that are considered priority and realizable during the year; find other PPAs in the AIP or other infrastructure projects with deficient in appropriation so that the items in the DF with unused appropriation/savings could be utilized thereto through augmentation or realignment; and adhere faithfully to Section 2 of P.D. No. 1445 and DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 in the utilization of 20% DF.

15.6	We recommended that Management revisit the provisions of the project.  Immediately initiate a meeting with the DA to discuss remedies to the identified problems with due consideration of the fact that, to date, there had been no suppliers found for the delivery of the material Microns UV Plastic.  However, if the project will not be materialized, what other alternative courses of action be done so that the fund allocated thereof could still be used optimally; advise the School Principal to provide, whatever available means to them, minimum care to the project to avoid further loss and deterioration to the structure; make a suggestion to the DA that projects for implementation in partnership with the Municipality should run through proper discussion and consultation with the latter.  This way, the needed materials for the project could be easily/rightfully sourced or procured, among other concerns.  Thus, the fund intended thereof will be effectively utilized; faithfully adhere to the provisions of Section 2 of P.D. No. 1445 in the implementation of the LGU’s various projects, programs and activities with a view that the resources of the government must be safeguarded against loss or wastage; and submit to COA a copy of the Project Proposal of the program.

16.6	We recommended that Management instruct the BAC to secure the lacking bidding documents and submit the same to the Accountant without delay.  Ensure that bidding documents of a particular procurement should be ready and intact at all times; Send a letter to the DA explaining in detail the reasons why the project remained unimplemented to date.  Personal discussion with them may serve the purpose better.  Proper information and consultation between the contracting parties may address pressing issues and problems surrounding the project and will avoid incurrence by the LGU of any penalties and sanctions as provided in the MOA in cases of non-compliance to the prescribed project processes, standards and requirements; and faithfully adhere to the provisions of Section 2 of P.D. No. 1445 in the implementation of the LGU’s various projects, programs and activities with a view that the resources of the government must be safeguarded against loss or wastage.

17.6	We recommended that Management revisit the provisions of the project.  Discuss with the DA on how the problem be solved with due consideration of the fact that, to date, there had been no suppliers found for the native chicken in which the procurement thereof would not violate the provisions of the procurement law.  However, if the project will not materialize, what other alternative courses of action be done so that the fund allocated thereof could still be used optimally.   Make a suggestion to the DA that projects for implementation in partnership with the Municipality should run through proper discussion and consultation.  This way, the need of the people will be rightfully provided/answered.  Thus, the fund intended thereof will be effectively utilized; faithfully adhere to the provisions of Section 2 of P.D. No. 1445 in the implementation of the LGU’s various projects, programs and activities with a view that the resources of the government must be safeguarded against loss or wastage.; and submit to COA the MOA covering the implementation of the project.

18.7	We recommended that Management revisit the Project Proposal of the activity.  Remind the MAO on the objectives of the project and advise him to follow/do all the activities to be undertaken that were stated therein to achieve the goals.  This includes the utilization of the trained municipal personnel to teach the fishermen association the diving skills so that the latter will become knowledgeable and ready to do the job     required of them as provided in the Proposal.  This will result to the use of the purchased diving equipment; advise the MAO and the BUB Focal Person to regularly monitor the condition and performance of the program as well as the other projects and activities, under the DA; and submit to COA the MOA covering the implementation of the program.

19.7	We recommended that Management advise the MAO to revisit the Project Proposal of the program and reconsider the objectives of the project.  This should also be instructed to be done by the association and for the latter to follow/do all the activities to be undertaken that were stated in the Proposal to achieve the goals.  Direct the organization to identify the causes why there was substantial non-compliance to the provisions of the Proposal.  Initiate a conference with the association to discuss matters at hand and seek practical solutions to the issues.  It would be better if representatives from the DA will be in the meeting so that their suggestions will be incorporated in the course of actions; instruct the MAO and the BUB Focal Person to regularly monitor the condition and performance of the project as well as the other programs, projects and activities under the DA.  Monitoring includes ocular inspection, confirming compliance to the provisions in the study brief and agreements and requiring submission of financial reports.  These ensure immediate action to the observed problems for the achievement of the project objectives as well as guarantee profitability and sustainability of the same; and submit to COA the MOA covering the implementation of the project.

20.6	We recommended that Management advise the barangay officials and civil society organization representatives, if there are any, that in identifying priority projects in the unit to be funded from whatever source, select the projects that are necessary, doable and readily implementable. Non-compliance hereof would result to wastage and possible loss of government resources; remind the Local Poverty Reduction Action Team (LPRAT) that before submitting to the DILG RO the list of priority projects for funding, that these should be reviewed carefully whether the same are necessary, doable and readily implementable; ensure compliance to the requirements set forth in Item 5.8 of DBM-DILG-DSWD-NAPC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 7 dated November 3, 2015 in case there is revision/changes in the project; and suggest to the LPRAT and the barangay officials of Barangay Oslao that if possible, to find an amount to be used in the opening and development of the road planned to be concreted so that the latter activity/project will commence as soon as possible.

21.6	We recommended that Management instruct the contractor to finish the two (2) projects without incurring additional delay.  Require them to render overtime if needed.  The projects had been overdue for a long time as such that requests for extension for its construction should no longer be entertained; require the contractor to submit the Executive Order or Memorandum duly signed by the Provincial Governor suspending the extraction of sand and gravel in the province.  The LGU should ensure that every request from the contractor for work suspension will be supported with documents showing the reason or reasons for the action before approving thereof.  The Management should meticulously evaluate the merit of the documents supported whether it warrants the approval of the same. Execute liquidated damages for every day of delay as required in Section 68 of the IRR of R.A. No. 9184; and adhere to Section 2 of Presidential Decree (P.D.) No. 1445, Section 68 of the IRR of R.A. No. 9184 and Item I of LBC No. 109 in the implementation of LGSF projects and other PAPs of the LGU.

22.6	We recommended that Management instruct the municipal engineer to prioritize and finish as soon as possible the DED of the evacuation center so that the conduct of the competitive bidding to determine the contractor will ensue immediately and the construction thereof will soon commence.  The MPDC is enjoined to monitor the timely implementation of the various projects of the LGU.

23.6	We recommended that Management refrain from disbursing funds without corresponding appropriation.  Submit to COA the legal basis on the procurement of land in the amount of P700,000.00 without corresponding budget; stop the payments of claims without complete supporting documents.  Submit to COA a certified photocopy of the Transfer Certificate of Title (TCT) and Tax Declaration (TD) in the name of the procuring entity or previous owner with annotation of sale; coordinate with the previous land owner for the immediate transfer of the certificate of title in the name of the LGU, if such document has not been made/executed yet; revisit the provisions of the MOA or the project study, if there is any.  Implement the project without delay in accordance with the provisions thereof.  If the conduct of a meeting with the representatives of the NHA is warranted to commence the activity, do so immediately; submit to COA the MOA or the project study covering the implementation of the project; enjoin the MPDC to monitor the timely implementation of the various projects of the LGU; and adhere to Sections 2, 4(1) and 4(6) of P.D. No. 1445, Item 2 of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 and Item 13.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 in the implementation of projects which involves procurement of land.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor create a committee that will
conduct physical count of PPE, afterwhich, require the Municipal Treasurer to
prepare the Property Card for each category of assets, in compliance with Sections
119 and 124 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume 1, to ascertain existence and
valuation of the property and establish accountability thereon.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the:

» OIC-Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to
reconcile their records regularly.

> OIC-Municipal Accountant to continue validating previous years’
transactions and effect adjustments to arrive at the correct account
balance.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the
OIC - Municipal Accountant to expedite retrieval of relevant documents to
substantiate the account balance, otherwise remit the amount to the National
Treasury in compliance with EO No. 431, 5. 2005 of the President of the Philippines.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the
OIC - Municipal Accountant to continue his efforts in determining the composition
of the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts to facilitate
reconciliation of both accounts. Also, effect the necessary adjustments in the books
once reconciled. Moreover, maintain Subsidiary Ledger for the accounts in
compliance with COA Circular 2015-009.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the OIC-Municipal
Accountant to expedite the retrieval of the relevant documents afterwhich, transfer
the Construction in Progress account to its appropriate asset accounts to reflect the
correct balance of the accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:

» OIC-Municipal Accountant to record the transfer of unexpended
balance for CY 2017 to Trust Liability-DRRM account and to revert
the amount of £2,218,247.10 pertaining to unexpended balance for
CY 2012 after subsequent enactment of the Sanggunian in compliance
with COA Circular No, 2012-002.

Y

Municipal Treasurer to transfer the cash corresponding to the
unutilized balance of £6,882,176.20 of the calamity fund for CY 2013-
2016 to a special trust fund account in compliance with the same
Circular to avoid misapplication of fund and to ensure that activities
therein are accomplished.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the
OIC-Municipal Accountant to reconstruct the subsidiary ledgers for CY 2013 and
below in compliance with existing regulations to facilitate validation of account
balances.

We recommended that the Acting Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Treasurer to strictly comply with the requirements stated under COA Circular No.
92-389 to prevent the possible re-use of those documents for other claims of same
nature as part of a sound internal control system in the disbursement of funds.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC or its
Secretariat and the Municipal Engineer to submit to the Audit Team all the
required supporting documents as presented in Annex 6 in accordance with R.A.
No. 9184 and COA Circular No 2012-001 and for evaluation. Otherwise, the same
may be suspended in audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the OIC-Municipal
Accountant to submit to the Audit Team all the required supporting documents for
evaluation. Otherwise, the same may be suspended in audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Bids and Awards
Committee (BAC) Secretariat to submit to the Audit Team the copies of the
contracts together with the supporting documents for review, evaluation and
inspection of the projects. Otherwise, the same may be suspended in audit.

Also, instruct the BAC to strictly adhere and comply with the pertinent
provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 on the prompt
submission of POs, perfected contracts and its supporting documents, to ensure
timely review, evaluation and verification thereof.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the OIC- Municipal
Accountant in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer to submit to the Audit
Team the 20 DVs together with their supporting documents for review and
evaluation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer
to:

* submit the list of all on-going government projects/programs/activities
and those that are to be implemented during the year;

« submit the monthly monitoring report in compliance with the above cited
regulations; and

* inform the Auditors that the appropriate project signboards and/or
public notices were already posted within the prescribed period for
validation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Budget
Officer (MBO) to appropriate at least 1% of their IRA for LCPC and provide
programs, projects and activities for the strengthening and implementation of the
PPAs of LCPC as required under DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120,

We recommended that the Municipality, through the Municipal Solid Waste
Management Board, secure the approval of its 10-year Solid Waste Management
Plan from the National Solid Waste Management Commission and make provisions
in their plan for the closure or eventual phase out of the controlled dump sites
within the period specified under the framework and develop and operate a sanitary
landfill site as final disposal site pursuant to the provisions under the above cited
regulations.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor coordinate
with the SB to develop and pass a GAD Code and instruct the MPDO, with the
assistance of the Municipal GAD Focal Point System (MGADFPS) to set up and
maintain a GAD database to comply with the provision of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA
Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the OIC - Municipal
Accountant to remit all taxes withheld within the prescribed period in order for the
Government to utilize the same and free the officials concerned of any liability in
terms of surcharges that may be imposed by BIR.

We recommend that management strictly observe the proper allocation and utilization of the municipality’s LDRRMF to projects and activities in conformity with the NDRRMC/DBM/DILG Joint Circular No. 2013-1.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management issue demand letters to the concerned officers and employees to immediately settle their outstanding cash advances.  Otherwise, cause the withholding of their salary pursuant to the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002.

observations.reason: As of December 31, 2017, the cash advances account had a balance of P180,307.50.

observations.recommendation: We recommend management to submit legal basis to support the disbursement / grant of Christmas Bonus as well as the Anniversary Bonus in the total amount of P1,240,000.00 to prevent disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: Partially implemented
observations.reason: Payment of disallowance was made thru salary deduction.

observations.recommendation: We recommend management to submit legal basis to support the disbursement / grant of Calamity Assistance to the Job Order Laborers in the total amount of P309,150.00 to prevent disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: Partially implemented
observations.reason: Payment of disallowance was made thru salary deduction.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the Municipal Mayor to exhaust all legal remedies against EM Paule Construction and Trading and Poiel Engineering and Management Services and impose / collect the necessary liquidated damages for the failure to complete and deliver the said project on time. Further, consider the idea of terminating the contract due to the default of the contractor.
observations.managementAction: The contract was terminated.
observations.reason: No liquidated damages imposed or collected.

observations.recommendation: We require management to explain why the procurement was made in two separate contracts instead of one time purchase and its preference to procure the same from a local supplier.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented
observations.reason: List of recipient was submitted by management.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management direct the concerned Municipal Accountant to submit to the concerned Auditor those lacking documents of the subject post-audited transactions to avoid suspension and/or disallowance in audit.  Henceforth, the said Municipal Accountant should always see to it that the DVs covering claims against government funds are supported with complete documents pursuant to Section 4 of P. D. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.
observations.reason: Partially implemented

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management:

Submit justification as to why the procurement of 149 cans of “Reliv” food supplement should not be disallowed in audit.

Direct the BAC to review Purchase Requests thoroughly to ascertain compliance of Section 18 of IRR of RA 9184.

Adopt public bidding as the general mode of procurement and resort to alternative method of procurement only in the highly exceptional cases provided for in the IRR of R.A. 9184.

Direct the Municipal Accountant to submit the aforecited documents as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.
observations.managementAction: All procurement was conducted through public bidding in compliance to RA 9184
observations.reason: No justification submitted by the Management

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer make representation to the Provincial Treasurer to confirm the validity and existence of the amount deposited and have this amount withdrawn thereat and be deposited in the municipality’s depository bank.  Also, refrain from using account title not prescribed in the Chart of Account in order not to give misleading information to the stakeholders.
observations.managementAction: Management made an inquiry from Provincial Treasury
observations.reason: Awaiting of Provincial Treasury’s verification.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management direct the members of the local finance committee of the Municipality of San Francisco, Cebu to refund honoraria they received as members of said committee for CY 2012.  Henceforth, the giving of honoraria to the members of the local finance committee be discontinued.
observations.managementAction: No action taken from the Management
observations.reason: No refund yet was made by the local finance committee but the practice of granting honoraria was discontinued.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management:

1. Notify the public through signboards of their projects at the project location.

2. Direct the   Municipal Engineer to provide the Commission On Audit through the –undersigned the list of ongoing projects/programs/activities and those that are to be implemented during the year and adhere to the other provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004.
observations.managementAction: No action taken from the Management
observations.reason: Signboard prepared did not conform with the prescribed format under COA Cir. 2013-004.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management enforce immediate settlement of all outstanding cash advances as of December 31, 2013.  Otherwise, the Local Chief Executive should advise in writing the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant to suspend the salaries of concerned accountable officers and employees to compel the settlement of their respective outstanding accounts.
observations.managementAction: Some of the cash advances were already liquidated
observations.reason: As of December 31, 2017, the cash advances account had a balance of P180,307.50.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to make all the necessary adjustments in order to present accurate, complete and reliable financial statements. We require the management to have regular and direct supervision in the accurate preparation and fair presentation of the Financial Statements in accordance to the PPSAS.
observations.managementAction: The Management directed the Accounting Office to effect the corrections and make necessary adjusting entries.
observations.reason: There are still accounts that need for adjustments.

observations.managementAction: The Management directed the GSO to complete physical count in the prescribed format and to reconcile with the books of the Accounting.
observations.reason: Complete physical count of inventories and PPE is still on-going.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend Management to:

a)	Require the employees to refund immediately the unexpended balance of the cash advances and enforce the immediate liquidation of overdue advances by sending demand letters to the concerned otherwise, the Treasurer is advised to withhold or suspend the salaries of the erring accountable officers which had not settle their cash advances as of December 31, 2016 without prejudice to the other sanctions as provided for under the aforementioned regulations.

b)	Direct the Accountant to issue Certification that that previous cash advances have been liquidated and accounted for in the books before granting additional cash advances.

c)	Strengthen measures to demand and assure the recovery of prior year cash advances made by officers and employees in and out of service.

d)	Strictly adhere with the provisions of Sec. 89 PD 1445, COA Cir. No. 97-002 and EO No. 298 and other rulings mentioned above.
observations.managementAction: Continuous deductions of refund from employee’s salary.
observations.reason: There are still outstanding balances at year-end.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Management ensure that procurement of goods and services should be undertaken in pursuant to Rule IV, Section 10 of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184 in order to have a wide dissemination of bid opportunities and participation of different private contracting parties and to obtain the most advantageous price.
We recommend that the Local Chief Executive instruct the concerned officials to ensure the timely submission of copies of government contracts and each of all the documents forming part thereof within five (5) working days from execution pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009, to ensure timely review and evaluation of the same, so that the Management could be immediately notified of any defects/deficiencies noted thereon.
observations.managementAction: The Management adhere to abide RA 9184.
observations.reason: Transactions are still subject for further evaluations.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Members of the Municipal Local School Board:

Prepare a budget with conscious and concerted efforts of observing compliance with the legal and regulatory requirements on SEF budgeting, allocation and utilization and refrain from accommodating expenditures for activities for which the DepEd has the direct mandate to execute; and

Request for inputs from the DepEd officials/School Division Superintendent in the prioritization of SEF projects and expenditures in accordance with the provisions of laws, rules and regulations prescribing the limitations on the use of the fund.
observations.managementAction: The observation is noted. Charging of ineligible expenses in the SEF had been stopped.
observations.reason: Transactions are still subject for further evaluations.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the foregoing, we recommend that Management:

To strictly observe the provisions of RA No. 10121 in planning and implementation of the projects, programs and activities of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office; and

To refrain from using the LDRRMF for purposes other than those authorized under Section 21 of RA 10121 and Section 3 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.managementAction:
This finding is noted.

The Management also committed to observe the guideline as per RA No. 10121 in planning and implementation of the projects, programs and activities of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office.
observations.reason: Transactions are still subject for further evaluations.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant and other Head of Offices concerned to submit the accounts and other financial reports of the Municipality regularly, on every 10th day of the succeeding month.
observations.managementAction: The Management requested the Treasury Office to submit the reports/accounts in the Accounting Office immediately.
observations.reason: Timeliness of submission of Disbursement Vouchers and its supporting documents and the Financial Reports has improved for CY 2017. However, submission of some DVs and its documents are still delayed.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive instruct the concerned officials to ensure the timely submission of copies of government contracts and each of all the documents forming part thereof within five (5) working days from execution pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009, to ensure timely review and evaluation of the same, so that the Management could be immediately notified of any defects/deficiencies noted thereon.
observations.managementAction: This recommendation is noted.
observations.reason: There are still delayed submissions of contracts.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management direct those involved in planning and implementation to monitor closely and hasten the implementation of the projects. Likewise, optimally utilized the 20% Development Fund allocated for the implementation of various projects, programs and activities that are envisioned to help in the attainment of social, economic and environmental development.
observations.managementAction: The Management directed the concerned office to fast-track the implementation of the said projects.
observations.reason: Not all projects were implemented at year-end.

observations.recommendation: We therefore recommend that the Management observe the prudent use of scarce resources and follow the limitations imposed under COA Circular No. 2012-003 dated October 29, 2012 and DILG Memorandum Circular No.2011-59 dated April 25, 2011 in order to minimize the cost of the conduct of these activities and save funds for other relative development projects.
observations.managementAction: The Management refrain from having an activity outside Region.
observations.reason: Transactions are still subject for further evaluations.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to withheld the amount of Php442,514.31 during the payment of the contract, otherwise, a Notice of Disallowance will be issued on the same amount. observations.managementAction: Reconcile noted variances between cashbook and ledger

observations.recommendation: Advise the Inventory Team or the Acting Property Custodian to revise the list of properties which must include the needed information mentioned above and to submit the same to COA so that it can be reviewed and reconciled by the latter against the record of the accounting.  The same report can also be used by the Audit Team to ascertain the existence, ownership and valuation of the LGU properties.

Not implemented

observations.recommendation: Construct or maintain a secured shelter or room where to store the unserviceable properties awaiting for disposal; Create a disposal committee who will spearhead in planning and implementing disposal procedures in accordance with the existing guidelines of disposing government properties; Dispose all unserviceable properties per recommendation of the disposal committee for added income of the LGU; Inspect and evaluate from time to time whether these private vehicles in the municipal garage are occupying for an official business.  Private vehicles without legit purpose should be removed from the garage and/or returned to the prospective owner.  The space vacated shall be utilized for municipal vehicles only.  Municipal vehicles should be always parked in the covered garage when not in use; Strictly observe Section 2 of P.D. No. 1445 in utilizing government scarce resources.
observations.managementAction: Follow audit recommendations
observations.reason:
Partially implemented

The covered garage was already deserted with private vehicles.  Others unserviceable properties were already kept in an unutilized room but some remained in unsecured areas. Further, most of the LGU vehicles were still parked in the uncovered places and  Disposal committee was still not created.

observations.recommendation: Prepare and issue a well-defined set of guidelines/policy governing the implementation of AICS program which must be aligned with the provisions of DSWD Administrative Orders No. 5 and 1 s. 2008 and 2011, respectively; prepare a determinable appropriation specifically for the AICS program; all applicants must be required to submit documents to establish identification of the claimants and the validity and legality of the claims.  Supporting documents to the disbursements must be complete and duly filled up; advise the MSWD that keeping of records on AICS program is paramount as one of the standard operating procedures in government transactions; set up a revolving fund to expedite delivery of service.  Custody of such fund must be devolved to the Municipal Treasury; and faithfully adhere to the intent of Section 2 of P.D. No. 1445 in the implementation of any program of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: Promulgate policies to govern AICS
observations.reason:
Partially implemented

Draft comprehensible set of policies/guidelines governing grants of financial assistance to individuals in crisis situation (AICS) had already been made.

observations.recommendation: Execute comprehensible guidelines/policies or MOA governing voluntary contributions by the LGU to various schools; ensure that payments of voluntary contributions to schools must be debited to Due from NGAs to maintain accountability; and faithfully adhere to the provisions of Section 2 of P.D. No. 1445 and Item 3.0 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 in the grant of financial aids to various individuals and/or institutions.
observations.managementAction: Execute guidelines or MOA for the LGU voluntary contributions to schools
observations.reason:
Partially implemented

The Office of the Mayor had already initiated the drafting of the comprehensible guidelines or Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) covering the program.  Other audit recommendations were still not complied.

observations.recommendation: Execute set of guidelines or MOA governing the fund transfers for operational fund to the barangays; ensure that operational funds to the barangays must be debited to Due from LGUs, an asset account instead of having them expensed outright to maintain accountability; and faithfully adhere to the provisions of Section 2 of P.D. No. 1445 and Item 3.0 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 in the grant of financial aids to various individuals and/or institutions.
observations.managementAction: Execute guidelines or MOA for utilization of Operational Fund
observations.reason:
Partially implemented

Draft of the comprehensible set of guidelines/policies covering the operational fund had already been made.  The rest of the recommendations remained uncomplied to date.

observations.recommendation: Immediately call the attention of the department heads concerned to timely prepare and post to the bulletin boards allotted for the purpose the required documents/reports enumerated in DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2011-134 in a manner prescribed in the provisions thereof.
observations.managementAction: Update and post the documents or reports
observations.reason:
Partially implemented

Updated documents/ reports were only posted in the bulletin board located in the municipal hall.  The bulletin board in the municipal gymnasium was not updated

observations.recommendation: Refrain from charging contributions for HIPADA from the 20% DF and submit to COA all documents as legal bases of the foregoing payments including the MOA covering the Project/Activity. Failure to comply may cause this Office to issue a Notice of Suspension or Notice of Disallowance as circumstances may warrant.
observations.managementAction: The Agency had appropriated 5% for GAD budget.  The GFPS had initiated already to include only GAD issues in the GPB.  Other audit recommendations are still not complied.
observations.reason:
Partially implemented

Payments for HIPADA were not noted as of validation date.  However, submission of the legal basis to COA were not complied to date.

observations.recommendation: Create a plantilla for GSO or designate an employee to the position; Encourage the GSO to practice stocking of a three (3)-month supplies so that immediate needs of the Agency will be addressed; Advise the BAC to prepare the Abstract of Canvass and forward the same to the GSO for the preparation of the PO; Instruct the GSO to prepare the PO with proper and complete information and have it conformed with the supplier.  Remind the GSO to tell the supplier that delivery of the supplies procured must be made direct to the GSO and in accordance with the terms and conditions stated in the PO; Require the Inspectorate Team to inspect the items delivered and prepare IAR if found in order.  Otherwise, return the goods to the supplier if found defective.  The IAR must be given to the GSO for its signature and furnish it, together with the PO and delivery receipt to the Accounting for recording.  It may be allowed that the end-user will prepare the ObR and DV but its preparation must be made after the delivery of the goods and the information therein must be based from the PO, IAR and delivery receipt; Inform the GSO that the received supplies must be properly kept, segregated and tagged and issuance thereof to the end-user must be recorded in a manner provided in Section 122 of the NGAS Manual for LGU; and Faithfully adhere to Section 122 of the NGAS Manual for LGU, Volume I in resolving other matters relating to procurement of supplies and materials.
observations.managementAction: Follow audit recommendations
observations.reason:
Not implemented

To date, there was no GSO hired nor designated.  Other audit recommendations were also not complied

observations.recommendation: Fast tract the acquisition and preparation of the sanitary land fill to be used as intended; Encourage every barangay or cluster of barangays to establish an MRF where sorting, segregation, composting and recycling of biodegradable, compostable and reusable waste materials will take place. Inform the municipal constituents to avoid throwing garbage anywhere and from making small garbage open dumpsites in any place in the Municipality; Ensure compliance to Section 10 of the R.A. No. 9003 where the LGU will collect non-recyclable materials and special wastes to be placed in the designated dump sites; and Adhere to the provisions of R.A. No. 9003 specifically Sections 10, 32, 33 and 37 thereof.
observations.managementAction: There was no JEV showed as proof of adjustments made
observations.reason:
Partially implemented

It was observed that garbage are already properly taken. There was also dissemination of information to barangay for the establishment of MRF and the immediate purchase of land to be used for land fill.  However, other audit recommendations remained uncomplied to date.

observations.recommendation: Provide at least 5% of the total annual budget for GAD PAPs as prescribed by the JMC; formulate and include only GAD PAPs that would support government’s commitment to promote equality among men and women to resources and opportunities and which specifically addressed gender issues and concerns; categorically include in the establishment of the GFPS the Executive Committee, Technical Working Group and/or Secretariat.  These are responsible in carrying the functions of the Point System; set up and maintain the GAD database to serve as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation; carefully plan, identify and consider gender issues and concerns in the LGU that these may be included in the GPB, with due consideration that optimal utilization of the appropriated amount for the program will be achieved; submit to the concerned government oversight agencies the GPB in prescribed form and in a manner stated in Sections 4.1.C.1 and 4.1.C.8.1 of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC and Item V of COA Circular No. 2014-001; and strictly adhere to the provisions set forth in R.A. 9710 and PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 in the creation and utilization of GAD fund.
observations.managementAction: Follow the audit recommendations
observations.reason:
Partially implemented

The Agency had appropriated 5% for GAD budget.  The GFPS had initiated already to include only GAD issues in the GPB.  Other audit recommendations are still not complied.

observations.recommendation: Convene with the SMWD officers and revisit the MOA.  Clarify with the SMWD whether there is a complete transfer of ownership of the Water System from the LGU to the Water District.  If in affirmative, the LGU should drop from the books the Water System as part of the PPE without prejudice to the issuance of NS, ND or NC by this Office due to violation by the LGU of the provisions enshrined in COA Circular No. 89-296.  Amend the provisions of the MOA if necessary to conform with the true/correct intention of both parties.  Ensure that the parties should have a uniform interpretation of the provision/stipulation stated in the MOA; and Adhere to the provisions and regulations governing disposal/divestment of government properties including its recording in the books.
observations.managementAction: To talk with officials of the SMWD to clarify issues
observations.reason:
Partially implemented

The Management had already initiated to talk with the SMWD to clear up provisions in the MOA and other documents.

observations.recommendation: Require the Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of all inventories and properties owned by the Municipality. The Inventory Committee prepare the prescribed Report of the Physical Count for Inventories (RPCI) and Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) upon completion of the physical count and reconcile the said property with the accounting and property records. Moreover, instruct the Municipal Treasurer to maintain property cards and issue Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment, and the Municipal Accountant to maintain Supplies Ledger Cards and PPE Ledger Cards.
observations.managementAction: Recommendation is well taken.
observations.reason:
Not implemented

The Agency still did not conduct physical count of all its properties as of December 31, 2017

observations.recommendation: Instruct the Municipal Accountant to cause the withholding of the accountable officers concerned salary when no liquidation of the previous cash advances is received on or before January 20 as provided for in Section 5.9 of COA Circular No. 97-002.
observations.managementAction: No additional cash advances had been granted to any employees unless previous cash advance has been settled or liquidated. The remaining balance reflected as of December 31, 2015 represent balances for new cash advances and balances for prior years which are deducted monthly salary on staggered basis. With regards to the confidential fund, liquidation of the same had been sent to COA Central Office waiting for the issuance of credit advice.
observations.reason:
Partially implemented

Some of the outstanding cash advances remained unliquidated

observations.recommendation: Require the Revenue Collectors to remit/turn over daily the collections to the Municipal Treasurer. Otherwise, she will hold/not issue additional accountable forms without the full turn-over of collections. Moreover, direct the Municipal Treasurer to immediately deposit the collections with the authorized government depository bank and stop the practice of retaining collections in her custody. Strengthen the internal control in the collection system by strict compliance with Section 29 to 32 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I and Local Treasury Operations Manual.
observations.managementAction: Recommendation is well taken
observations.reason:
Partially Implemented

observations.recommendation: Require the Liquidating Officer and Municipal Treasurer to update and record all financial transactions of the LGU daily. Direct the Revenue Collectors to maintain cashbook to record all collections received and all remittances to the Liquidating Officer.
observations.managementAction: Recommendation is well taken
observations.reason:
Partially Implemented

Not all cashbooks were updated daily

observations.recommendation: Strictly adhere Section 7, Rule II of the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 and prepare PPMP and consolidated APP. Require the BAC to furnish the Office of the Auditor of the updated PPMP and APP yearly for us to validate and ensure that all procurement undertaken by the LGU were based on the approved annual procurement plan.
observations.managementAction: Recommendation is implemented
observations.reason:
Partially implemented

Not all departments did submit PPMP

observations.recommendation: Stop the practice of Issuing Certificate of Acceptance upon project completion and wait for the one (1) year period from project completion before it is issued to the contractor.
observations.managementAction: Recommendation is well taken
observations.reason:
Partially implemented

The Agency had tried to comply with the requirements at all times

observations.recommendation:
1.	Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to prepare the Report of Checks Issued (RCI) daily and forward the same to the Accounting Office together with the disbursement vouchers and other supporting documents.
2.	Require the Municipal Accountant to submit the RCI together with the DVs and its supporting documents within first ten days of the ensuing month in accordance with the provision of COA Circular No. 2009-006.
3.	Reprimand all accountable officers concerned for the immediate submission of all the RCIs and its supporting documents to the Auditor.  Otherwise, their failure or refusal to submit papers relative to government obligations is a ground for administrative disciplinary action and disallowance of the transactions as provided in Section 39 (1&3) of PD. 1445. Besides, failure to render accounts for a period of two months after such accounts should be rendered is penalized in Article 218 of the Revised Penal Code.
observations.managementAction: Recommendation is well taken
observations.reason:
Partially implemented

The DVs together with the RCI were not submitted within the required period

observations.recommendation: Direct the Municipal Budget Officer and the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management (LDRRM) Officer to submit the required reports immediately and adhere strictly the provisions cited in Section 70 of NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I and Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
observations.managementAction: Recommendation is well taken

Partially implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management should use the 20% Development Fund for those expenditure items in accordance with the provision of Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011 of the Department of the Interior and Local Government and the Department of Budget and Management.
observations.reason: Reiterated in CY 2016
