observations.reason: In CY 2016, the municipality still exceeded PS limitation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the concerned Municipal Mayor direct the persons liable to settle immediately the amount disallowed in audit amounting to P4,254,143.00 for the payment of cash incentives to its officers and employees including the contractual personnel.
observations.managementAction: The Management already filed an appeal of the Notice of Disallowance last CY 2016
observations.reason: Under appeal

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to see to it that all expenditures for repairs be supported with complete documentation and ensure proper observance of the procedures and to avoid incurrence of excessive expenditures. Further, require the personnel in charge at the motor pool to initiate the maintenance of the individual records of the equipment and adopt measures to protect the equipment and motor vehicles of the municipality.
observations.managementAction: The management complied  the COA recommendation and Mayor decides to buy new vehicles instead of repairing the old and surplus vehicles.
observations.reason: As of Dec. 2016, Repairs and Maintenance – Heavy Equipt – P1,375,475.15 and Repairs &Maint – Transpo – P1,440,823.15.

observations.recommendation: We recommend management to reconcile the result of the physical count of the Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) with the accounting records and effect necessary adjustments thereof, preferably before the year ends so as not to reiterate this observation.  Further, cause the preparation of the subsidiary ledgers / cards which shall be kept for each class of PPE to record the description, acquisition, transfer/disposal and other information about the asset. Also, prepare the lapsing schedule of depreciation for each item of the PPE.
observations.managementAction: Physical inventory was conducted as of December 31, 2016.
observations.reason: Total PPE per physical inventory did reconcile with the amount per books.  As of December 31, 2016, the PPE per books amounted to P112,943,829.52 while the amount per inventory is only P92,332,166.92 or a difference of P20,611,662.60

observations.recommendation: Require management to exhaust all the legal remedies to require the San Francisco farmers and Fishermen’s Trading Association to settle the long overdue loan including the interest as provided in the memorandum of Agreement.
observations.managementAction: Management had not continuously demanded for payment of the outstanding balance from the association last CY 2016
observations.reason: As of December 31, 2016, the receivable account remained in the books without movement.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the above, we require management to submit explanation/justification on the payment of fuel claim even if the vehicle used is a private vehicle.

Further, we recommend the agency to stop the practice of granting fuel to privately owned vehicle and strictly adhere to the provisions of the Manual on Audit for Fuel Consumption of Government Vehicles.
observations.managementAction: Management justified that the there was no prohibition in using privately owned vehicle in the performance of official function and that it is justifiable that fuel consumptions incidental thereto are also allowed.
observations.reason: As of Nov. 2016, travel claims still include expenses for fuel amounting to P42,827.74  .

observations.recommendation: We recommend the Accountant to make the necessary adjustments to correct the erroneous recording of the above transactions. The NGAS Chart of Accounts should always be used as the basis in the proper use of accounts and in the recording of transactions. Proper care should always be exercised in the recording of financial information to reflect a fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Management replied that the said recommendation will be implemented in CY 2016.
observations.reason: Per verification, the same were not yet implemented.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend the following courses of action to rectify the foregoing deficiencies:
The Inventory Committee be directed to segregate  the unserviceable properties and equipment from those properties which are still serviceable but no longer needed by the agency and enter the same  in the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) pursuant to Section 59, Volume I of LGU NGAS, Manual;
observations.managementAction: Physical inventory of PPE was conducted but the identification / segregation of unserviceable properties were not yet done.
observations.reason: As of December 31, 2016, management had not acted on the disposal of various unserviceable properties kept at the motorpool.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant verify the Construction in Progress and other affected accounts and effect the necessary adjustments in order to reflect the correct balances in the Financial Statements. Also, require the maintenance of the Registry of Public Infrastructures and monitor accounts/assets not recorded in the books of accounts and cause the preparation of the subsidiary ledgers to substantiate the recorded balances in the Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: The recommendation will be implemented in CY 2016.
observations.reason: As of December 31, 2016, the financial statement of the municipality showed a CIP accounts of P9,763,370.22.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to conduct a thorough verification of the dormant accounts recorded in the books and take necessary action to resolve the issue.
observations.managementAction: The recommendation will be implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant be directed to verify the Due From and Due To Other Funds and other affected accounts and effect the necessary adjustments in order to reflect the correct balances of the controlling accounts and in the Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: For implementation in CY 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant should provide for a yearly depreciation of the Property, Plant and Equipment pursuant to Section 335 to 359 of NGAS Manual, Vol. 3 for LGUs
observations.managementAction: No depreciation was provided.
observations.reason: The agency failed to compute depreciation on all depreciable assets.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the management refrain from using the 20% Development Fund in paying expenditure items enumerated under Section 4 of the Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011 of the Department of the Interior and Local Government and the Department of Budget and Management.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by the Management
observations.reason: Reiterated in CY 2016 AAR

observations.recommendation: We recommend management to stop immediately paying employer’s share to the Fund in excess of what is required by law and instead comply strictly with the above regulations effective immediately to avoid disallowance in audit.   Also, coordinate with Pag-ibig on the possible application of the over remittance of employer’s (government) share for succeeding dues on monthly contribution, otherwise, the excessive remittance shall be disallowed in audit pursuant to Section 103 of P.D. 1445.
observations.managementAction: No action taken from the management
observations.reason: Awaiting for the response

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the construction materials totaling P1,036,304.00 purchased for the improvement of the Auction Area of the Municipal Abattoir and Roof Deck of the Public Market be capitalized to fairly present the Property, Plant and Equipment account in the financial statements. The Municipal Accountant must be reminded that any improvements, alteration, replacements, major repairs, etc., when it extends the life, increase the capacity or improve the efficiency or safety of the property must be capitalized.
observations.reason: No action taken by the management

observations.recommendation: Refund the amount and refrain from disbursing honoraria not in accordance with Budget Circular No. 2004-5 dated March 23, 2004
observations.managementAction: Management commit to demand from the concerned employees for the refund of the honorarium received.

observations.recommendation:
The Municipal officials concerned should refund immediately the disallowed honorarium amounting to P106,052.50.

Appropriate sanctions must be filed against the officials who deliberately collected the honorarium and those who authorized the collection thereof despite their knowledge of its irregularity.
observations.managementAction: Management commit to demand from the concerned employees for the refund of the honorarium received.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend to the Local Chief Executive to follow the provisions stated in Joint Circular No. 2015-001 dated January 8, 2015 on the Salient Features and Guidelines on the Confidential Fund.

Likewise, we recommend to the Municipal Accountant and the Budget Officer to advise the Local Chief Executive on the documentary requirements needed in the granting of additional cash advances for Confidential Fund.
observations.managementAction: The Management followed this recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
Enjoin Management to refrain from using LDRRM Funds to activities which are not related to the purpose of the fund.  We further recommended that the maintenance and operating expenses of the LDRRM Office be given a separate appropriation that maybe under the Mayor’s Office.

The Municipal Accountant and Budget Officer are enjoined to meticulously check the propriety of the transaction/charges before certifying the availability of appropriations and the validity and completeness of the transactions.
observations.managementAction: This audit finding is noted.

observations.recommendation:
Enjoin Management to prioritize those programs, projects and activities stipulated in RA 7160.

Henceforth, utilities, office supplies and traveling expenses of staff and teachers, as well as, expenses in relation to travel activities of LGU and LGU employees will be charged to their specific appropriations, otherwise, the same shall be disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: Management had already refrained from charging Special Education Fund to Program/Project/Activities which are not stipulated in RA 7160.

observations.recommendation: We recommend to Management to observe utmost economy by refraining from conducting LGU activities outside the locality so that other programs, projects, activities beneficial to the community can be prioritized.
observations.managementAction: Noted and trainings were approved by the DILG.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and
Management agreed to return the unutilized PDAF of
P3,500,000.00 to the Bureau of Treasury pursuant to the
Supreme Court Decision dated November 19, 2013.
observations.managementAction: No additional comment.
observations.reason: Not implemented

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and
Management agreed to reduce the amount of P4,000.00
monthly communication allowance granted to all
municipal heads and sangguniang bayan members to the
reasonable limits that an official may consume for
official business transaction.
observations.managementAction: is well taken.
observations.reason: Not implemented

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and
Management agreed to require the Revenue Collectors to
remit/turn over daily the collections to the Municipal
Treasurer. Otherwise, she will hold/not issue additional
accountable forms without the full turn-over of collections.
Moreover, direct the Municipal Treasurer to immediately
deposit the collections with the authorized government
depository bank and stop the practice of retaining collections
in her custody. Strengthen the internal control in the collection
system by strict compliance with Section 29 to 32 of the
NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I and Local Treasury
Operations Manual.
observations.managementAction: During cash count, it was noticed that the revenue collectors still did not remit collections to the liquidating officer on a daily basis
observations.reason: Not implemented

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and
Management agreed to require the Liquidating Officer and
Municipal Treasurer to update and record all financial
transactions of the LGU daily. Direct the Revenue Collectors
to maintain cashbook to record all collections received and all
remittances to the Liquidating Officer.
observations.managementAction: is well taken.
observations.reason: During cash count, it was observed that the liquidating officer and municipal treasurer still did not update and balance daily

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and
Management agreed to direct the Municipal Budget Officer and the Local Disaster Risk
Reduction Management (LDRRM) Officer to submit the required reports immediately and
adhere strictly the provisions cited in Section 70 of NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I and
Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
observations.managementAction: is well taken
observations.reason: Submission of the reports were still not made in a manner prescribed by rules and regulations

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the


           ➢ Acting Municipal Accountant  and  the  Municipal  Treasurer  to
             reconcile their records regularly.


           ➢ Acting Municipal Accountant  to  continue  validating  previous
             years’ transactions and effect adjustments  to  arrive  at  the
             correct account balance.

We recommended that the  Municipal  Mayor  require  the  Municipal
   Accountant to revert the unsubstantiated payables to  the  Unappropriated
   Surplus of the General Fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Acting
   Municipal Accountant to  expedite  retrieval  of  relevant  documents  to
   substantiate the account balance,  otherwise  remit  the  amount  to  the
   National Treasury in compliance with EO No. 431, s. 2005 of the President
   of the Philippines.

We  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Mayor  require  the  Acting
   Municipal  Accountant  to  continue  his  efforts  in   determining   the
   composition of the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other  Funds  accounts
   to facilitate reconciliation of both accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:


           ➢ Municipal Assessor to  furnish  the  Municipal  Treasurer  with
             Assessment Roll;


           ➢ Municipal Treasurer to furnish the  Municipal  Accountant  with
             Real Property Tax Account Register/Taxpayers index card at  the
             beginning of the year, in compliance with Section  20,  Chapter
             3, Volume l of the MNGAS.

We recommended that the  Municipal  Mayor  require  the  Municipal
   Accountant to  continuously  exert  efforts  to  locate  the  records  to
   identify the borrowers and prepare subsidiary ledgers in support  of  the
   account balance afterwhich, collect the receivables due from borrowers.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor  require
   the Acting Municipal Accountant to expedite the retrieval of the relevant
   documents afterwhich, transfer the Construction in  Progress  account  to
   its appropriate asset accounts to reflect  the  correct  balance  of  the
   accounts.

We  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Mayor  require  the  Acting
   Municipal Accountant to prioritize the validation of Due to BIR, Due  to
   GSIS and Due to Pag-IBIG balances to reflect the correct balances of  the
   accounts.

We recommended that the  Municipal  Mayor  require  the  Municipal
   Treasurer to remit to the National Treasury the  balance  of  P254,940.00
   representing unutilized funds of completed projects funded out of PDAF in
   compliance with the Supreme Court Decision on the illegality of PDAF  and
   EO No.  431 and Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445.

We  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Mayor  require  the  Acting
   Municipal  Accountant  to reconstruct the subsidiary ledgers  for  CY  2013
   and  below  in  compliance  with  existing  regulations   to   facilitate
   validation of account balances.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor  establish  the  LCPC  and
   require the Municipal Budget  Officer  (MBO)  to  provide  the  necessary
   appropriation representing one percent of the IRA for programs,  projects
   and activities for the protection of children.

We recommend that Management strictly observe the proper allocation and utilization of the Municipality’s LDRRMF to projects and activities in conformity with the NDRRMC/DBM/DILG Joint Circular No. 2013-1.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor ensure that the 20% Development Fund be utilized to fund priority programs or projects which are capital in nature in accordance with the provisions of DILG-DBM Joint memorandum Circular No. 2011-1.

We recommend that the Municipal Engineer comply the provisions of Circular No. 0213-004 and ensure regular submission of the list of projects at the beginning of the year in proper format as well as the quarterly information of PPAs of the municipality.

We recommend that  implementation of programs/projects/activities budgeted in lump sum should have the prior approval by the Sangguniang Bayan pursuant to the Supreme Court ruling in the case of Hon. Luis Quisumbing, et al. vs. Hon. Gwendolyn F. Garcia, et. al, GR No. 175527 promulgated on 08 December 2008.

We recommend that Management strictly observe the provisions of Section 313 of RA 7160requiring submission of the financial statements pertaining to the economic enterprises to COA within the prescribed period.

We recommend that for those cash advances granted after December 31, 2011 that has remained unliquidated, Management issue demand letters to the concerned officers and employees to immediately settle their outstanding cash advances or face the consequences of the suspension of their salaries and/or filing of malversation case against them.

We recommend further that Management institute appropriate sanctions pursuant to Section 9 of COA Circular No. 2012-004 against those employees whose cash advances were granted prior to December 31, 2011 but have remained unliquidated to date despite final demand stated under Section 6 thereof.

We recommended that the LCE cause the creation of an Inventory
Committee and instruct them to commence the conduct of the physical inventory of all
LGU properties.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to strictly
observe COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 31, 2015 and draw the
suggested adjusting entry to correct the overstatement of PPE account, to wit:

DR CR
Prior Year’s Adjustment P3,800,000.00
Disaster Response and Rescue Equipment
P3,800,000.00

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant to strictly observe the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-001 and that
all fund transfers received must have a Memorandum of Agreement with the
contracting party to include a provision specifying the duties and responsibilities of
the municipality and the third-party beneficiaries. The MOA must also include a work
and financial plan that would identify and address all details of the project.

We enjoined the local officials to utilize the unexpended balance of the
LDRRM fund for disaster risk reduction and management program, project &
activities.

We recommended for the fast-tracking of implementation of development
projects to provide constituents the benefits due them.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the corresponding
disbursement vouchers together with the related supporting documents pertaining to
the one hundred seventy-three (173) checks totaling P4,144,389.63. Henceforth, they
must see to it that the reports submitted are complete and intact.

We therefore required the concerned LGU personnel to (1) to submit a written
explanation why the negotiated procurement (community participation) for the above
projects were conducted by CENRO-San Juan and not by LGU San Francisco, (2)
submit a written explanation why the mode of procurement was negotiated when the
contract cost for the establishment of cacao plantation was above the threshold set
forth by the Manual on Community Participation, submit a justification for the
participation of the LCE in the opening of bids on the procurement/production of
seedlings at CENRO San Juan; (3)Explain the reason behind resorting to direct
contracting with Catmon Community-Based Forest Management Project Development
Organization (CCBFMPDO), represented by Valerio B. Raagas, for the two projects
even in the absence of the required conditions to validate this mode of procurement;
(4)Submit the Final report accompanied with a map indicating the
established/accomplished area, list of actual recipients and respective area developed
prepared by LGU San Francisco and the accomplishment and terminal report
supported with GIS generated maps and photographs prepared by DENR.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Local Treasurer
and Accountant to observe strictly COA Circular No. 2012-001 and that all fund
transfers must have a Memorandum of Agreement to include a provision that the
infrastructure project must be validated upon completion through conduct of
inspection by the Municipal Engineer and prepare an inspection report based on the
statement of work accomplished by the barangay to be attached to the liquidation
report.

We recommended that the Local Chief Ezecutive require the Municipal
Treasurer to see to it that all AOs be covered by fidelity bond.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Budget
Officer and Municipal Planning and Developement Coordinator to strictly adhere to
Section 287 of RA 7160, Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 Budget Circular No.
2007-03 and GPPB Resolution No. 18-2012; and ensure that only projects aligned
with the purpose/objectives of the 20% Development Fund be funded from such
appropriation.

We recommended that the LCE cause the discontinuance of the payment of
communication allowance to the member of the Information Board pursuant to COA
Circular No. 2012-003 dated October 29, 2012.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, being the chair of the Local
School Board advise the observance of the provisions of Section 272 of RA 7160 and
recommend to the school administrators in their respective locality the revision of
their school improvement plan to include expenses enumerated under DepEd Order
No. 12, s. 2012.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive cause the provision of
financial and technical assistance to the barangays for the establishment and
operations of functional and standard-based MRFs.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: cause the
discontinuance of the use of open dump sites and enjoin the Sanguniang Bayan
to pass an ordinance for “No open Burning” and “No Littering” policies.

We recommended that in future similar projects to be implemented by the LGU,
the LCE, the BAC and the Municipal Engineer should ensure that funds are
available and complete detailed engineering is undertaken before projects are
implemented to avoid delay in the delivery of basic services

1.6	We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to make all the necessary adjustments in order to present accurate, complete and reliable financial statements. We require the management to have regular and direct supervision in the accurate preparation and fair presentation of the Financial Statements in accordance to the PPSAS.

3.7	We recommend Management to:
Require the employees to refund immediately the unexpended balance of the cash advances and enforce the immediate liquidation of overdue advances by sending demand letters to the concerned otherwise, the Treasurer is advised to withhold or suspend the salaries of the erring accountable officers which had not settle their cash advances as of December 31, 2016 without prejudice to the other sanctions as provided for under the aforementioned regulations.
Direct the Accountant to issue Certification that that previous cash advances have been liquidated and accounted for in the books before granting additional cash advances.
Strengthen measures to demand and assure the recovery of prior year cash advances made by officers and employees in and out of service.
Strictly adhere with the provisions of Sec. 89 PD 1445, COA Cir. No. 97-002 and EO No. 298 and other rulings mentioned above.

4.4	We recommend that the Management ensure that procurement of goods and services should be undertaken in pursuant to Rule IV, Section 10 of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184 in order to have a wide dissemination of bid opportunities and participation of different private contracting parties and to obtain the most advantageous price.
4.6 	We are reiterating our recommendation as stated per par. 4.4 above.

5.5	We recommend that the Members of the Municipal Local School Board:
Prepare a budget with conscious and concerted efforts of observing compliance with the legal and regulatory requirements on SEF budgeting, allocation and utilization and refrain from accommodating expenditures for activities for which the DepEd has the direct mandate to execute; and
Request for inputs from the DepEd officials/School Division Superintendent in the prioritization of SEF projects and expenditures in accordance with the provisions of laws, rules and regulations prescribing the limitations on the use of the fund.
5.7	We are reiterating our recommendation as stated in par. 5.5 above.

6.7	In view of the foregoing, we recommend that Management:
To strictly observe the provisions of RA No. 10121 in planning and implementation of the projects, programs and activities of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office; and
To refrain from using the LDRRMF for purposes other than those authorized under Section 21 of RA 10121 and Section 3 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

7.5	We recommend that the Municipality should implement the provisions of COA Circular 2007-001 and ensure proper granting, utilization and liquidation of fund transfers.
Moreover, the Municipal Accountant should review all outstanding accounts and record adjustments for fund transfers that actually partake of the nature of donations.
Further, the Municipality should institute action against NGO/PO’s and their officers/organizers that failed to implement the programs and projects funded by the Municipal government.

8.6	We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant and other Head of Offices concerned to submit the accounts and other financial reports of the Municipality regularly, on every 10th day of the succeeding month.

9.5	We recommend that the Local Chief Executive instruct the concerned officials to ensure the timely submission of copies of government contracts and each of all the documents forming part thereof within five (5) working days from execution pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009, to ensure timely review and evaluation of the same, so that the Management could be immediately notified of any defects/deficiencies noted thereon.

10.4	We recommend that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to follow the provisions stated in Joint Circular No. 2015-001 dated January 8, 2015, to ensure the submission on time the documentary requirements needed in the granting of cash advances for Confidential Fund.

11.5	We recommend that Management direct those involved in planning and implementation to monitor closely and hasten the implementation of the projects. Likewise, optimally utilized the 20% Development Fund allocated for the implementation of various projects, programs and activities that are envisioned to help in the attainment of social, economic and environmental development.

12.8	We therefore recommend that the Management observe the prudent use of scarce resources and follow the limitations imposed under COA Circular No. 2012-003 dated October 29, 2012 and DILG Memorandum Circular No.2011-59 dated April 25, 2011 in order to minimize the cost of the conduct of these activities and save funds for other relative development projects.

13.3  We recommend that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to withheld the amount of Php442,514.31 during the payment of the contract, otherwise, a Notice of Disallowance will be issued on the same amount.  Moreover, instruct the Municipal Engineer, the Bids and Awards Committee and the Technical Working Group to meticulously review all the documents and the basis of the Approved Budget of the Contract before proceeding to the procurement process. Further, require the Municipal Engineers to update themselves with the rules and regulations relative to the new issuances issued by the DPWH on the implementation of the Infrastructure projects.

14.3 We recommend that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to withheld the amount of Php542,961.31 during the payment of the contract, otherwise, a Notice of Disallowance will be issued on the same amount.  Moreover, instruct the Municipal Engineer, the Bids and Awards Committee and the Technical Working Group to meticulously review all the documents and the basis of the Approved Budget of the Contract before proceeding to the procurement process. Further, require the Municipal Engineers to update themselves with the rules and regulations relative to the new issuances issued by the DPWH on the implementation of the Infrastructure projects.

15.4 We recommend that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to withheld the amount of Php151,783.07 during the payment of the contract, otherwise, a Notice of Disallowance will be issued on the same amount.  Moreover, instruct the Municipal Engineer, the Bids and Awards Committee and the Technical Working Group to meticulously review all the documents and the basis of the Approved Budget of the Contract before proceeding to the procurement process. Further, require the Municipal Engineer to update themselves with the rules and regulations relative to the new issuances on the implementation of the Infrastructure projects.

16.4 We recommend that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to withheld the amount of Php180,739.81 during the payment of the contract, otherwise, a Notice of Disallowance will be issued on the same amount.  Moreover, instruct the Municipal Engineer, the Bids and Awards Committee and the Technical Working Group to meticulously review all the documents and the basis of the Approved Budget of the Contract before proceeding to the procurement process. Further, require the Municipal Engineer to update themselves with the rules and regulations relative to the new issuances on the implementation of the Infrastructure projects.

17.9  We recommend that the Local Chief Executive:
Require the contractor to rectify the deficiencies of the project, otherwise, refund the amount of Php29,181.60 to the Municipality;
Inform the contractor on the structural defects and require the same to correct the construction deficiencies at no cost to the Municipality; and
Direct those concerned in the implementation of the projects to give emphasis on the quality of all on-going and upcoming projects so that comfort of the populace at large can be provided.

1.5 We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant
and Treasurer to effect the required reconciliation and to perform the same
regularly, pursuant to Section 181 (c) of the GAAM, Volume I, to present fairly
the cash balances in the financial statements.

2.5 We recommended that Management advise the Inventory Team or the
Acting Property Custodian to revise the list of properties which must include
the needed information mentioned above and to submit the same to COA so that
it can be reviewed and reconciled by the latter against the record of the
accounting. The same report can also be used by the Audit Team to ascertain
the existence, ownership and valuation of the LGU properties.

3.6 We recommended that Management construct or maintain a secured
shelter or room where to store the unserviceable properties awaiting for
disposal; Create a disposal committee who will spearhead in planning and
implementing disposal procedures in accordance with the existing guidelines of
disposing government properties; Dispose all unserviceable properties per
recommendation of the disposal committee for added income of the LGU;
Inspect and evaluate from time to time whether these private vehicles in the
municipal garage are occupying for an official business. Private vehicles
without legit purpose should be removed from the garage and/or returned to the
prospective owner. The space vacated shall be utilized for municipal vehicles
only. Municipal vehicles should be always parked in the covered garage when
not in use; Strictly observe Section 2 of P.D. No. 1445 in utilizing government
scarce resources.

4.5 We recommended that Municipal Accountant record expenses in the
year they are incurred. Unpaid expenditures should be taken up in the books as
payables. Strictly adhere to the provisions of Sections 4(8) and 119 of P.D. 1445
when recognizing financial transactions in the books.

5.6 We recommended that Management prepare and issue a well-defined set
of guidelines/policy governing the implementation of AICS program which must
be aligned with the provisions of DSWD Administrative Orders No. 5 and 1 s.
2008 and 2011, respectively; prepare a determinable appropriation specifically
for the AICS program; all applicants must be required to submit documents to
establish identification of the claimants and the validity and legality of the
claims. Supporting documents to the disbursements must be complete and duly
filled up; advise the MSWD that keeping of records on AICS program is
paramount as one of the standard operating procedures in government
transactions; set up a revolving fund to expedite delivery of service. Custody of
such fund must be devolved to the Municipal Treasury; and faithfully adhere to
the intent of Section 2 of P.D. No. 1445 in the implementation of any program of
the LGU.

6.6 We recommended that Management execute comprehensible
guidelines/policies or MOA governing voluntary contributions by the LGU to
various schools; ensure that payments of voluntary contributions to schools must
be debited to Due from NGAs to maintain accountability; and faithfully adhere
to the provisions of Section 2 of P.D. No. 1445 and Item 3.0 of COA Circular No.
2012-001 in the grant of financial aids to various individuals and/or institutions.

7.6 We recommended that Management execute set of guidelines or MOA
governing the fund transfers for operational fund to the barangays; ensure that
operational funds to the barangays must be debited to Due from LGUs, an asset
account instead of having them expensed outright to maintain accountability;
and faithfully adhere to the provisions of Section 2 of P.D. No. 1445 and Item 3.0
of COA Circular No. 2012-001 in the grant of financial aids to various
individuals and/or institutions.

8.5 We recommended that the Local School Board plan, consider and
include in the budget only PPAs which are among those authorized under the
provisions of DECS-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1 and within the tenet
enshrined in Section 4(7) of P.D. 1445.

9.5 We recommend that Management direct the MDRRMO to plan, develop
and implement only PPAs with a view that optimum use of the fund to reduce
disaster risks and build disaster-resilient communities will always be upheld;
instruct the Accountant to revert from the Special Trust Fund to the General
Fund any amount of the LDRRMF still not fully utilized after five (5) years;
require the Accountant to disclose in the Notes to FS the unexpended balance of
the LDRRMF as prescribed in Item 5.1.16 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 and
as shown in the sample form provided in Annex D thereof; and advise the
MDRRMO and the Accountant to prepare and submit monthly reports on
Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF to COA in a manner prescribed in Item
5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

10.6 We recommended that Management immediately call the attention of the
department heads concerned to timely prepare and post to the bulletin boards
allotted for the purpose the required documents/reports enumerated in DILG
Memorandum Circular No. 2011-134 in a manner prescribed in the provisions
thereof.

11.5 We recommended that Management refrain from charging
contributions for HIPADA from the 20% DF and submit to COA all documents
as legal bases of the foregoing payments including the MOA covering the
Project/Activity. Failure to comply may cause this Office to issue a Notice of
Suspension or Notice of Disallowance as circumstances may warrant.

12.5 We recommended that Management create a plantilla for GSO or
designate an employee to the position; Encourage the GSO to practice stocking
of a three (3)-month supplies so that immediate needs of the Agency will be
addressed; Advise the BAC to prepare the Abstract of Canvass and forward the
same to the GSO for the preparation of the PO; Instruct the GSO to prepare the
PO with proper and complete information and have it conformed with the
supplier. Remind the GSO to tell the supplier that delivery of the supplies
procured must be made direct to the GSO and in accordance with the terms and
conditions stated in the PO; Require the Inspectorate Team to inspect the items
delivered and prepare IAR if found in order. Otherwise, return the goods to the
supplier if found defective. The IAR must be given to the GSO for its signature
and furnish it, together with the PO and delivery receipt to the Accounting for
recording. It may be allowed that the end-user will prepare the ObR and DV
but its preparation must be made after the delivery of the goods and the
information therein must be based from the PO, IAR and delivery receipt;
Inform the GSO that the received supplies must be properly kept, segregated
and tagged and issuance thereof to the end-user must be recorded in a manner
provided in Section 122 of the NGAS Manual for LGU; and Faithfully adhere
to Section 122 of the NGAS Manual for LGU, Volume I in resolving other
matters relating to procurement of supplies and materials.

13.5 We recommended that Management issue final demand letter to the AOs
for the immediate liquidations of the cash advances. Failure of the latter to
comply may cause this Office to execute remedial procedures to collect the same
pursuant to COA Memorandum No. 2004-014 dated February 24, 2004. Strictly
observe the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 in the grant, utilization and
liquidation of cash advances.

14.5 We recommended that Management instruct the AOs whose
unliquidated cash advances aged more than 31 to 365 days to settle the same
with the Accounting Section without delay; Send demand letter to the AOs
whose unsettled cash advances had been outstanding for more than one year to
liquidate the same immediately. Failure to comply may constitute a valid cause
by the LGU to withhold the AOs’ claims (salaries, terminal leaves and other
benefits) from the former, if there is any; and Strictly observe the provisions of
COA Circular No. 97-002 in the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash
advances.

15.5 We recommended that Management fast tract the acquisition and
preparation of the sanitary land fill to be used as intended; Encourage every
barangay or cluster of barangays to establish an MRF where sorting,
segregation, composting and recycling of biodegradable, compostable and
reusable waste materials will take place. Inform the municipal constituents to
avoid throwing garbage anywhere and from making small garbage open
dumpsites in any place in the Municipality; Ensure compliance to Section 10 of
the R.A. No. 9003 where the LGU will collect non-recyclable materials and
special wastes to be placed in the designated dump sites; and Adhere to the
provisions of R.A. No. 9003 specifically Sections 10, 32, 33 and 37 thereof.

16.6 We recommended that Management provide at least 5% of the total
annual budget for GAD PAPs as prescribed by the JMC; formulate and include
only GAD PAPs that would support government’s commitment to promote
equality among men and women to resources and opportunities and which
specifically addressed gender issues and concerns; categorically include in the
establishment of the GFPS the Executive Committee, Technical Working Group
and/or Secretariat. These are responsible in carrying the functions of the Point
System; set up and maintain the GAD database to serve as basis for gender-
responsive planning, programming and policy formulation; carefully plan,
identify and consider gender issues and concerns in the LGU that these may be
included in the GPB, with due consideration that optimal utilization of the
appropriated amount for the program will be achieved; submit to the concerned
government oversight agencies the GPB in prescribed form and in a manner
stated in Sections 4.1.C.1 and 4.1.C.8.1 of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC
and Item V of COA Circular No. 2014-001; and strictly adhere to the provisions
set forth in R.A. 9710 and PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 in the
creation and utilization of GAD fund.

19. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant should determine the details
of the beginning balances of the unremitted taxes and pay the same to the BIR without
delay.

22. We recommended that Management submit to COA the documents required in the
subject Notices of Suspension and to settle the amount stated in the Notices of
Disallowance.

23.6 We recommended that Management convene with the SMWD officers
and revisit the MOA. Clarify with the SMWD whether there is a complete
transfer of ownership of the Water System from the LGU to the Water District.
If in affirmative, the LGU should drop from the books the Water System as part
of the PPE without prejudice to the issuance of NS, ND or NC by this Office due
to violation by the LGU of the provisions enshrined in COA Circular No. 89-
296. Amend the provisions of the MOA if necessary to conform with the
true/correct intention of both parties. Ensure that the parties should have a
uniform interpretation of the provision/stipulation stated in the MOA; and
Adhere to the provisions and regulations governing disposal/divestment of
government properties including its recording in the books.

require the Acting Municipal Accountant to adjust the under recording in the account of P100,000.00.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the concerned municipal officials in charge of the peace and order submit legal basis for the collection of contributions from the barangays and the corresponding liquidation thereof. observations.recommendation: It is recommended that Special Education Fund be utilized for expenses that are authorized in accordance with the Local Government Code and the Joint Circular No. 01 s. 1998 of the Department of Education, Culture and Sports, Department of Budget and Management and the Department of Interior and Local Government.

We recommend that the concerned Municipal Mayor direct the persons responsible to settle immediately the amount suspended in audit amounting to P346,000.00 for the payment made to PCSO for the counterpart of the municipality for one unit ambulance.

observations.recommendation:

 2.  Direct the Municipal Engineer to provide the Commission On Audit through the -undersigned the list of ongoing projects/programs/activities and those that are to be implemented during the year and adhere to the other provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management enforce immediate settlement of all outstanding cash advances as of December 31, 2013. observations.reason: As of December 31, 2016 Due from Officers and Employees account had a balance of P548,246.78.

observations.recommendation:

observations.recommendation: We recommended
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 Finding No.1

Require the employees the granting of cash advances according to pertinent provisions specifically of COA Circular No. 97-002 Sections 5.7 and 8 and Section 89 of PD 1445 resulted to the overstatement and understatement of relevant assets as well as expense utilization and liquidation of cash advances. And enforce sanctions in case of failure of the accountable officer to liquidate his/her cash advances on time. b. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to send demand

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant shall prepare the Utilization Report on the Local Disaster Funds and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor. This way, evaluation as to the proper utilization and disbursement of disaster funds can be undertaken at the most appropriate and efficient manner.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 Finding No.2
observations.reason: submitted to COA for review.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that tax collection efforts be intensified. Remedies provided under the Local Government Code such as auction, levy and distraint of personal property has to be carried out to recover the tax delinquency. Tax information campaign and rewards should be vigorously explored and pursued to encourage payment. It would be wiser also to cause the publication of delinquent properties and sending billing notices to defaulting taxpayers.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 Finding No.3
observations.reason: The LGU did not exert all its efforts to collection target because rate is too high.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: a. Make representation with the PCL officials and request that seminars/trainings where participants come from the province should be conducted in the province in order to minimize the cost of the conduct of these activities and save funds for other relative projects. b. Send participants (SB members) only to seminars with agency that is in their area of concern.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 Finding No.4
observations.reason: LGU overlooked this observation and recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Local Chief Executive require the Local Treasurer and Accountant to observe strictly COA Circular No. 2012-001 and that all fund transfers must have a Memorandum of Agreement to include a provision that the infrastructure project must be validated upon completion through conduct of inspection by the Municipal Engineer and prepare an inspection report based on the statement of work accomplished by the barangay to be attached to the liquidation report.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 Finding No.5
observations.reason: Manpower and time constraints hinder conducti ng of inspections. Hence reiterate d in this years audit observat ions.

observations.recommendation: Since fiscal responsibility rests upon the Head of the Agency, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to submit the accounts of the municipality regularly on every 10th day of the succeeding month. Likewise all accountable officers are to turn-over to the accounting division for recording purposes reports as well as source documents within five (5) days after the end of each month.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 Finding No.6

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer to deposit intact all her collections including all collections turned over to her daily or not later than the next banking day. Initiate disciplinary measure against collecting officers who are perennially delayed in remitting their collections to avoid the risk of misuse or loss of funds.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 Finding No.7
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasurer deposited all her collections on December 31, 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive remind the Municipal Accountant to refrain from granting full Transportation Allowances to officials who are issued or actually using government vehicle. Full adherence to Section 56 of the General Appropriations Act of 2015, Section VI of COA Circular No. 75-6 and Local Budget Circular No. 103 is required. Proportionate amount pertaining to the actual days when government transportation facilities were used should be deducted from their monthly Transportation Allowances. We also recommend that each trip ticket be properly accomplished and the authorized passenger officials be indicated.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 Finding No.8
observations.reason: The LGU made required adjustments.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the Technical Working Group of the GAD Focal and Budget to prepare and submit the GAD Plan and Budget and Accomplishment report as required under the rules cited.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 Finding No.9
observations.reason: GAD Plan and Budget were submitted to COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that contracts of job order workers should contain statements of duties and responsibilities or equivalent terms to serve as standard with which to base all their accomplishment.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 Finding No.10
observations.reason: HRMO made sure that all issued JO contracts contains statements of duties and responsibilities.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BAC Secretary and all other Department Heads concerned to strictly adhere with the practice of checking as to completeness of documentations before claims are paid. Such measure must be carried out to preclude the suspension or disallowance of transactions during post-audit. The Accountant should check on whether the documents submitted are properly approved and signed by the concerned officials.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 Finding No.11
observations.reason: Some important document s were not submitted to COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct: 1. The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer to observe the guidelines in the utilization of the 20% DF in compliance with the DILG-DBM Joint Circular 2011-01. 2. The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to install appropriate controls over the proper utilization thereof.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 Finding No.12
observations.reason: Some items included in this years budget were implemented even if they were not in compliance with DBM rules.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that whenever medicines were procured, management should ensure that documents required in EO no. 49, DOH AO no. 163 series of 2002 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 are complied with to ensure that items procured originated from legitimate sources and that they are effective as well as genuine. Also, require that the Property Custodian submit to Accounting Monthly Report of Medicines Issued as a basis for dropping from inventory account and taking up Medicines Expenses. The BAC should also declare a bid as non-responsive if the CPRs submitted and the eligibility documents are expired.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 Finding No.13
observations.reason: required document s were submitted to COA

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous year recommendation for strict compliance to Sections 74 and 111 of PD 1445 to avoid incurrence of negative balance in cash in bank accounts. Specifically, we suggested the following remedial steps: a) Create a task force that will take charge in the preparation of the BRS for the five LBP accounts setting a time-frame for its completion. The team is required to prepare and submit to the head of the agency monthly progress reports with emphasis on the reconciling items, copy furnished the audit team. b) Make written representations with the depository banks to ensure prompt submission of the Bank statements. c) Maintain an updated SL which will show the details or breakdown of the balance of the controlling account appearing in the general ledger. d) Require the Municipal Accountant to exert enough effort for the reconciliation of the negative balance reflected in the books of accounts against the balance of the bank statements as of December 31, 2013, and effect adjustment thereof.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2013 Finding No.1
observations.reason: Bank Reconcili ation Stateme nts as of Decembe r 31, 2014 not submitte d to COA.

observations.recommendation: 1. Require the contractor to rectify/correct on work deficiencies pursuant to Section 7.0 of Annex E of R.A. 9184. 2. Require the concerned officials to exercise due diligence that items before processing of claims: a) the projects/works have been inspected and accomplished according to its plans and specifications; and, b) that infrastructure procedures have been faithfully complied as prescribed under RA 9184 and its implementing rules and regulations to ensure the truthfulness of the transactions and their validity before effecting payment.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2013 Finding No.3
observations.reason: Projects still not 100% complet ed despite this years observat ions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management: 1. Instruct the official concerned to strictly implement Section 122 of PD 1445 and Section 124 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for Local Government Units, Volume I; 2. Create Property Inventory Team with representatives from the offices of the Municipal Mayor, Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Assessor, and the accountable officer of every department of the LGU to conduct complete physical inventory of all properties owned by the municipality at least once a year; 3. Direct the inventory team to prepare inventory report thereof using the prescribed form of the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RCPPE), properly accomplished, and furnish the Municipal Accountant a copy of the report for reconciliation purposes, all unserviceable equipment, properties should be included in the physical count and render a report thereon for possible disposal and or dropping from the books of account; and 4. Direct the Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer/Municipal Treasurer to maintain updated PPE ledger cards. Periodic reconciliation of the accounting records and inventory reports of PPE should be undertaken, and if there are discrepancies noted between the two records, effect immediately the affected accounts, so that necessary adjustments on the correct balances of PPE accounts be reflected in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2013 Finding No.7
observations.reason: Property Inventory Team were not created.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive should: 1. Require the Municipal Accountant to strictly follow with the accounting procedures contained in Section 105-110 of chapter 6, Manual on the New Government Accounting System for LGUs regarding maintenance of special accounts and subsidiary ledger in the General Fund; and Section 5.0 of COA Circular 2012-002 for reporting on the Sources and Utilization of Fund for LDRRMF. 2. Require the Municipal Accountant to immediately assign special accounts and maintain separate subsidiary ledgers in the GF for the LGUs 5% GAD & 5% LDRRMF in order to have readily adequate and reliable information as to assets, liabilities and the corresponding object of expenditures, fund balance, and if these were in line with the rules cited.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2013 Finding No.11
observations.reason: Required subsidiary ledgers were not made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management refrain from payment of similar nature unless the budget review is already done. The budget for the ensuing year should be prepared within the prescribed period to facilitate timely review. Full adherence with the PS limitation under Section 325a of the Local Government Code is also enjoined to avoid disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2014 Finding No.1
observations.reason: LGU personnel started paying the ND issued for this observati on.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management adhere to Sec. 323 of the Local Government Code. Only existing contractual obligations authorized in the previous budget may be paid under a re-enacted budget. No new contracts shall be paid until the budget for the current year is approved.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2014 Finding No.2
observations.reason: Late preparation and submission of new budget.

observations.recommendation: We recommended management observe Section 4(6) and 111(2) of PD 1445 and refrain from releasing the Retention Money if contractor fails to submit complete supporting documents prescribed under paragraph 6.2 of Annex E of the Revised IRR of RA 9184 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012, particularly Section 9.1.1.5. It would also be wise to withhold the Retention Money within one year after project completion (Defects Liability Period) unless the above requirements are complied. This way, rectification of project defects is better assured as the management already has the money in the bank.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2014 Finding No.3
observations.reason: Some documents were attached to DVs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Assessor is advised to promptly submit the Assessment Roll to the Treasurer who in turn, is required under the rules cited, to submit to the Municipal Accountant the certified list of taxpayers with the amount due. With the submission of these documents, the Municipal Accountant may now set up Real Property Tax Receivable/Special Education Tax Receivable at the beginning of the year.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2014 Finding No.4
observations.reason: Receivables recorded after submission of Assessment Roll.

observations.recommendation: We recommended: a. Require the contractor/supplier to submit corresponding Certificates of Product Registration at least, at the time of delivery. b. Refuse acceptance if the product is not covered by a CPR. c. The Technical Working Group (TWG) of the Bids and Awards committee should also verify the authenticity of the CPRs submitted. d. Require the supplier to submit a Certificate of Good Manufacturing Practice and Certificate of Authority as a Distributor from the manufacturer.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2014 Finding No.5
observations.reason: required document s were submitted

observations.recommendation: We recommended: a. The Municipal Treasurer shall see to it that all these vouchers with its complete supporting documents shall be returned to the Office of the Municipal Accountant after the checks are released. b. The Municipal Accountant to check and compare the Disbursement Vouchers with the Report of Checks Disbursements by the Treasurer to ascertain if the vouchers are complete before the same are recorded in the books of accounts and transmitted to the office of the auditor.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2014 Finding No.6

observations.recommendation: We recommended that for the use of transportation and heavy equipment necessary Trip Tickets are undertaken and Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption should be prepared by the Municipal Treasurer. We also suggest that all the information required in the Trip Ticket be properly filled up to make it useful and relevant basis for future decision making.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2014 Finding No.7
observations.reason: Monthly reports and trip tickets were prepared with fuel consumption report.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management direct the General Services Officer to complete the inventory-taking of PPE and Inventories for CY 2015 so that the Accounting unit can easily reconcile with their recorded PPEs and Inventories and so that those accounts can be presented fairly in the Statement of Financial Position.
observations.managementAction: The Management assigned personnel to conduct a physical inventory and reconcile the Books of Accounting and General Supply Office on the Property, Plant, Equipment (PPEs) and Inventories Account.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that Management:
Require the employees to refund immediately the unexpended balance of the cash advances and enforce the immediate liquidation of overdue advances, otherwise, the Treasurer is advised to withhold or suspend the salaries of the erring accountable officers which had not settle their cash advances as of November 30, 2015 without prejudice to the other sanctions as provided for under the aforementioned regulations.

Direct the Accountant to issue Certification that that previous cash advances have been liquidated and accounted for in the books before granting cash advances.

Strictly adheres with the provisions of Sec. 89 PD 1445, COA Cir. No. 97-002 and EO No. 298 and other rulings mentioned above.
observations.managementAction:
The recommendations were noted. 

Some officials and employees were deducted in the payroll for refund of their unliquidated cash advances.

observations.recommendation: We recommend to the Local Chief Executive to instruct the Accountant to locate the documents or trace the original records of these accounts and prepare adjustments so that these accounts can be presented fairly in the Statement of the Financial Position.
observations.managementAction: After discussing and investigating the lump sum of Receivable and Payable Accounts, there were adjustments made in these accounts to have a fair presentation of Statement of Financial Position.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend to the Local Chief Executive to:

Require the Municipal Treasurer to let the Disbursing Officer explain why she did not immediately return the unexpended balances considering that these were already double payments. Impose necessary sanction if warranted.

Also, direct the Municipal Accountant to properly monitor the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances, and to meticulously review and check  all the detail of the cash advances, including the pay periods, purposes and other particulars as part of the brief description of the payrolls covered by the Disbursement Vouchers for cash advances and liquidations prior to certifying the completeness and validity of the same.
observations.managementAction: This audit observation is noted and duly complied with.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the LGU refrain from granting honorarium without legal basis and follow the provisions of law.
observations.managementAction: The Management observed the audit findings.

observations.recommendation: Direct Management to require among others, the Official Receipts and Memorandum of Agreement in fund transfers to Government and Private Organizations to establish the validity and propriety of transactions.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality complied this ruling.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend to the Local Chief Executive to direct those concerned in the preparation and processing of government contracts to submit the same to COA five (5) days after perfection thereof.  

We also recommend that the Accountant will require a received copy of the contract during processing of payment.
observations.managementAction: The subject finding has been noted and was duly corrected. However, the problem encountered happened because of the delay of the supplier/contractor and submission of performance bond. BAC do its best to submit the contract on time.

observations.recommendation: We recommend to the Local Chief Executive to instruct the Bids and Awards Committee to be meticulous in following the process on procurement and abide strictly to the IRR of RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: The BAC is trying to be vigilant and judicious in the procurement process of similar amount or more.

observations.recommendation: We recommend to the Local Chief Executive to instruct the Municipal Accountant to withheld the amount of Php49,758.34 during the payment of the contract, otherwise, a Notice of Disallowance will be issued on the same amount.  Moreover, instruct the Bids and Awards Committee and the Technical Working Group to meticulously review all the documents and the basis on the Approved Budget of the Contract before proceeding to the procurement process.
observations.managementAction: Noted

observations.recommendation: We recommend to Management to direct those concerned in the implementation of the projects to give emphasis on the quality of all on-going and upcoming projects so that comfort of the populace at large can be provided.
observations.managementAction: The Management directed the contractor to repair the portion of the said minor surface depression. The noted defects have been implemented and notified.

observations.recommendation: We recommend to management to direct those involved in planning and implementation to monitor closely and hasten the implementation of the projects especially those funded out of the 20% Development Fund, to achieve the desired objectives   in the delivery of economic and social developmental projects, so that targeted beneficiaries can benefit the comfort of these projects.
observations.managementAction: The Management directed those who are involved in planning and implementation of identified projects to fast track the implementation of said projects. In CY 2014, 63% was accomplished and 80% in CY 2015 in 20% LDF. The remaining identified projects are still on-going.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management increase the appropriation for the maintenance of the above-mentioned Farm to Market Road. Moreover, regulate the use of the road for logging trucks to avoid further deterioration.
observations.managementAction: Noted

We recommended and
Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive must see to it that the
20% of IRA be optimally utilized to help attain desirable socio-economic
development and environmental outcomes.

Management agreed to open a separate bank account, as
Special Trust Fund, for the unexpended balance of

Council within the next five years. Require the Municipal

Fund (QRF) and the DRRMF-MOOE and transfer the same to the Special Trust Fund
under the account “Trust Liabilities- Disaster Risk Reduction management Fund
(Code 2-04-01-020) in the Trust Fund books. Likewise, direct the latter to maintain
subsidiary ledgers based on source year.

and circular and submit the same to the Office of the

liquidation of cash advances as provided in COA Circular No.
2012-001.
observations.managementAction: Recommendation is well taken.

observations.recommendation:

consolidated APP. Require the BAC to furnish the Office of the

validate and ensure that all procurement undertaken by the
LGU were based on the approved annual procurement plan.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and
Management require the BAC to ensure that the LGU as the
procuring entity abides the standards set forth in the IRR of
RA 9184. Require the Municipal Accountant to forfeit the bid
security of the bidder with the Lowest Calculated Responsive
Bid/Highest Responsive Bid or the bidder with the Single
Calculated/Rated Responsive Bids who fails refuses or is
unable to submit the documents required under Section 37.1 of
this IRR or to make good its bid by entering into a contract with
the procuring entity or post the required performance security
pursuant to Section 40.1 of the said IRR. Moreover, direct the
BAC to disqualify the said bidder, and to initiate and complete the post qualification process on the bidder with the
second LCRB/Highest Rated Bid pursuant to Section 40.2 of
the said IRR.
observations.managementAction: is well taken
observations.reason: The Agency had tried to comply with the requirements at all times

observations.recommendation:

observations.managementAction: is well taken

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and
Management agreed to:
1. Instruct the Municipal
Treasurer to prepare the Report of Checks Issued (RCI) daily and forward the same to the
Accounting Office together with the disbursement vouchers and other supporting
documents.
2. Require the Municipal
Accountant to submit the RCI together with the DVs and its supporting documents within
first ten days of the ensuing month in accordance with the provision of COA Circular No.
2009-006.
3. Reprimand all accountable
officers concerned for the immediate submission of all the RCIs and its supporting
documents to the Auditor. Otherwise, their failure or refusal to submit papers relative to
government obligations is a ground for administrative disciplinary action and
disallowance of the transactions as provided in Section 39 (1&3) of PD. 1445. Besides,
failure to render accounts for a period of two months after such accounts should be
rendered is penalized in Article 218 of the Revised Penal Code.
observations.managementAction: is well taken.
observations.reason: There were still missing DVs out from the submitted DVs to COA

received by the DILG Regional Office.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and
Management agreed to:
1. Conduct a comprehensive feasibility study/assessment on their capacity to operate,
maintain, administer and pay the loan amortization before contracting loan with any of the
financial institutions.
2. Efficiently and effectively maximize the available resources so that operation of the existing project would earn
income and more benefits could be derived therefrom.
3. Not to transfer the ownership of the waterworks and/or sewerage facilities and land to the SMWD.
4. Observe strictly the rules and regulations in the disposal of government property to avoid
wastage of government funds.
observations.managementAction: Only the operation, maintenance and administration were transferred to SMWD and not the PPE as per annexation agreement. Copy of the feasibility study had already been furnished to COA.

constructed cost of the Level III Water System, loss of its transfer and overpayment of
P674,349.06 by SMWD.

Otherwise, such failure / or settle the same shall constitute a valid cause for the withholding of their salaries and the instruction of other sanctions. Besides, require her to attach a certification that the previous cash advances have been liquidated and accounted for in the books.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and
Management agreed to require the Revenue Collectors to remit/
turn over daily the collections to the Municipal Treasurer.
Otherwise, she will hold/ not issue additional accountable
forms without the full turn-over of collections. More-over, direct
the Municipal Treasurer to immediately deposit the collections
with the authorized government depository bank and stop the
practice of retaining collections in her custody. Strengthen the
internal control in the collection system by strict compliance
with Secs. 29 to 32 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I
and Local Treasury Operations Manual.
observations.managementAction: The Agency still practice not to turn over the collections by Revenue Collectors to the Municipal Treasurer
observations.reason: Partially implemented

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and
Management agreed to require the Municipal Treasurer to
record the financial transactions of the Municipality daily
pursuant to Sections 26, 27 and 28 of the NGAS Manual for
LGUs, Vol. II. Likewise, direct her to update and balance all the
transactions and reconcile the same with the cash on hand as
provided in Section 63 (D) of LTOM. Compel all the Revenue
Collectors to maintain Teller’s/Collector’s to record all
collections received and all remittances to the designated
Liquidating Officer in accordance with Section 63 (C) of LTOM.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer still did not update and balance daily all financial trans-actions in the Cashbooks
observations.reason: Partially implemented

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and
Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Treasurer to
prepare the RCI and RCD and forward the same to the
Accounting Office together with the supporting documents.
Require the Municipal Accountant to submit said documents
within first ten (10) days of the ensuing month in accordance
with the provision of COA Circular No. 2009-006.
Reprimand all accountable officers concerned for the
immediate submission of the same to the Auditor. Otherwise,
their failure or refusal to submit papers relative to government
obligations is a ground for administrative disciplinary action
and disallowance of the transactions as provided in Section 39
(1 &3) of PD No. 1445. Besides, failure to render accounts for a
period of two months after such accounts should be rendered
is penalized in Article 218 of the Revised Penal Code.
observations.managementAction: implemented
observations.reason: Fully implemented

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and
Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant,
Municipal Budget Officer, Municipal Treasurer, Inventory
Committee, Municipal Engineer, and LDRRM Officer to submit
the required reports immediately and adhere strictly the
provisions cited in the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Vols. I and II
and COA Cir. Nos. 2013-004 and 2012-002.
Otherwise, failure on the part of the officials concerned to
submit the documents and reports mentioned herein shall
automatically cause the suspension of payment of their
salaries until they shall have complied with the requirements
of the Commission. Further, no appropriation authorized in the
General Appropriations Act shall be available to pay the salary
or any official or employee who violates the provisions of this
section, without prejudice to any disciplinary action that may be
instituted against such official or employee. All of these were
provided in Section 122 (2 and 3) of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: Some of the mentioned reports were not submitted to COA in a manner prescribed by laws, rules and regulations
observations.reason: Partially implemented

Office then to the DILG RO for review and approval. Develop GAD
programs, activities and projects to strengthen the
Municipality’s gender-responsive governance and ensure that GAD Fund is

GAD Accomplishment Report (GAD AR) based on the DILG-approved GPB or the enacted
